# Thread: NutrEval + GI Effects --- go with antibiotics suggestion?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nutreval-gi-effects-go-with-antibiotics-suggestion.21480/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gina R — 2018-06-16T04:03:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nutreval-gi-effects-go-with-antibiotics-suggestion.21480/post-243502>

HI I posted this in my journal but I'm trying to decide if I should go the antibiotic route, so I'm going to post it here too...
Labs are dropbox links at the bottom. Thanks for reading!

**Basic Info:**

37 yo female, will be 38 in october, single, business owner, NTP
history of "Ulcerative Colitis" Diagnosis and conventional meds, including almost 5ys of Remicade, "symptomatic" from age 19 to age 30. Got of meds 2011, flared 2013 and did FMTs, and since then no "classic UC symptoms".

**Top current health concerns:**

-   fatigue + neuro/cognitive episodes that started late April 2018 after a flight to Austin, TX and then after May 2 following a flight back home to Baltimore, MD.

The best way to describe it when it's happening, it feels like the onset of a mushroom trip --- visual disturbances (less peripheral vision, and extreme fixation/notice on certain details), brainfog, poor memory, finger touch/sensation is off, feel "clumsy" with walking, bumping into things, basically feeling like the onset of magic mushrooms... When it's really bad, I don't feel like I can drive and am so out of it, I don't leave the house. Some background about mushroom use, I had been micro-dosing for about 8 months with taking 1.5-1.75 grams once/month or once every couple months. HOWEVER, during my trip in Austin, I took 1.75 grams twice in a 4-day time span. But, the first ever episode happened the day I landed and BEFORE I took any mushrooms. Not sure if there's any cause/effect relationship there....These episodes last varying lengths --- first one in Austin lasted 3 days, next one in Maryland lasted 2.5 weeks, then 9 days of feeling 80% normal, then next one in Maryland lasted 2 weeks.

Did a niacin challenge test and no effect with 50mg of niacin on empty stomach. Tested that 2x in one day and then 2x another day.

-   "fungal" issues toes/toenails that has been going on for almost 10 years but increasing in severity last year or so, affecting all toenails except one. Previously, lots of oozing sores on the toes, between toes. Toes are sometimes red/purple if cold or really hot. Nails were turning all yellow, thick, discolored. However, as of June, there is evidence of re-growth of normal toenail. In May, I had recently cut out all fermented foods & kombucha and was applying iodine topically.
-   abdominal distention/bloat for about 2 years now. On and off but last 6 months or so, consistently distended no matter what I eat. Previously tried low-fodmap and I thought I had some relief but that didn't last more than 4 months or so. Prior practitioner suspected SIBO, said test was negative Spring 2017. There's more history here but trying to keep to most relevant. Currently very distended and stools are Bristol 6-7 with other daily GI distress.
-   missed menstrual cycles. I was regular for about 5 years until Spring 2017 after I traveled to the Middle East and didn't get my period there which I should have. Since April 2017 to June 2018, I've only had 6 periods.
-   facial puffiness/body composition puffiness.

**Daily priorities:**

-   Morning light with mobility movement, meditation almost daily, mid-day sun when it's out (most times in bathing suit) and evening walk after sunset. I need to be better at doing this in the winter here in Maryland but I do this now consistently. Red light at night, redjuvenator, wear blue blockers, acupressure mat 3-4x/week, epsom salt baths 2-3x/week. I wake on my own about 6am. Acupuncture 1/week. Currently have been doing electro-scalp acupuncture after recent episodes started.
-   I haven't done any form of intense exercise since mid April 2018 before my trip to Austin.
-   RO water + liquid minerals added.
-   Currently taking naps almost daily.
-   \*EDITED TO ADD\* "Paleo" diet, egg yolks, white rice, ghee. Was eating some forms of organic corn but stopped when recent LRA revealed strong reaction. (The first food template I did was GAPS, then Paleo, then AIP for about a year and recently swung back out into "paleo".) Working on keeping complex carbs higher than in years past but still keeping fat high.

**Current dilemma with next step bc of a couple conflicting opinions:**

-   Bc of GI effects results, bc of overgrowth of ecoli and a few others, functional practitioner/DOM suggests 2 weeks of oral tetracycline then 10 days fluconozale along. Taking a particular probiotic orally, via vagina, via suppository and up nose while taking the tetracyline and fluconozale. Continuing with the probiotic for 6 months afterwards.
-   Current acupuncturist who is also DOM suggest herbal formula to combat the overgrowth and not to do the rx antibiotics...but that would take 6+ months to balance. Also suggests to test for lyme and possibly add in herbal formula protocol for lyme.

**Questions:**

-   Main question - do antibiotics + antifungal course? And if not, what is a better option to tackle the GI issues and these neuro/cognitive episodes + fatigue?
-   Test for lyme? There's lots of controversy about testing, so which one to do and also does it matter if "lyme" or not?

There is SO much more information but this is what's present and current.

I don't have many supportive relationships here/community, so my temporary relocation to Austin is to explore some professional and personal possibilities. Winters here also don't work for me, so I need to be somewhere year-round with good sun along with lots of like-minded people/community. My optimal health seeking also radiates out to hoping to be able to have a child at some point. Lots of financial issues, current lease is ending and also a reason of going out to Austin to stay with a friend to regain some footing with finances.

I could go on and I'm glad to share any more information. I hope Jack may see this and be able to put in some input about my current dilemma with next step?

Thanks in advance and for everything you all do.

**GI Effects May 2018** ---
[https://www.dropbox.com/s/j4qg8tj4v3n43v9/GI Effects May 2018 wo name.pdf?dl=0](https://www.dropbox.com/s/j4qg8tj4v3n43v9/GI%20Effects%20May%202018%20wo%20name.pdf?dl=0)

**NutrEval Plasma April 2018** ---
[https://www.dropbox.com/s/vp15vwulytdnn1i/Nutreval Plasma April 2018 wo name.pdf?dl=0](https://www.dropbox.com/s/vp15vwulytdnn1i/Nutreval%20Plasma%20April%202018%20wo%20name.pdf?dl=0)

I have 23&me done but I just have the raw data so far. Not sure what system is best to run it through to extract the data? I'm open to running through whichever database is recommended to get the best information from it.

Thank you!

### OptimalSkr — 2018-06-18T18:33:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fiber-optic-cable-and-emfs.21486/post-243692>

So they are installing fiber optic cables in my subdivision, (Bell Canada) and I have to allow it being put half way up the lawn. Then I can give consent to the next portion up to the house where they bring it in. I called the company and they said that no signal (Bluetooth) is emitted from the box in the ground or the one on the house. (I am not sure if this is accurate and will have to measure with a meter to be sure)

[https://www.bell.ca/Bell\_Internet/Products/Fibre-to-the-home](https://www.bell.ca/Bell_Internet/Products/Fibre-to-the-home)

But my other question here for some savvy EMF people is that: because the fiber optic cable promises speeds up to 5x what is currently used (DSL and Cable) when it is connected to the router and broadcast on Wifi - **_is that signal going to be 5x more harmful than the current one?_** **_Is this going to be the equivalent of 5g for internet?
_**
Should I be freaking out about this, or am I over reacting? They currently have slower plans here in Canada for the roll out of 5g cell tower, but I am worried about this.

Thanks.

# Thread: I Didn't Know Jack When I Didn't Know Jack
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-didnt-know-jack-when-i-didnt-know-jack.21491/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sara — 2018-06-19T22:41:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-didnt-know-jack-when-i-didnt-know-jack.21491/post-243816>

Hola, Good People …

I'm a 70-year-old SaddleTramp living in a wilderness area. I reside in a vintage Spartan trailer made of metal (thank my intuitive stars) and am on the verge of grounding it. The population here is around 3500 people across 6928 miles of territory known as Catron County in New Mexico. My immediate location consists of around 500 people. We have 2 or 3 cell towers many miles away and I understand that the Forest Service would not approve the installation of 5G. I'm surrounded by pine forests, which are one of the most healing of Natures' growths. I take no meds, do not fall ill and the cancer I was diagnosed with 42 years ago is non-existent. I did not seek medical help, btw. I'm a proponent for proper hydration above all things that we put into our bodies and I love the sun. Almost everyone else here is sick.

I'm grateful to the people who led me here.

Thank you.

### caroline — 2018-06-20T03:03:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-didnt-know-jack-when-i-didnt-know-jack.21491/post-243837>

Hi Sara ....I love your storey! but - why is everyone around you sick?????? Did I take that too literally?

Please start a journal in the journal section and tell us lots more.....

### Sara — 2018-06-20T16:17:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-didnt-know-jack-when-i-didnt-know-jack.21491/post-243873>

Journal? Maybe I can't find the journal section until I become a paid member, which I can't do until next month? But to expound on the story of here:

Oh, I do mean literally sick: COPD, rheumatoid arthritis, auto-immune def, thyroid conditions, heart disease, diabetes, misshaped bodies on a par with walmart in between overweight and obesity; the meth and alcohol community that won’t quit with some psychopathic results. People here hold on to their diseases; it defines them, gives them something to talk about and, of course, it’s all genetic. There is no talking them through it; no alternative method toward health except medication. Food allergies are rampant here. I used to try to talk to people about hydration and was told by all that they drink a lot of water, although I never, not ever, see them drink water; I see them drink soda, Arizona and other teas, juices, energy drinks, coffee, but not water. In my little neck of the woods, we have an 89-year-old geezer, a friend, who has prostate cancer and has been self-medicating with milk of magnesia for years, evacuating every 3-5 days. He has eaten a SAD diet all his life. One day, he came in to say goodbye; he would be dead in a matter of days because he could no longer poop. A friend and I rehabilitated his diet, put him on pro-biotics, digestive enzymes and psyllium awash with proper water and he’s mostly a happy camper now. At 89, he has his complaints, but he’s pooping away and no longer making declarations of impending death. And, astonishingly, in this outdoor environment, most people live indoors. I am completely perplexed by this. We have some people who go for constitutional walks every day. They walk on the road and never think of walking in the forest, which is right behind us and is, basically, pine with high healing qualities. There are 2-track roads out there; easy pathways on which to tread.

### Inger — 2018-06-20T17:30:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-didnt-know-jack-when-i-didnt-know-jack.21491/post-243880>

Welcome Sara!

Well everyone can start a journal, I guess, no need to be a paid member for that?

Sounds interesting, your observations! Yes, what did it help to live in the wilderness, if you are not outside?
Is it some kind of hippie community or what is it where you live  Why are people living there? Do they have some common goal?

Did you tell that 89 year old gentleman to tan nude too?

Please keep on sharing your observations and your experiences, so glad you found us

### Sara — 2018-06-20T23:32:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-didnt-know-jack-when-i-didnt-know-jack.21491/post-243912>

The “old gentleman” is an outdoorsman. He’s out chopping in his wood pile, gardening, puttering and generally finding stuff to do. If I suggested he do it naked, he’d have a cow. Sometimes, when I bring him his Holy Tea in the evening, he’s already abed in his pajamas and asks me to wait to enter until he’s back in his bedroom. Lol! This is outlaw territory, one of the last bastions of the wild west, if not the last, where a law was passed that all male residents of legal age must own a gun; pretty sexist if you ask me. The southern tip of The Hole In The Wall Trail begins just down the road from me and Geronimo was born on the west fork of the Gila River, about 4 miles from here as the crow flies. There’s also the old Pony Express Mail Trail. We’re a wild bunch, leftovers from old ranching families that don’t ranch anymore. We have a large elderly community with a lot of die-offs and many residents who are on disability; a lot of opioid use. It’s the outsiders, such as myself, who come in that give this place life. And why do we come? We are drawn here for different reasons: health reasons; anti-societal reasons; anti-government reasons. Like that. I was drawn here as the last refuge of the end of the world as we’ve known it. The stage of the entire world seems to reside on the other side of these southern Rockies. It’s like a great wall of sorrows. In short, we are outcasts by choice. We outfit and guide dudes into the wilderness for hunts and for pleasure. We have our loggers who seem to have begun to poach wood to sell to wood-burning stove users and we have our elk antler gatherers. We’re a tourist mecca like a ghost town, consisting of Anglos and Mexicans, not a Native person in sight; they’ve vacated the territory, through force, for the Mescalero Rez across the state to the Southeast. It’s quite a beautiful rez.

There is a very small, almost non-existent group of like-minded people who are aware of modern day realities; geo-engineering, emf pollution; gmo seeding; 5G; etc. Most others cannot comprehend what the hell is going on. Try as one might, the battle for awareness is already lost.

Where do I find the journal section?

### Azo — 2018-06-19T23:20:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cancer-journey.21492/post-243822>

Dr. Paul Stallone from Phoenix, AZ (who has three doctorates: MD, ND and DO) stated in 2008 that the vaccinations I received in boot camp as a young man had harmed my body, and he thought it was the cause of my prostate issue at that time.

I started having high PSA readings about 2005. I'm now age 71. I had started to "get in" to health foods in 1974, and I didn't really go to doctors for many years, so I ignored the PSA. I have believed for many years in earthing, exposing the body to sunshine, and "getting back to nature"; however, I was not able to practice this to nearly as great a degree as I would have liked. By 2012, my PSA was alarmingly high, along with other indications of prostate cancer (blood tests, DRE, ultrasound, MRI). Since I was against breaking the capsule, I traveled 1000 miles to see Dr. Robert Bard in NYC. Dr. Bard did not require a biopsy (which I believe is harmful), because it can release circulating tumor cells. I have read recent research that states as few as 100 circulating cancer stem cells can cause metastasis of a cancer (to elsewhere in the body). Dr. Bard read me the riot act, and said I should get a laser ablation, which I finally did on November, 29, 2012, by Dr. Daniel Sperling. That caused me a lot of grief and disrupted my entire body. When I was on the table having the ablation, I had an involuntary spasm on their multi-million dollar table, which was a 3 T Tesla MRI-guided ablation.

Afterward, the problem was not resolved, but seemed even worse; I had many complications, including a hernia, which I trace to constipation after having the ablation. Dr. Sperling offered to repeat the procedure, but I was afraid of it, and refused. So I have tried many different remedies, with no success. This led me to Dr. Jerry Tennant, whose office is in Colleyville, TX. I believed in his theory, which was a take-off from Dr. Robert Beck's book, Body Electric. I traveled to see Dr. Tennant in May 2018, because I was concerned that circuitry in my body was not functioning correctly. Dr. Tennant, upon examination, told me that all 6 of my body's circuits were in reverse polarity and basically shut down. He used his "biomodulator" and transducer, which purportedly emit both electromagnetic and scalar energy, to correct the situation. However, when I returned to his office 2 days later, according to their tests, only 3 of the 6 circuits remained open. Then, for reasons unknown to me, he dismissed me that day (via his office manager) as a patient.

I have thousands of hours of study, and immediately recognized, once I stumbled onto Dr. Kruse's work, that I had found some real gems of information worth knowing. I realized that I had had cataract surgery in 2010, and it flashed back in my mind that my body has been in a decline since that time. The artificial plastic lenses block 100% of UVA and UVB light and a large amount of red spectrum light as well. I am very interested in ideas on how to correct this artificial-lens situation.

On June 6, 2018, I suffered a major health attack. I had a few episodes of bright blood in my urine, went to a small ER, which gave me an oral antibiotic (Bactrim) and sent me home, to "see your urologist." A half hour after taking the pill, I suffered severe nausea and vomiting, which lasted for about 12 hours. Since that, I was admitted through another hospital's ER, and I have been diagnosed with a large malignant mass on my right kidney. Of course, the conventional treatment is nephrectomy.

I am very frustrated with so-called "conventional medicine" and the "standard of care," which is based on neither science nor sound logic, but upon legal rubbish which has been worked out for political machinations and is not in the patient's favor, but rather in the profit-making machine's favor. Now I am in the revolving door, and find myself searching for any reasonable path to pursue. (My first follow-up with the urologist, since hospitalization, is tomorrow.) I am open to any and all reasonable suggestions, to help with my dilemma.

### Sheddie — 2018-06-22T18:34:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cancer-journey.21492/post-244067>

> [Azo said:](/goto/post?id=244009)
>
> Thank you very much, Dr. Kruse, for your interest and for replying to me. I tremendously appreciate it, as I am struggling to make decisions.
>
> One more question...how much of an impediment are my \[cataract\] lens implants to receiving nourishment from the light to my pituitary and pineal gland? What possible correction could be done?

Hello Azo; I relate to much of your experiential commentary on conventional 'factory-model' medical care when it comes to chronic illness and the 'standard' long-term ineffectiveness, even harm.

Last year, I found out about 2 months after having standard UV-blocking IOL implants, that **blue-blocking IOLs** do exist but were never offered me even though I provided my medical history records of developing 3 autoimmune diagnoses over 20 years earlier following one 'successful' ruptured spinal disk repair by a neurosurgeon. I considered going for more eye surgeries to swap out the 'standard' IOLs I have, but the first surgeries were extremely tricky due to my lens capsule condition and damaged corneas. There was risk of permanent blindness the first time, even with traveling to a world class eye surgeon.

At this time, I am concentrating on skin exposure as my best route of UV exposure. For this, it was doable to put up a privacy fence and take the sun in full nudity despite the fact I have had one melanoma surgery on a shoulder site, and get strong advice from my oncologist to avoid the sun, use sunscreen, and so on. I don't know if concentrating on skin UV exposures 'corrects' my minimalist retinal pathway for natural light, or, if IOL replacements will ever be an option for me.

Like you, I would like to learn more about what I can expect from leaning primarily to UV skin exposure, and what more I could do to address the fact my retinal pathways are limited to less than 40% normal UV penetration... This, along with dietary Leptin sensitivity efforts, DHA from seafood, hydration, and monitoring nnEMFs in my home. After some 40 years of essentially a sunless lifestyle, I am near my first anniversary joining this site and learning from Dr. Kruse and the Forum.

Please, keep the faith in consulting with exceptional medical care providers that you can trust for your immediate discovery of the mass. The better specialists factor in a patient's 'whole' health status; one can get the feel for that in the consult communications. Instead of fear, consider your intuition honing in on the best guidance for you in these present hefty decisions to make. I so hope the urology team provides excellent options for taking care of that mass as soon as possible. I look forward to when this crisis is past and we'll have your welcome participation and contribution toward pre-existing medical and aging concerns leading up to now. God Bless.

**One approach** is to destroy the cancer cells and the virus. A good analogy would be a singer singing a note that can shatter glass.

**The other approach** is to assist the mutated cell to a healthy operating condition by bolstering its energy, increasing its frequency and returning it to the state of a healthy cell. If the cell's oscillations are normal and strong then the virus cannot replicate inside of it.

### Inger — 2018-06-23T20:26:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cancer-journey.21492/post-244178>

> [Sheddie said:](/goto/post?id=244067)

> At this time, I am concentrating on skin exposure as my best route of UV exposure. For this, it was doable to put up a privacy fence and take the sun in full nudity despite the fact I have had one melanoma surgery on a shoulder site, and get strong advice from my oncologist to avoid the sun, use sunscreen, and so on.

Sheddie, you got melanoma, even if you barely was in the sun all your life?

One of my bosses got melanoma this year too… and she has always been avoiding the sun…..said she could never tolerate it.
The melanoma was/is on the right side of her neck... a place that get really strongly irradiated when you are on the cellphone a lot….
I have told her about how important the sun still is, but it is hard for her. She is a doctor too, a Vet. She/her doctor did not even measure her vit D levels. which must be really low…. I told her she really need to do that test and she said she will do. I guess it must be really hard when you get so strongly warned about the sun from your doctor.

### Sheddie — 2018-06-24T09:37:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cancer-journey.21492/post-244217>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=244178)
>
> Sheddie, you got melanoma, even if you barely was in the sun all your life?
>
> One of my bosses got melanoma this year too… and she has always been avoiding the sun…..said she could never tolerate it.
> The melanoma was/is on the right side of her neck... a place that get really strongly irradiated when you are on the cellphone a lot….
> I have told her about how important the sun still is, but it is hard for her. She is a doctor too, a Vet. She/her doctor did not even measure her vit D levels. which must be really low…. I told her she really need to do that test and she said she will do. I guess it must be really hard when you get so strongly warned about the sun from your doctor.

Inger, I could not now believe that sun and sunburn directly and exclusively cause skin cancer, or even put us at especially great risk for melanoma. That is too simplistic to believe, although it does create a market for products. True, as a kid I would snorkel every summer day for hours in a lake and got bad sunburns each year across just my shoulders. The 'lucky' find last year of a 'stage 1a' of the worst kind of melanoma was from a while-I-was-there-for-something-else in the dermatologist office -- a chance biopsy of a small flat pink blot that had been unchanged on my shoulder as long as I could remember. As an undergraduate I worked summers as a bartender to make enough to live through the school year without needing to work more than some small part-time student's job. Since I tended bar in evenings, I made daytime use of a nude beach for those three summers near my great summer job. That was the last time I paid any attention to getting sunlight or a tan -- until last year.

Of course, there were a few beach vacation weeks over the next 40 years but my main regular and preferred recreation was weekend hiking and primitive camping in always-shady forests. I was a mother, with a full-time job, and slowly getting through part-time graduate school at the university where I worked. Really, I don't know how I did all I did at that age, but for sure, I never had a minute to linger outdoors or take off my glasses to consciously take in the sun or early bright daylight. I'm sure there must be a number of factors that combine for someone to develop skin and other cancers. I didn't avoid the sun; I just ignored it and spent my days under artificial light as well as being a 'night owl' still awake past midnight, maybe sleep deficient averaging 6.5 hours.

A reasonable explanation for rapidly rising cancer and autoimmune diseases likely has a strong relationship to the basic workplace design for working adults. This coming after the environment designed for children in school for years. Here, I'm learning what supports mitochondrial health and what seems absolutely vital to maintain general health over a long life span. The present natural-light deficient lifestyle is something even high income career/professional workplaces do not correct. I also had a workplace chemical exposure over a number of years, followed by allergies, and today I'm a diagnosed 'chemical sensitive.' Some environmental assaults we may encounter we simply never know are dangerous, how to avoid, much less how to protect from, or even recover from. We think maybe government bureaus like OSHA are doing that? Where are the 'environmental/occupational medicine' research departments? There was only one in the country when I wanted to know more about long-term/low level solvent poisoning. The literature was sparse, too, mainly 'covered' by some evaluation of apparent wasting disease among some Italian shoe factory workers. The remedy, from health care experts, was no more sophisticated than, well, just avoid solvents for the future. Easier said than done.

Early in my new status as disabled, no medical history-taking ever brought up the subject of possible dangers I might have been exposed to at work. I always mentioned my years of solvent exposure but it wouldn't be included by the medical people 'trained' to take medical histories. Later, after I needed a back surgery (big chemical exposure event there!) I never recovered from the post-op onset of what was to become three autoimmune disease labels. There is no medical treatment to reverse these co-morbidities, only drug 'treatments' for symptom management -- which I think would have killed a chemical sensitive like me by now had I not refused them. I was also a lacto-ovo vegetarian for 25 years and was probably pushing myself toward eventual "job burnout" on the verge of thyroid failure at the time I injured my back in my early 40s. Different events happen to different people, but it seems we have chronic degenerative illness too much in common around similar age milestones.

It makes sense to me that over time, meaning the same as in aging, risk statistics for cancers, autoimmune diseases, heart disease, and stroke may have a solid common denominator in how much natural light enters into population lifestyle over a lifetime -- for everyone at every age in a world that has dumped ever more mega tons per annum of toxic synthetic chemicals into the 'natural' environment. We could, by now, be experiencing what happens after reaching a saturation limit for toxin overload. There should by now be at least another 50 or more publications like Rachel Carson's _Silent Spring,_ the eye-opening _s_tudy on the chemicals in DDT. If tumors are the body's way of walling off toxins it cannot eliminate, where's the research reporting on what's contained in tumors (carcinogens?) after surgical removal? Or, would that information, publicly available, open a can of worms that global corporate entities are not willing to be responsible for? With a 15% chance of developing a second melanoma, I decided to add to my 'lifestyle' sunlight and hydration, and reduce wireless radiation; not ignore the sun like I had, or avoid it because mass media memes bombard us with 'sun-causes-skin-cancer' messages. In fact, I'm thinking my minimum 44 minutes/day, weather permitting, might not be enough since I haven't felt or seen a sunburn once yet this year...

> I'm wearing them now however

Keep at it ... it's not just about your eyeballs. At all.

# Thread: My personal introduction
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-personal-introduction.21494/>

### Corey Nelson — 2018-06-20T02:26:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-personal-introduction.21494/post-243832>

Hello,

I've known of Jack for about 7-8 years and had always thought his protocols seemed valid and very interesting (it seems so hard to deny that light and cold will alter physiology and gene expression - and little did I know there was so much more to it) but for most of that time, my youthful arrogance kept me from diving in.

As you will see in my signature a lot of that arrogance caught up with me, in 2014-16 or so. A couple of years of putting my life back on track, and I began to investigate and apply the protocols, somewhat slowly.

Late last year I said "why not" and went into deep dive mode; I've been all in since then, and everything I've learned and applied has worked phenomenally well for me (also see signature) and always leads me to the next step. Not to mention the profound truths that really undercut a lot of what I thought I knew.

That's fine; being vulnerable and brand new is a great place to grow.

One of my next steps is to become a gold or platinum member, and I'm pretty sure eventually I'll go for the Quantum Health Practitioner listing with whatever that entails. Currently able to afford the Patreon only but grateful for everything.

At the moment I'm just honored to be here and interact with a community built around this remarkable, abrasive, and kind individual's ideas and wisdom

Corey

PS: Does anyone know of a way to see posts \*I\* have "Liked" or another convenient way to sort/create a favorites list of individual posts, within the forum? Still exploring the mechanics. Thanks.

Hi Corey and welcome. It has always been amazing to me how this all makes complete sense and has from the very beginning - even without any kind of a science background.

The three legged stool is all encompassing and is all we need to get started and keep going.....

I love your story of acceptance and denial and then arriving at vulnerable and brand new and ready to have skin in the game!

Being brand new and vulnerable and open is a great place to be - I remember the day very well!

looking forward to your journal....

sorry I don't know the answer to your tech question - but someone else will I am sure.

### Inger — 2018-06-20T17:55:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-personal-introduction.21494/post-243884>

Welcome Corey
Excited to hear more about your deep dive (awesome  ), details and what you changed - what the results where

### Corey Nelson — 2018-06-20T19:01:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-personal-introduction.21494/post-243892>

> [Jenelle said:](/goto/post?id=243875)
>
> Hi Corey ~ if you hover over your name in top right corner, then click on "Your Profile Page" ~ you can then click on your activity and find your posts and things you've liked. It may not be exactly what you are looking for, but it's been helpful to me. Welcome!

That is exactly the type of thing I was looking for. Now I have a quick and easy way to see everything I've liked in one place. Thank you.

### Corey Nelson — 2018-06-20T19:21:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-personal-introduction.21494/post-243894>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=243884)
>
> Welcome Corey
> Excited to hear more about your deep dive (awesome  ), details and what you changed - what the results where

Thank you.

Deep is kind of a relative term I suppose, I'm as deep as I can cognitively get for now but not pushing myself in an unkind way. I haven't gone as deep as I plan to in terms of understanding - definitely getting there slowly and steadily though, and using many practical recommendations every single day (mostly all in my signature).

Moving on to results - I think it's hard to really pinpoint exactly what it feels like having better "quality of life" but that seems to be the most noticeable overall effect. For sure I feel more patient, accepting, and happy with less (I think I've heard Jack K. talk about this). There's really a great sense of belonging and being pretty happy with my situation even though it isn't "perfect." Less obsession. This is both a great effect of healing and a route to further healing.

I really appreciate Buddhist philosophy and to some extent practice (though, I have been practicing less meditation recently, just feels like the correct thing at the moment - following my intuition here) and so to me being less obsessed and more content are some of the real keys in life.

More objectively speaking I am noticing a massive improvement in mood, better sleep, sex drive is way way up (can easily be with my wife every single day as she prefers, which was never the case before in our 2.5 years of marriage - speaking as a guy that's a major change!), slow and steady improvements in energy, and symptom relief from erratically occurring heart palpitations and angioedema and urticaria. Previously I'd get swelling episodes where my lip would blow up about triple size and my body would get large, itchy, angry hives. Swelling now happens, albeit to a lesser degree, if and only if I end up sitting near a router for more than an hour or so, or heavily use a cell phone.

After listening to Matt Maruca some I also quit exercising (had been lifting with kettlebells once per week, running once a week, hiking once a week, and doing a mobility type day) because I realized I was way too fatigued and foggy to be in the right place to do that. That's been another great thing for me, just parting with that habit as an absolutely necessary part of life and knowing it's there to enjoy when I'm well.

Quit caffeine for the most part (I've had it maybe once every 2-4 weeks this year, usually in tea form) and that really improved sleep but also increased brain fog. I still feel like I'm usually at about 50-60% of my former peak cognitive capacity but I can find other things to enjoy and appreciate in life. I just feel happier and I can be happy at this level. That's currently, along with my fatigue, my most noticeable symptom, though. I just don't have the depth I used to intellectually or it takes a lot longer to get there, and I can't really focus on things for more than an hour or two at a time before I get fatigued mentally and need to do something restful.

Actually, I should clarify - 50-60% of former absolute peak is enough to live a good and productive life, and it may be ideal to be here most of the time with occasional bursts of insight and productivity, definitely better than trying to always force the ceiling higher and stay at peak all the time. Also, I think I am currently more likely to listen to myself and my body when I need a rest or a break - after years of working through pain and discomfort and basically creating a neoliberal inhuman existence for myself (partly out of motivation to succeed and partly just having something to prove). Very unhealthy habits of mind that I think are absolutely socially conditioned. Huge paradigm shift, not having much to prove. SO despite sounding a bit burnt out still I don't really FEEL that way, I just have a feeling of being realistic and it is often the case that I back away from something requiring intense focus after not so long - if it changes, great, but that's where I am.

Based on what had happened and the foolishness I was engaged in going back to 2011-2014 - use of modafinil and an Rx stimulant, absolute caffeine abuse, blue light way too often (I knew better but got complacent and stopped wearing my Uvexes I had at the time), EMFs 24/7, some really serious life changing trauma to cap it all off - I'm lucky I only got out with what I did.

If it takes years to level off and be "healed" that's okay!

Oh, I also restarted cannabis in 2015 after a 10 year period of having quit it, and it wasn't a bad crutch - helped with the trauma and (I now know) regulating mitochondrial function despite blue light and EMF damage - but I got a big wakeup call in the form of some near fatal cannabinoid hyperemesis episodes (body temp dropped to 95f at one point, dehydration making me so weak I could barely get help to go to ER) - and now that I'm entirely off the weed again (and glad to be) that's a new healing chapter, too.

I noticed that most of my issues were extremely "nonlinear" in their onset and wow, was that a wakeup call. I really am fascinated and horrified to see what begins happening individually and societally as these devastating and rapid effects begin to leap down to the next lower age groups of young "millennials" and "generation Y."

My old neighbor who's a year older than me, when I was living in an EMF hell (landlord in attached premise had dozens of wifi network devices and there were 3 smart meters, the suites were partially below ground), had the following all within the space of a year: hysterectomy, stroke with loss of function in one eye (now regained), and a mental breakdown where she got the police called and was evicted for attacking her boyfriend. Really sad. She is/was a gamer so there's your case study in 24/7 EMF + PM blue light.

I think I covered the rest in my signature but happy to share whatever I can, for curiosity sake or in case it helps anyone.

### drezy — 2018-06-20T22:26:26+0300

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=243903)
>
> Not true......all one needs is sunlight for this to work. No labs needed.

FWIW the very first time I picked up my grandmother's BP cuff I was 14. My BP was 140/90. It's easy to remember because one of my uncles saw and said "Oh shit!" Surely no fun first experience. Pretty much every doc visit on record measured it at that range.

My hands were ALWAYS cold to the touch. I think that even limited some social interaction. People wince when you put a cold hand on them.

I opened my ears up around here over the last year (sun baking, cold, and nnemf avoidance strategies) I measure 120/70 regularly. My wife still regularly tells me that my hands seem so warm now.

To answer the thread question. The penny dropped for me when I decided to go all in and prove Doctor Kruse's well meaning but way over the top theories wrong. I noticed that many members weren't all in (lots of cold aversion) so I'd go as all in as I could. I'd try it out find it fails and move on.

Well, I'm still here and that says it all. I've never been a sports fan, but every month's webinar and Q&A is like my Super Bowl. I get 24 Superbowls a year.

I love how we have collided on this path......... it has been a resurrection of life and light for me.

### Mike David — 2018-06-29T00:36:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-did-the-penny-drop-for-you.21495/post-244522>

First time in the cave today but probably not as cool as Jacks cave but still very awesome. I found Jack on YouTube while looking up Graham Hancock videos. Graham told me how important it was to question the so called experts and make good observations. Needless to say Jack was singing the same song. But was this another half assed scheme cooked up on the internet? Well he wasn’t selling me anything in fact had a lot of great content for free! ALOT. His message hit home getting in touch with nature was something I knew deep down I had to do. Everything made good sense and is backed by awesome observations and good science. Despite that I tried in vein to discredit his teachings. Needless to say epic fail. Things really took hold this last winter I started reading the books he suggests and randomly reading blogs. I found the books helped a lot with the terminology and the general mindset of a good mad scientist (digging deeper). I bought a emf detector and blue blockers and starting spending most of my time outside with my one year old playing and studying. I found the info. Took much better once I dropped the old paradigm linear thinking and went all in mentally. Started eating seafood every day and drinking shit loads of good water. I haven’t seen a dr. In like 5 years but I was feeling tired a lot and had plenty of aches and pains. After doing just the easy stuff I dropped 20 lbs. in three months no more aces and pains and for the first time since I was little I don’t have white spots on my fingernails. Skin cleared up and looks great. Now it’s time to step it up. Starting CT and daily fasting getting religious about sunrises no matter what and taking detailed notes while systematically consuming this blog. Also am going for the gold has to help in consuming this blog. Thanks to all of the great people on the forum.

# Thread: What is the appropriate latitude for my genes?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-appropriate-latitude-for-my-genes.21497/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Nicole O'Neil — 2018-06-20T21:48:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-appropriate-latitude-for-my-genes.21497/post-243897>

Hi everyone,

I have some 23andme data I would like to share and was hoping to get some input on what my optimal latitude location would be?

My mother's haplogroup is 12a. Here are my homozygous mutations: VDR tag, MAO-A R297R, BHMT-02, BHMT-04, BHMT-08 and here are my heterozygous mutations: COMT V158M, COMT H62H, MTR A2756G, MTRR A66G, MTRR A664A. SHMT1 C1420T.

Is this all the information needed? I heard Jack mention this in one of his presentations.

Also, another question. Morning sunlight is typically between 6AM-9AM. The sun rises at different times all year round, how can we determine the optimal timeframe with the changing seasons?

Thanks!

### Jack Kruse — 2018-06-20T21:51:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-appropriate-latitude-for-my-genes.21497/post-243898>

[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Haplogroup](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Haplogroup)

### Nicole O'Neil — 2018-07-09T16:08:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-appropriate-latitude-for-my-genes.21497/post-245425>

**Haplogroup L. Is this the correct information needed?**
**
Thank you**

### Jack Kruse — 2018-07-09T16:10:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-appropriate-latitude-for-my-genes.21497/post-245426>

L is equatorial......

### JanSz — 2018-07-09T18:50:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-appropriate-latitude-for-my-genes.21497/post-245456>

**

> [Nicole O'Neil said:](/goto/post?id=245425)
>
> **Haplogroup L. Is this the correct information needed?**
> **
> Thank you**

Haplogroup L** may refer to:

-   [Haplogroup L (Y-DNA)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Haplogroup_L_\(Y-DNA\)), a human Y-chromosome (Y-DNA) haplogroup
-   [Macro-haplogroup L (mtDNA)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Macro-haplogroup_L_\(mtDNA\)), a human mitochondrial DNA (mtDNA) macrohaplogroup that is at the root of the human mitochondrial phylogenetic tree. Its subclades are:
    -   [Haplogroup L0 (mtDNA)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Haplogroup_L0_\(mtDNA\)), a human mitochondrial DNA (mtDNA) haplogroup
    -   [Haplogroup L1 (mtDNA)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Haplogroup_L1_\(mtDNA\)), a human mitochondrial DNA (mtDNA) haplogroup
    -   [Haplogroup L2 (mtDNA)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Haplogroup_L2_\(mtDNA\)), a human mitochondrial DNA (mtDNA) haplogroup
    -   [Haplogroup L3 (mtDNA)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Haplogroup_L3_\(mtDNA\)), a human mitochondrial DNA (mtDNA) haplogroup
    -   [Haplogroup L4 (mtDNA)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Haplogroup_L4_\(mtDNA\)), a human mitochondrial DNA (mtDNA) haplogroup
    -   [Haplogroup L5 (mtDNA)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Haplogroup_L5_\(mtDNA\)), a human mitochondrial DNA (mtDNA) haplogroup
    -   [Haplogroup L6 (mtDNA)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Haplogroup_L6_\(mtDNA\)), a human mitochondrial DNA (mtDNA) haplogroup
    -   Haplogroup L7, a human mitochondrial DNA (mtDNA) haplogroup; now [Haplogroup L4a (mtDNA)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Haplogroup_L4a_\(mtDNA\))


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Thread: December 2017 paper on melanopsin being in skin/fat/vessels means what?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/december-2017-paper-on-melanopsin-being-in-skin-fat-vessels-means-what.21500/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 1

### Jack Kruse — 2018-06-20T23:35:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/december-2017-paper-on-melanopsin-being-in-skin-fat-vessels-means-what.21500/post-243913>

What connects your inner light to your filament? Is your life electric because your cells make a plasma called water? Is your filament, called your gut, connected properly to your brain by sunlight and water? Are your brain and gut set up via coherent coupling of cycles between food and light of the day in your retina and brain? The vagus nerve does maintain this "watery connection" between the brain and gut over a water electric network. These two systems are harmonic oscillators that need to be properly coupled to work together. What connects these two oscillators in our body? Water networks do in both organs. Don't we realize it? Sunlight makes the water in our cells a liquid crystalline PLASMA. That water is created by our mitochondria and this is how eukaryotes to bring the sea onto a land-based existence. Liquid crystalline = coherent water = exclusion zone water. Liquid crystalline water possesses long-range orientational order by pointing all the molecules by pointing in the same direction. It also allows for a translational order that allows them to keep their position as they move. Liquid crystals are mobile and flexible and highly responsive. This allows them to undergo rapid changes in orientation or phase transitions when energy is added or subtracted from the water. This is why cell water responds vigorously to exogenous electric or magnetic fields. It is why water responds vigorously to temp, pressure, pH, hydration, and concentrations of inorganic ions like phosphorus. I think the Universe is powered by water electricity and I believe every cell is powered in the same way. CSF comes from the water in blood plasma and then its chemistry is changed by the choroid plexus of the brain by iodine and salt. Salt levels control the physics of the exclusion zone of CSF. In my opinion, It is the single most key mechanism in the brain from a quantum perspective. It changes the redox potential of the entire organ. Water is the energy that life runs on. Water is the universal electron and proton donor. When you understand these concepts, you begin to see that proton and electron currents in water made by sunlight create currents inside of cells and over extracellular distances that are capable of delivering physical and chemical messages concerning the redox status of all parts of the cell. Water networks communicate this information by way of the vagus nerve. The water in blood plasma is a non-homogenous mix of water that has low UV absorption rate that can be changed as its dielectric constant changes with the addition of salt, anions, and iodine. Once the water in blood plasma becomes partially structured, this fraction of cellular water has better UV light assimilation as its viscosity and refraction are altered. This is a clue for those who still remain vitamin D3 deficient despite getting "adequate" sunlight and/or use tanning beds that some other process in them remains lacking.

Water is redox in humans. Water molecules are more than just made up of hydrogen and oxygen ions. These ions have electric charges and form dipoles. Water is not inert, its ions are dynamic, always rotating and vibrating. Due to the various bonds and kinks that form among water molecules, clusters of water can emit a multitude of electromagnetic frequencies depending on the number of water molecules and their bonds in the clusters. With different structures of water clusters, different electromagnetic frequencies are produced. This means that water can resonate at any virtually any frequency. How does it do this? Water must first absorb ambient electromagnetic radiation to re-emit it. Sunlight can activate oxygen’s outer electrons to higher energy levels and in 0.0000001 seconds, returns to ground level, emitting photons with a frequency of about 1,000,000,000,000,000 Hertz. Water is, in fact, a repository for electromagnetic radiation of all types. The human optical windows, however, operate precisely between 250nm-780nm. Proteins can utilize 200nm -900nm light. Water consists almost entirely of a type of elementary particle called a photon (a photon is a boson and bosons are the glue that holds matter together) and only a very small amount of water is actual matter and it is hypothesized that water is 974,600,000,000 parts photons to 1 part matter (hydrogen and oxygen). Water is mostly photons and photons are the force carrier (messengers of information) of the electromagnetic field. This explains how electromagnetic information is propagated at the molecular level, via water. All biomolecules are dependent on water. We are made up mostly of water, specifically structured, liquid crystalline water, which is imbued with a multitude of electromagnetic frequencies. We are always immersed in a plethora of natural and unnatural electromagnetic radiation in our environments, some beneficial, others harmful and it is constantly changing the liquid crystalline water in our bodies faster than we can think. The ambient electromagnetic environment is literally changing the way living water behaves in our bodies to create health or to fuel disease. This has tremendous implications for biology and medicine.This sets in motion the requisite core of chemical reactions that restore the local and global energy balance of all living things. The vagus nerve is our electric filament, the spark of life, found between seas of water in us. It is that nerve that connects two organs photoelectrically using water as its plasma to make free electrons/protons. Water is present on either end of this nerve. You must become a water muse, first and foremost. The vagus nerve controls the parasympathetic portion of the autonomic nervous system as the picture below shows.

It begins in the area postrema of the 4th ventricle. The vagus is part of the parasympathetic system that lowers the stress response. Its activity drops chronically when the sympathetic system is constantly turned on. The sympathetic system outflow of the stress stimulus begins in the paraventricular nucleus (PVN). The vagus nerve is the calming portion of the ANS and antagonizes the PVN to lower stress and INFLAMMATION. The vagus nerve is also key in allowing mitochondria to oscillate at 100 Hz to fat burn. Balancing both arms is critical in avoiding diseases, and creating allostasis. Autonomic nervous system (ANS) regulation is a function of light and water properly activating one another to control magnetic flux in the spinning ATPase on the 5th cytochrome. The vagus nerve connects dissimilar "bodies of water" within the human body to make them work coherently. They use the light in that environment to make a connection or allow a mitochondrial disconnection which disrupts energy flows. The stimulus of light on our skin and retina changes the water domains in CSF in the brain and in the gut. 99.8% of CSF is made up of cell water. This is how the “central digital” circadian system from the RPE of the eye, the skin, and gut are linked to the SCN which sits in a cistern of CSF above your optic nerves in your skull. This allows all mitochondria to vibrate as one. The “analog circadian system” of the gut is linked to solar light exposure from food electrons and to the timing of food grown in the environment that cells get in our mitochondria so that the signals can be yoked via the area postrema (AP) and the median eminence (ME) in the brain. These two areas in the brain have no blood-brain barrier. They allow water and light communication to occur, so the electron density in the CSF is accurately tied to the local environment these neurons can sense. Electron density and coolness are linked in water. This is true in the ocean and in our cells and CSF cavities. For example, when we eat, 60% of blood flow is shunted from the peripheral blood to the gut’s mesenteric system by the autonomic nervous system. The brain senses and pays attention to these shifts, using the vagus nerve, as its main information highway. When the sun hits our skin (UVA and IRA light), nitric acid (NO) is released and 40-60% of blood flow raises to the skin surface to become radiated by solar radiation. This energy transfer is sensed by the vagus nerve in our superior cervical ganglion and sent to the brain to lower our stress response in the PVN. This stimulates melatonin cycles to work properly. So if your lifestyle buries the sun, you create a stress response and if you do it long enough you might end up with a disease.

(**This means ever skin cell in your body irradiated by blue light causes a circadian mismatch and lowers melatonin WHEREVER the irradiation occurs.
Nobody realizes that the skin can destroy the gut because of this Dec 2017 paper because enterocytes are designed to be turned over ever 24-48 hours......and that connection is made by the vagus nerve. It is a GAME CHANGE**R)

Cells are designed to be a repository of collectible wisdom from nature’s energy and information buried in sunlight and geomagnetic pulsations of Earth. Blue light irradiation of the eye or skin DESTROY THAT LIBRARY OF WISDOM.

That energy and data come to cells as waveforms and their energy and data are stored in water. The energy is coupled in cycles and not thermalized. This is how cells remain far from equilibrium. Given our cellular design to capture and collected and decipher waves, no human being itself should be considered impaired innately, instead, there are environmental shortcomings that cause the impairment. _Materials:_

The perfect diet.
The perfect exercise program.
Every supplement they desire.

_Protocol_:

The subject lives in a pitch black room shielded from light at all times and and the subject is shielded from all native electromagnetic fields. (This is performed for an indefinite period of time.)

No Light. No magnetism.

Everything else.

What happens?

After one day? Five days? Two weeks?

What happens?

# Thread: Corey's Optimal Journal

### Corey Nelson — 2018-06-22T03:39:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coreys-optimal-journal.21504/post-244013>

Hello everyone.

Quote is from Zen Master Suzuki-Roshi. It is in the title as a reminder to me. I also like this one from Jack (paraphrased): every problem contains the seed of a solution, or necessary change.

Big thanks to Jack and all the members. I've been lurking and learning so much and increasing my wisdom all year thanks to all of you. Happy to be a contributing member of this community now .

Soon I will put a condensed version of my rambling intro posts ([https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/my-personal-introduction.21494/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/my-personal-introduction.21494/)) here as a summary of where I am, currently.

Just checked my genomic report for mtDNA haplogroup, and it is H2b which traces to the Unetice culture in around 49.5N latitude as well as the Yamnaya in the 53.4N so that is interesting and a bit lucky...

Ending on an image that made me smile today. Today wasn't too easy, worked about 3 hours and napped about 3 hours and it can sometimes be frustrating that I'm not as healed as I'd like to be - but I remain grateful for my progress.

### Corey Nelson — 2018-07-01T03:01:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coreys-optimal-journal.21504/post-244641>

Here's an edited-down version of my rambling intro post. The next post after this one will be a general series of updates from the past week or so.

_Which protocols?_ See my signature for every protocol/Rx I am following and as of the time of writing this I've been doing most of 0those for about 6-7 months, i.e. since the end of last year. Some (methylene blue) are more recent.

_On applying Kruse protocols:_ I'm as deep as I can cognitively get for now but not pushing myself in an unkind way. I haven't gone as deep as I plan to in terms of understanding - definitely getting there slowly and steadily though, and using many practical recommendations every single day (mostly all in my signature).

_On results, patience, and acceptance:_ better quality of life seems to be the most noticeable overall effect.I feel more patient, accepting, and happy with less (I think I've heard Jack K. talk about this). There's really a great sense of belonging and being pretty happy with my situation even though it isn't "perfect." Less obsession. This is both a great effect of healing and a route to further healing.

_More on tangible results: m_assive improvement in mood, better sleep, sex drive is way way up (can easily be with my wife every single day as she prefers, which was never the case before in our 2.5 years of marriage - speaking as a guy that's a major change!), slow and steady improvements in energy, and symptom relief from erratically occurring heart palpitations and angioedema and urticaria. Previously I'd get swelling episodes where my lip would blow up about triple size and my body would get large, itchy, angry hives. Swelling now happens, albeit to a lesser degree, if and only if I end up sitting near a router for more than an hour or so, or heavily use a cell phone.

_Taking a meditation break:_ Have had a consistent meditation practice (daily) for a few years now, took about a month off recently, now back to daily in the AM sun.
_On quitting exercise:_ After listening to Matt Maruca some I also quit exercising (had been lifting with kettlebells once per week, running once a week, hiking once a week, and doing a mobility type day) because I realized I was way too fatigued and foggy to be in the right place to do that. That's been another great thing for me, just parting with that habit as an absolutely necessary part of life and knowing it's there to enjoy when I'm well.

_More on patience:_ Both quitting exercise and taking a meditation break felt like the kindest things I could do for myself, and I interpret this as a sort of acceptance of the process of healing rather than constantly striving to "do something" in order to accelerate it.
_Quitting caffeine, brain fog:_ Quit caffeine for the most part (I've had it maybe once every 2-4 weeks this year, usually in tea form) and that change has really improved sleep but also subjectively increased brain fog (clearly it was masking the symptom, obviously). However I don't miss it and now recognize it was disconnecting me from my body's rhythms.

_Dealing with fatigue, but overall happier:_ I still feel like I'm usually at about 50-60% of my former all-time peak cognitive capacity of several years ago, but I can find other things to enjoy and appreciate in life. I just feel happier and I can be happy at this level. A bit of cognitive lag (again more relative to myself than relative to some absolute or other-person based standard - I don't feel it's noticeable), along with my fatigue, is my most noticeable symptom, though. I just don't have the depth I used to intellectually or it takes a lot longer to get there, and I can't really focus on things for more than an hour or two, three tops, at a time before I get fatigued mentally and need to do something restful. There is no reason to assume this is permanent and I feel pretty accepting and patient about it.

_Self-acceptance, rejecting neoliberalism and self-commodification, and paradigm shifts:_ I think I am currently more likely to listen to myself and my body when I need a rest or a break - after years of working through pain and discomfort and basically creating a neoliberal inhuman existence for myself (partly out of motivation to succeed and partly just having something to prove). Very unhealthy habits of mind that I think are absolutely socially conditioned. Huge paradigm shift, not having much to prove.

_Lucky and grateful:_ Based on what had happened and the foolishness I was engaged in going back to 2011-2014 - use of modafinil and an Rx stimulant, absolute caffeine abuse, blue light way too often (I knew better but got complacent and stopped wearing my Uvexes I had at the time), EMFs 24/7, some really serious life changing trauma to cap it all off - I'm lucky I only got out with what I did. If it takes years to level off and be "healed" that's okay!
_
Cannabis (now quit also) as a crutch, hyperemesis scares_: I also restarted cannabis in 2015 after a 10 year period of having quit it, and it wasn't a bad crutch - helped with the trauma and (I now know) regulating mitochondrial function despite blue light and EMF damage - but I got a big wakeup call in the form of some near fatal cannabinoid hyperemesis episodes (body temp dropped to 95f at one point, dehydration making me so weak I could barely get help to go to ER) - and now that I'm entirely off the weed again this year (and glad to be) that's a new healing chapter, too.
_Nonlinearity and symptoms jumping to younger generations:_ I noticed that most of my issues were extremely "nonlinear" in their onset and wow, was that a wakeup call. I really am fascinated and horrified to see what begins happening individually and societally as these devastating and rapid effects begin to leap down to the next lower age groups of young "millennials" and "generation Y." There are SHIT TONS of memes from teens/early 20s complaining about being tired all the time.
_My neighbor the EMF/blue light casualty:_ My old neighbor who's a year older than me, when I was living in an EMF hell (landlord in attached premise had dozens of wifi network devices and there were 3 smart meters, the suites were partially below ground), had the following all within the space of a year: hysterectomy, stroke with loss of function in one eye (now regained), and a mental breakdown where she got the police called and was evicted for attacking her boyfriend. Really sad. She is/was a gamer so there's your case study in 24/7 EMF + PM blue light.

Again see signature for a succinct discussion of my protocols. Actually here's a current copy for preservation since it is liable to change over the duration of my membership here:

**Age:** 31 **Sex:** M **Joined:** June 2018 / **Began** CT & Other Protocols Dec. 2017
**Location:** NC, USA (36.2N Latitude; **mtDNA** haplogroup H2b (Unetice/Yamnaya)=49.5N to 53.4N Latitude)
**Dx:** PTSD, Cannabinoid hyperemesis syndrome\*, EHS (symptoms: urticaria, heart palpitations, angioedema)
_\*interested to discuss CHS as a mitochondrial disease (TRPV-1 pathway, scalding hot showers, low body temps, ECS involvement)_
**Current Therapies / Protocols:** CT ~3x/wk (45-60+ min, 30lb ice) | UV/IR indoors | AM noon and PM sun (+fasting in UV for mitophagy) | Daily Seafood | Seasonal and LCHF eating for 2H depletion | Aquafina/Pellegrino/Iceland Glacial water | Methylene Blue
**Health WINS:** PTSD symptom free, no cannabis use and no more CHS, extremely cold adapted, morning sun every day this year, daily seafood, fixed my EMF and light environment, can tolerate 3+ hours strong midday UV without burning.
**Health Goals:** Improve fatigue, lifetime protocol adherence, permaculture/homestead self-sufficiency, test deuterium/heteroplasmy/DHA/telomeres, obtain 20 gauss Magnetico, more trips far South.
**Related Goals:** Upgrade **to** Gold or Platinum member, eventually Quantum Health Practitioner. Get caught up on recommended reading, webinars, etc.
**No surgeries/RXs**.​

### Corey Nelson — 2018-07-01T03:28:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coreys-optimal-journal.21504/post-244643>

Now for some random contemporaneous updates:

This past week was great for protocol compliance except skipping one CT bath (2 instead of 3), and especially got tons of sun (AM, noon, PM). Also happened to be a great week in terms of vivid dreams, good energy levels (on an upward trend), and great mood for the most part. Lots of seafood, too.

My methylene blue dosage since I began it about 2.5-3 weeks ago has been 10mg, 3x/day. As of today, I am bumping that up to 20mg per dose to gauge results. I am aware some people dose a mere 60mcg which seems to just be a random number pulled from nowhere, and that there is speculation about a U-shaped response curve. I am also aware safety data indicates much, much higher doses than I'm currently taking are safe so I'm willing to give the higher dose a chance.

I've been really enjoying a podcast called The Liminalist with Jasun Horsley and it touches on many areas I find interesting - high strangeness, conspiracy, self-actualization, mindfulness and spirituality. He's not like anyone I've ever listened to. Anyway the reason I bring it up is because he has some great things to say about "knots of shame" existing in the body and the concept of "fragmented self" as it relates to trauma. I've been using these some as tools for introspection (e.g. feeling a bit guilty about missing a CT bath, and sitting with that feeling). I could say a lot more but it's pretty personal and wouldn't really translate, so I'd just say check the podcast out for yourself if you want a treat, IMO.

Another podcast I enjoy in general is Skeptiko, with Alex Tsakiris. It isn't really the debunker type of skeptical show, though. In particular a couple of guests that have been very interesting recently are Bernardo Kastrup and Dr. Dean Radin. Both of them present some interesting concepts of why something like some version of idealism/platonism may be a better model for describing the universe than strict materialism. In that model, consciousness is at the "bottom" of the traditional epistemic scientific pyramid (consciousness?>physics>chemistry>biology>neurology>psychology>consciousness) underneath physics rather than at the very top. Seems like a good semi-related discussion to some of the things that come up around Dr. K's talks and papers.

I've noticed some days I get a really nice "buzz" that kicks in around mid-afternoon and sticks with me until bedtime, and this makes sense with what we know about POMC. It clearly correlates, for me, with both getting lots of morning sun, and with cultivating a sense of satisfaction and acceptance. Particularly when I allow myself to accept where I am and be happy with less rather than grasping, this buzz kicks in. It feels almost drug-like but very wholesome. I'm sure some of the other members will know what I mean. OH, another thing that definitely facilitates this is keeping EMF exposure as low as possible.

Closing with a photo - I had mentioned quitting exercise but I am doing a walk every morning now after I meditate in AM sun. The other form of activity I'm enjoying is cruising on a longboard when the urge hits, it's one of those things that is just so fun and flowing that it doesn't feel like exercise, more like a reward. This is from the other day - I skated around a river greenway then went wading in the river and did some sungazing while enjoying my POMC buzz . If anyone enjoys board sports of any sort I highly recommend longboarding as a fairly cheap, very fun, low impact way to carve and cruise around.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-07-01T05:21:09+0300

Stay focused on what matters in life; leave everything and do something you've never done before and eliminate the unnecessary. Time is malleable and we can create and recreate its meaning when we know what matters in life and let go of what doesn't.

### Corey Nelson — 2018-07-02T03:11:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coreys-optimal-journal.21504/post-244732>

Some general thoughts

Went on a nice waterfall hike today at a picnic in the mountains with family and friends of family.

Current supplements

-   Just stopped msm/organic sulfur after a few months (I think most supplementation should be temporary)
-   Zinc balance (with copper) every other day or so
-   Selenomethionine every other day or so
-   K2mk4 every other day or so
-   Occasionally mg glycinate
-   Kelp very occasionally for iodine, once every week or two (overdid this one a couple months ago and resulted in insomnia and heart palpitations, ie hyperthyroidal symptoms)

Have gotten comments from my wife to the effect that I'm looking very "solid" and that's despite not doing any exercise aside from light stuff mentioned above for about 6-8 weeks. Leanness and muscularity seem to be up, weighing in the low 180s, just feeling much better overall since giving myself a break.

### Corey Nelson — 2018-07-17T00:45:24+0300

All is well and the gradual upward trend continues.

A couple of small adjustments to the regimen:

-   Taking a break from methylene blue (also, didn't notice additional benefit @ 20mg 3x/day as opposed to 10mg 3x/day)
-   Instead of 3 CT sessions per week year round, going to do 2-3 per week in summer, and 3-5+ in the winter (mostly speaking of actual ice baths, but the shower water is cold enough and ambient temps low enough here in winter to get plenty of additional opportunities)

I have found one of my favorite times to get light now is actually in the evening. Still doing my sunrises and noon suns of course, but the subjective quality of each is quite different, and I'm loving the feeling specifically of the sunset and dusk. It's incredibly mellow and calming.

### Corey Nelson — 2018-07-28T01:35:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coreys-optimal-journal.21504/post-246819>

Nothing much to report that's new, except for one thing. Yesterday I smashed my phone (by accident!) and it stopped working; I expected to get a replacement today but that was delayed until tomorrow...

And it's like I'm having withdrawal symptoms.

I am VERY fastidious about blue light and reasonably good about EMFs, I keep it in airplane mode and mostly use it mindfully, don't really use it just because I'm feeling idle or to check notifications. I get plenty of AM sun and other times, etc.

But in its absence, I have realized I really am sort of addicted to communicating with family members, checking certain sites, and listening to podcasts, however infrequently. I'm a junkie for my favorite podcasts especially (none of whom would qualify as manipulative, IMO).

Just some food for thought. Hopefully this enforced break will do my dopamine some good. I'm alternately feeling frustrated about not having the device, and more tranquil. Good wake-up call.

### Corey Nelson — 2018-07-28T01:49:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coreys-optimal-journal.21504/post-246820>

Something to share for a bit of fun, the neighborhood has recently been selected to get an "upgrade" to smart meters, and the local monopoly company offers NO opt-out in this state (but they do in other states, and so do the neighboring companies - I'd write my congresspeople if I wasn't moving from this house soon - in fact I already pamphleted the neighborhood and drafted some letters but I just have more important things to focus on, it turns out).

Being that the analog meter was an attractive aspect of living here, I had a simple padlock on mine, and a "Keep Out" sign above the meter, knowing it wasn't impenetrable; but why not. Well, someone came by, stuck a door-hanger that said "We've installed a new smart meter" which I grabbed; went to the back, came back around to ring the doorbell a few times, and then gave up and left a door-hanger that said "Sorry we missed you - please call to schedule an appointment to have your new smart meter installed." Yeah, sure...

And now I'll let this pic speak for itself; it may take more than just a set of bolt-cutters to do an "upgrade" now  Wondering how long I can string this along by being non-compliant and refusing to discuss it.

### Corey Nelson — 2018-10-31T19:51:47+0200

Hi everyone, still at it, with nothing much to report BUT slow and steady improvement; less fatigue, better mood and productivity. My signature could use some minor updates but the general outline is still close enough! I'm excited as I'm coming up on the anniversary of when I started all of my current protocols, which would be sometime in the winter. Saw approximately my 300th consecutive sunrise not long ago.

A few small updates:

\-Currently on a break from methylene blue, but I've found two separate methods I like about equally well: 10mg 3x/day sublingual; and 80mg 3x/day oral.

\-Paying attention to Dave Oshana, enlightenment transmission teacher (for further info I recommend a 2013 episode of The Lonely Guru podcast on which he was a guest)

\-For breath work especially during CT or chilly mornings, I've decided my favorite is: x, 4x, 2x - that's inhale, hold, and exhale. Plug any number you like in for x; the two sequences I use most commonly are counts of 5-20-10, and 10-40-20. You could try 7-28-14 if you wanted. Warms me right up and feels great too. I learned it from a Tony Robbins tape of all places

### Corey Nelson — 2018-12-18T16:41:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coreys-optimal-journal.21504/post-258358>

Hello everyone,

Things are going well, still sticking to the mitochondriac lifestyle.

The wood stove in my new place was down for about a month, meaning the house stayed between 50-60f (with the exception of using room heaters). During this time I did not do ice baths, but I'm outside naked every morning in 20-30f weather, and have been doing lots of cold showers. I got in a cold mountain waterfall day before yesterday and it felt awesome, totally warm, then I sungazed. Just sublime.

I'm very cold-adapted but I haven't officially been doing ice baths, in other words.

Still eating oysters or sardines daily, getting as much sun and outside time as possible, etc.

No supplement use currently, and haven't missed them. Haven't felt a need for methylene blue either.

I got my D-TERMINATOR test results back and was surprised the results were both in the high 140s ppm deuterium. I am aware the test results may not be consistent (which is a damn shame) but I do plan to do a DDW protocol: up to 2.5L of 110ppm per day (made by mixing Qlarivia-25 with aquafina at a 3:1 ratio, which will cost about $11.38 per day) for 3 months in early 2019. Then I will retest.

Feeling much better with zero caffeine ever, and minimal alcohol (light social drinking a few times a month, mostly Mendoza Argentina malbecs).

I'm doing dry fasting a few times a week in the interest of depleting deuterium, and it's gotten really easy. Clearly my ADH levels have increased.

My productivity is up enormously and my fatigue symptoms are about 2-3 out of 10 most days. No need to nap.

I've begun lifting weights once per week again, and I'm taking a 5pm walk whenever I can.

Just went Platinum, here's to quantum health coaching and writing/blogging about it to help spread the word in 2019!

In other news looks like I have a very mild case of shingles. The rash onset was a few days ago and it's hardly been noticeable, but there's a mild tinge of pain just starting today, like a 1 on a scale of 1-100, barely noticeable.

I am guessing it is due to a temporary very bad environment of EMFs and the usual bullshit while traveling to see family and accompanying my wife while shopping in some stores, etc. Thankfully I am home now where the RF is <0.005mw/m^2 and the LF fields are very low in most areas. Been digital detoxing as much as possible, too, even before the rash hit.

Since I've been doing my naked 20 degree Fahrenheit mornings, sunbathing, CT, etc, I am guessing my redox has improved to the point where I didn't really get the usual prodromal symptoms and the rash (knock on wood) is miniscule and minimally annoying even though the bad environment triggered the onset. I've never had it before.

Fatigue is way, way down, can't remember the last time I took a nap, which is huge (and I love napping, just haven't felt like it).
