### Phosphene — 2018-11-12T22:54:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-255523>

I try not to get caught up in general news, but the California fires are getting to me. I spent a long-ago summer in Northridge (pre-earthquake) and, well, I’m just glad my instincts prevailed and I’m no longer anywhere near CA, beautiful as it is.

I think [@Matty\_M](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/11546/) is out there? Prayers/good vibrations for him and all other CA residents.

### caroline — 2018-11-13T05:24:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-255533>

Matty seems to be safe and sound and in Mexico ......even without his passport. Apparently he was able to cross the border by foot. with a driver's license and then get a flight to Cancum

### Phosphene — 2018-11-15T23:45:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-255714>

Ah Matty the intrepid traveler—angels have his back for sure and I can’t wait to witness all the good he does for the world. Saw there’s some kind of movie being filmed by/with him in PDC? Didn’t watch yet since I’m trying to stay off social media but difficult when so much new Kruse stuff seems to be there. Understandable for building the business empire but not very compatible with the teachings.

I’ve been in a funky mood all week since my friend was here. She was in awe of the obvious foundation of love that exists here, and how lucky I am to have a gem of a man who lives and breathes to love, support and forgive me. He really is my rock, holding everything together with the household when my health went off the rails over the years. Still have much to explore in why intimacy on all levels is still so difficult.

One thing I know for sure—it was a hellish mistake to get entrenched in a new relationship before being completely free of the marriage. I REALLY thought I was so close to freedom during the separation, and that I was only “technically” married while waiting for him to finally, finally agree that an amicable divorce was the best way out of the mess. I realize I was just repeating a pattern from early on of jumping ship when things got tough. Things are still tough, but it’s pretty obvious this man is in my life for a reason, despite our seemingly irreconcilable differences and lack of clear couple goals. Let’s hope I can figure it out before too much more time slips by.

As much as it may have been a hellish mistake, the inexpressibly deep relationship with my crippled foreign yogi held many blessings as well. I got word that he’s finally moving on and starting to date again, which is good of course, but bittersweet. I’m afraid we are quite permanently entangled and will always hold melded chunks of each other’s heart, mind and soul.

Another part of my funk is of course the departing sun, but this is the first time EVER that I’m going into winter with awe, gratitude and acceptance.

Husband is taking the boys into New York City this weekend for two small-ish rock/punk concerts—The Pixies and Jack White, plus some sightseeing and YMCA sleeping. Good bonding (not that they need it). I was invited but frankly those loud, bright concerts are no longer my thing, plus NYC 5G nonsense of course. This is tough since this is very much his thing.

I’m choosing to stay here all alone and dive deep—maybe even the electric totally off. A few candles, webinars, CT, chanting, yoga, simple fish, hot tea, member Q&A on Sunday. Maybe even a few minutes naked in the first snow of the season. That would be a first for sure!

Have a few looming tasks I need to complete and it will be so much more at ease going into this weekend retreat with those done and off my mind. Will be DONE by tomorrow afternoon. No excuses.

### Inger — 2018-11-16T13:13:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-255745>

Phosphene,
running away never helps...
If you stay fully present, accepting and open, soft and tender... do not close yourself, be vulnerable, and at the same time do what is true for you, it will work out exactly as it should!

I think we always have to begin with ourselves. To be true to ourselves.
It is not easy, I know. It is going to be painful too. But I cant see any other way.
I am still learning.......

### Phosphene — 2018-11-21T15:24:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-256176>

Thank you Inger. I’m indeed no longer physically running away from my situation, but I still find myself hiding from my disheveled mind. This past summer of relatively good sun exposure was so luscious and healing, but I can feel mood dropping fast now after the time and UV change, despite more CT. I am ramping it up now, pushing past the persisting aversion.

This is the faint remaining imprint from my first ever nude snow angel! (I did wear a hat so it’s oddly headless. That should be some kind of horror story—nude headless snow angel...)

The snow had been much deeper but drizzly rain followed and the 10 heavy, wet inches is disappearing fairly quickly.

I could only stay with bare skin against the snow and pesky drizzle on front for about 2 minutes before I retreated to my robe and went back upstairs. Then I got annoyed with myself for wimping our so quickly, did some deep breathing, and went back down. The second time there was less of the acute burning sensation of extreme cold, and I could relax into it more. I stayed for about 5 more minutes before my elbows started aching. Took a tepid shower about half an hour later, and the cool skin temp persisted beyond the shower for another hour or so. Some shivering, but not too intense.

More to say about my weekend retreat, but wanted to post this success. Busy getting ready to visit the old homestead for the holiday.

### drezy — 2018-11-21T16:44:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-256187>

That snow angel has really nicely cold adapted heat producing buns. I think I even see an ember on a couple of those leaves!

> [Phosphene said:](/goto/post?id=256176)
>
> This past summer of relatively good sun exposure was so luscious and healing, but I can feel mood dropping fast now after the time and UV change, despite more CT.

If it's not built into your inner emotional dialog\* then maybe use the perspective of an inner finance expert and "account for and consolidate your gains" when you can. Think about it this way, like most of us, you spent decades out of the sun in humanity's blue-lit zoo and covering your surface from natural light. If I were your financial advisor I'd be telling you to "realize" the gain and minimize losses for the next (solar) cycle.

I can at least say for myself that, on the course I used to be on, the last 1.5 years would have been predominantly health and sanity "losses" for me and for my wife. Feeling better despite another year of aging feels like a big deal to me and it feels like winter is thee to remind me just how tied to the sun it all is. I'm calling it out here year end '18 winter will be my nakedest ever.

Who knows, you may have inspired [@JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) to post his own naked snow angel picture. I do hear that snow angels get finer with age like a good wine.

\* - There's a range of temperaments from lotus eater hippy dip through Eeyore at least. I'm not sure how many meditations it takes to alter this all that much and the weird thing is that even temperament actually seems quantized to me. The planet would be a dystopia if everyone have the same lotus eating or Eeyore temperament. The reason I say this is that almost every naked and now cold morning in the sun I try to let at least one old thing that I know resonate with something new.

This bit resonated with your post this AM:
"Many of those who show the most gain in changes in gratitude are those who started in the negative side(temperament) of the spectrum." - from an interview with an author about his book on gratitude studies.

Aka people with a tendency to have or be driven negative (internal) emotionality (dialog) stand way more to gain (health and mental) by working in a little gratitude practice. Take that overly optimistic people. Let me see you turn that into lemonade! Nevermind you were bound to anyway.

Seeing that it's damn near Thanksgiving who knew that in 2018 I'd be extremely grateful for the nakedest neurosurgeon and his students. Past me sure couldn't have predicted that.

Have a great Thanksgiving [@Phosphene](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19648/).

### Phosphene — 2018-11-22T16:01:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-256279>

Happy Thanksgiving all!

I am so grateful for sunrises! And the will to get up for them. How did I miss this all my life???

### Phosphene — 2018-11-26T16:05:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-256615>

Much to unpack in [@drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/) ‘s insightful post above. Grateful for this community.

My temperament can range from hippy dip to Eyeore and back again over the course of a few hours. Yoga and meditation help, but I’ve stupidly slacked off of them lately. Family and friends have more or less adapted to the waves, the ones I still interact with at least.

I have been gravitating more to the critical, pessimistic, fatalistic end of the spectrum, and was called out on it yesterday. My dopamine has taken a deep dive with the lack of sun, and I’ve been having an internal (and external) bitch fest over why I’m still here in Pennsylvania fall/winter gloom when I KNOW I could be feeling so much better further south. Planning the trip to Mexico helps, but it’s so infuriating that I don’t feel able to stay there longer, even though I could liberate a small fraction of “retirement” savings to do it. Living at the ashram showed me how little I really need to happily thrive, how much I value being around likeminded folks, and how much my mood stabilizes around them. My husband is likeminded to some degree, but we are so far apart still on so many issues. More counseling? Financial advisor? God I just don’t know.

All I know is that I will NOT spend another winter cooped up her sulking. I was hoping that perhaps adapting to CT could make it bearable, but my adaptation isn’t happening fast enough, and the currently short term benefits from my still sporadic CT practices don’t seem to be enough to offset the deep gloom. A bit of panic is setting in that I’ll be turning 50 in a few months somehow still entrenched in these same fucking mental ruts. Samskaras.

[https://www.yogapedia.com/definition/5748/samskara](https://www.yogapedia.com/definition/5748/samskara)

Here is the forecast for the next week. Not horrendous temps, but no sun at all. Ugh. Eyeore is dominating at the moment...

Ok, maybe I’ll just go back outside for some CT.

### Phosphene — 2018-11-27T16:07:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-256700>

Almost forgot my goal of checking out tanning salons for the first time ever. Not ideal, but gotta do something. I will definitely look into the lizard lamps too. Used to have one for the turtles but it burned out. We put them out in real sun for the summer and discovered that they just happily hibernate in their tank for the winter without the extra light.

Sleep has been sketchy which surely isn’t helping. Waking up multiple times either hot or cold, and rising well before sunrise. Need to get back to meditating then again. Was very bright with this full moon cycle even with cloud cover. I wonder if the skylights in the bedroom aren’t doing me any favors? Can’t open them, unfortunately.

More CT planned for today.

23&me is having a sale that ends at noon Pacific time today, so I have a few more hours to convince myself that giving all my data to the matrix won’t be the end of the world.

Looks like are coming out with a new report covering how medications are metabolized. I intend to avoid meds as much as possible, but this might be helpful for my father, who is on an atrocious mix of heart and psych meds and has struggled with the cocktail all his life.

[https://blog.23andme.com/news/23and...-direct-to-consumer-pharmacogenetics-reports/](https://blog.23andme.com/news/23andme-granted-the-first-and-only-fda-authorization-for-direct-to-consumer-pharmacogenetics-reports/)

Could only get halfway through that one. Where do you find this stuff???

Yeah totally jealous of your weather. Rub it in.

I could be here, but I’m not.

I have open invitations to go back and stay with either of the two friends with MS I was assisting. Challenging but rewarding job, and the French bulldog was fun. I still believe the Kruse protocols would help them, at least to prevent further deterioration. So many other considerations and responsibilities though...

At least I can tell her to ditch the pink shirt.

I’m committed to staying here and tying up my loose ends, one way or another.

No more running.

Except for my Mexican reprieve that is!

Sending encouragement your way Phosphene!

### Phosphene — 2018-11-29T04:28:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-256892>

Had a little chat with my chica [@Inna](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20770/) and this came to mind. G’night friends—sweet dreams.

I want the sun to shine on me
I want the truth to set me free
I wish the followers would lead
With a voice so strong it could knock me to my knees

Hold on world 'cause you don't know what's coming
Hold on world 'cause I'm not jumping off
Hold onto this boy a little longer
Take another trip around the sun

If I jumped into the ocean to believe
If I climbed a mountain would I have to reach?
Do I even dare to speak? To dream? Believe?
Give me a voice so strong
I can question what I have seen

Hold on world 'cause you don't know what's coming
Hold on world 'cause I'm not jumping off
Hold onto this boy a little longer
Take another trip around the sun

Around the sun
Around the sun
Around the sun
Let my dreams set me free
Believe, believe
Now now now now now now

### Phosphene — 2018-12-04T15:06:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-257233>

Well it’s official—got plane tix and my mom is finalizing the timeshare reservation. Way too big and fancy a joint for me but it’s a gift so can’t complain. Something tells me I will like the simple and hugely optimal week after at Isla much more... I’ve only spoken marginally about possibly staying on, but at least it won’t be a total surprise. Hopefully I can figure a way to stay even longer, but two weeks is a good start. And I’m grateful for it.

Big time battery drain here with this godforsaken unending cold and cloudy weather. There was a peek of sun right around sunrise today that gifted me this view for all of 5 minutes. I burst into tears and gave thanks to Nature for her healing beauty.

### Phosphene — 2018-12-31T18:00:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-259269>

Just a quick update. I’m still here folks, but needed some time away from the forum. Found myself getting quite obsessive with it and spending far too much time with phone in hand since I still (STILL!!!) haven’t gotten an Ethernet connected computer.  (Had an ancient Chromebook that is just about dead.) Getting some financial things in order to change that when I return from Mexico. Finding it hard to prioritize all the expenses.

Speaking of Mexico, hallelujah it’s coming up! Starting to plan the cenote excursions and think I may contact Senor Cruz...

Much to say, but not ready to get back into the journal yet. Found this gem at a used book store for $5 and it’s been keeping me busy, between hanging out with my awesome (though tech-addicted) sons.

I also picked up One Hundred Years of Solitude, which somehow I’ve not read yet.

Looking for some suggestions for inspirational/motivational beach reading for my sons (ages 18 and 21). The older one just graduated college, and younger one just started.

I am printing and bringing this as well (for me at least). Quite a back story from the ashram which I will someday tell, but want to share the text for now, in case you’re looking for something else to study...

Opening passage...

“Give up all questions except one: ‘Who am I?’ After all, the only fact you are sure of is that you are. The ‘I am’ is certain. The ‘I am this’ is not. Struggle to find out what you are in reality.

Discover all that you are not -- body, feelings, thoughts, time, space, this or that -- nothing, concrete or abstract, which you perceive can be you. The very act of perceiving shows that you are not what you perceive.”

[https://advaita.com.br/wp-content/uploads/2010/08/1-I-Am-That-Nisargadatta-Maharaj-Resumo.pdf](https://advaita.com.br/wp-content/uploads/2010/08/1-I-Am-That-Nisargadatta-Maharaj-Resumo.pdf)

Happy New Year!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-259310>

Happy New Year! I love that ^^^^^^^ the "I am" is certain.......nothing else is

Torrential rain much of the day yesterday but still got a good amount of time on the ocean. Slipped and fell on polished concrete and hurt my elbow, but seems fine enough now.

Had resort credits to use for excursions so did an aquatic adventure—snorkeling and zip lining in a cenote, and swimming through a cavern, then more snorkeling at a third location—Akumal—a cenote/ocean inlet. Nobody took pictures. Memories only!

Resort is stupidly big—will not be back here. Notice eczema improving, so can’t be all bad.

### Phosphene — 2019-01-18T03:17:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-260659>

All settled in at Isla Mujeres and exhausted after day 1 of OPTIMAL vacation!

Fuller scoop tomorrow.

### Phosphene — 2019-01-20T19:14:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-260870>

Wow, where do I start? I’ll just give a few notes now and expand later. Don’t want to waste any of this precious time on my phone.

First, isla is gorgeous and Lloyd is a gracious, knowledgeable and accommodating host. Not to mention god damn hilarious at times. The apartment is simple but comfortable, with optimal night lighting. I’m getting an education in simple lighting hacks.

We are out the door by 7am to catch a 5 minute taxi to the sunrise beach. We bring breakfast, lunch (awesome ceviche) and water and stay the whole day.

There’s actually quite a mini quantum crew here. Mike Russo and his oldest son happen to be here in an Airbnb, and we’ve been meeting everyday for sunrise and local exploration. It’s even rumored that Matt Maruca may make an appearance today/tomorrow. Looking forward to meeting him.

All in all, it’s exceeding my expectations. Much more to say.

### Phosphene — 2019-01-20T21:39:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-260881>

I should add that the MIA resort beach/lagoon is one of several, and we rotate based on conditions. We go nowhere near the actual resort. I prefer the one we are at now—very low pop density and more rugged, but less swimable.

### Phosphene — 2019-01-21T16:38:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-260923>

[@Matty\_M](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/11546/) did arrive and I’ve had a blast picking his brain a bit and getting some insights for engaging my sons. It’s so impressive what he’s accomplishing at the age of 19.

The whole crew met again for sunrise today.

Have fun with whatever’s next in your journey, sweet baby girl Boo.

### Phosphene — 2019-02-23T03:58:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-263846>

Wow, can hardly believe that nearly a month has passed since my last update. Mid-January to mid-Feb is my witching month—where I’m normally dead to the world. Strangely enough, though I may have seemed rather dead to the forum, I’ve been experiencing my life for the first time in...I don’t even know how long.

Details to come, but I just want to express my extreme gratitude for Dr. Kruse and the magical people on this forum. I feel a ‘Success Story’ post coming on, because there is simply no other explanation for how I feel...

I turn 50 tomorrow, and can’t wait for the gorgeous second half of my life.

Here’s to loving family and friends sending actual pictures and cards via snail mail ON TIME. How do they do that??? Good old Aunt Sandra—I need to visit her.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-263847>

Now that we know better..........

xo

### Phosphene — 2019-02-25T02:36:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-263911>

Birthday oysters!

These were at a riverfront restaurant you can kayak right up to. I see that happening often this summer. Really wanted to be outside but it was cold miserable rain.

New Orleans style, lightly broiled with a spicy remoulade sauce. 6 for $10—not bad.

And even more! A local grocery store had bags of 20 for $12—need to see if they have any left.

Check out the tiny reddish baby crab in this one—still alive. There were four little crabs in all, which was a bit freaky at first. But since Freak is my middle name they went down the hatch as well.

Then off to see a symphony orchestra at one of the local universities. Small school, small venue, even smaller crowd, and back home by 9:30. Perfect!

### Phosphene — 2019-03-03T15:47:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-264356>

I’ve been hibernating, and coming down off my post-Mexico high quite a bit. But it doesn’t worry me much because I know I can get it back. I know I can ramp the dopamine right back up in a few short weeks when the weather breaks. There is already a bit of UV, but the weather has been shit and upper 30’s F is my current limit for enduring sustained full winter sun skin in the game.

Right now I’ve embraced cold, dark and boring, and though I’m still not a fan of cold I realize the gift of just...being here in the winter with my loving and forgiving family. They still think I’ve lost it, and I do need to distance myself from their less-than-optimal habits much of the time and just do my own thing. Hopefully they are observing and learning. My eldest starts his first “real job” (sigh) next week serendipitously near my parents, and will be staying with them until he gets his bearings. They are all looking forward to the arrangement.

My mind is running, flipping, churning through ideas for getting me south next winter, mid-January through mid-March to start, then true snowbird eventually. I need to make it happen. I WILL make it happen.

I gaze at this picture often to remind me of the fantastic time in Mexico, and motivate to get back near a southern ocean, where the magic really is.

One thing I know for sure—I thrive in the company of other mitochondriacs!!!

### Phosphene — 2019-03-05T20:56:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-264530>

I still have much to say about Mexico, but want to get a few rough notes out here that I will refine for another post in the “visiting Mexico” threads.

I’ve always been frugal with money (though in retrospect I see where I could have done even better...) So I challenged myself to make my trip to Isla Mujeres as inexpensive as possible. Here is the rough breakdown for my 9 days:

Round trip airfare from Philadelphia to Cancun: $276 (plus $24 optional trip/medical insurance) = $300

Shuttle from airport to ferry station (after negotiation; cheaper bus also available): $20

Ferry to Isla (round trip): $16

Food: $8-$15 per day (very possible if you mainly prepare your own and make breakfast/lunch the main meals). I’m using an average of $12/day: $108

Accommodation (8 nights, private room with shared bath and kitchen): $200

Taxis to and from beach (or a 2 mile walk): maybe $20 for the week since the rides were usually split 3 ways and Inna and I sometimes walked. Lloyd wisely maximizes his time at the beach IN THE WATER and always took taxis.

Incidentals (tips, bottled water, and small souvenirs/gifts for me and family): $40

My unlimited T-Mobile cell plan included calls, texts and data in Mexico so there was no additional expense here, and was fairly reliable.

So about $700 (plus getting to/from your local airport) for 9 days in high season mitochondrial paradise. (Okay, 8 days when you knock off travel time on each end.)

Obviously it would be more if I chose to splurge on fancy meals out, booze, or excursions. (I only did that once, and the tab was generously covered by another visiting mitochondriac. Well, ok—four of us split a celebratory bottle of Malbec on the last night as well. Thanks [@Inna](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20770/) !)

Also remember that the average price per day drops with a longer stay since the flight is the biggest chunk.

Overall, BEST TRIP OF MY LIFE!!!! Less glamorous but much more healing than any “vacation” experience. More to come on this.

Thanks again [@shah78](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1152/) !!

### LieselK — 2019-03-05T21:05:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-264531>

That sounds like perfection. I really don't like glamorous vacations. Simple and healing seems like more on point for vacationing! As a thrift-lover myself, I'd say you did well!

### Phosphene — 2019-03-05T23:41:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-264540>

There were a few downsides which I’ll talk about too, but I knew about most of them headed into this adventure and just chose not to focus on them. I had a few minor freakout moments (which are to be expected with foreign travel, especially solo) but overall people were friendly and helpful and things went pretty much as expected.

I guess one of the biggest takeaways was my attitude adjustment. Or at least steps in that direction. I am extremely self-critical, and tend to unconsciously reflect that criticism and judgment onto other people and situations. (Something that made me a very effective software technical writer/editor/QA tester—noticing errors, inconsistencies, redundancies, inefficiencies...)

I made a conscious effort in Mexico to just accept things as they were, and not overreact if something didn’t jibe with my preconceived notion of how I thought it should be. I found this so much easier after a few days in the presence of hardcore mitochondriacs (Lloyd and Inna).

I’m not sure my family agrees that I’ve made progress in this area, but I know I have and that’s all that really matters for now.

### Phosphene — 2019-03-06T20:36:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-264629>

I’m guessing because it’s a small island and the beach areas are either private or public and pretty tightly monitored for activity. It felt very safe for this reason, but it was also clear that you couldn’t just plop a tent down somewhere. There were some cool properties for sale though, and I know at least one member has sights on possibly creating a retreat center of some kind. Part of my vision as well.

### Phosphene — 2019-03-06T22:48:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-264650>

I should be working on the taxes, but in other news...

I relented and gave in to the Ancestry DNA kit I received from oldest son for Xmas. Results are in, and not the least bit surprising with heritage (except for the smidge of Swedish and Norwegian, which is awesome). But after running raw data through Genetic Genie I’m a bit concerned with all the red and yellow in my snips/snaps report. I held off doing this mainly for the privacy issue, but also for the major rabbit hole. I need to get some shit done, and this isn’t helping.

### Phosphene — 2019-03-15T22:45:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-265595>

Got 4 hours of UV6 and 70 degrees today! Springs a comin’.

On the down side, was doing some yard work and coming to the sad conclusion that most of my huge evergreens are dying.

Now most of them are 20-40 years old so I would expect some natural attrition, but this is just spooky. Even younger ones seem affected. Lower branches bare, needles falling in huge numbers, overall transparency... I’ll get some pictures soon—I was too disturbed to document today.

Still plenty of birds though. If they leave I will too.

### LieselK — 2019-03-15T22:55:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-265596>

>

Interesting. I'll have to ask my neighbors if they feel the needle fall is increasing here. I was shocked at how much they drop when we moved to NC but I have nothing to compare to other than my parents' OLD arborvitae which dropped a ton too. We have tons of birds too thankfully and not so thankfully (when the hawks eat my chickens.)

### Phosphene — 2019-03-22T23:25:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-266242>

So it occurred to me that my trees might simply be starving. The soil here is hardpacked clay and I’ve never fertilized the trees beyond letting the leaf/needle litter stay in place. Unlike the acres of hardwood forest that was here “naturally” before, all the evergreens were transplanted as ornamentals. I’ll bet they just need some nutrients, and I hope it’s not too late. Must research what to use—maybe compost tea and fish emulsion.

### Phosphene — 2019-03-26T19:20:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-266506>

Brrrrr...I’ve got the girls out in UV6 full sun, but it’s only 42F here with 10 mph north wind making it feel 36. Actually my chair is blocking some of it so my torso is rather toasty and just the extremities are chilly. It’s not at all comfortable but I’m still out here. Clouding up again the next few days. Grrrr.

I’ve got yet another idea  for my main solar spot. My raised beds disintegrated several years ago (along with the rest of my life) and instead of going through all that hassle to build them again I might try this. If I create a U-shape bed with the opening facing southwest I can, I think, get private sun a good chunk of the day.

The TEDx is pretty cool and worthwhile.

[https://strawbalegardens.com/](https://strawbalegardens.com/)

That sounds like a great idea! Much cheaper than building up beds again or putting up privacy fence!

### Phosphene — 2019-03-27T19:27:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-266588>

LOL! No you don’t want disintegrating beds! So many levels of meaning.

And yes my cool surfaces are indeed shivering. Do goose bumps increase the surface area of exposed skin? Am I grasping something to do with topology, or way off?

I listened to a bit of Scholastic Bowl on WVIA the other day (remember that??) and was thoroughly annoyed that I didn’t get several physics answers that were right there....almost.

I’m back outside in 42F sun working on my redox again—pumping back up to tackle the introspective issues that I tend to avoid. I’m so NOT the stereotypical female who wants to TALK about everything. Just the opposite. It’s an area in which I need balance, certainly.

I’m in my sun spot and yes I will keep in mind the sun’s angle, and the shadows cast for my new foxhole garden. I dig that term and will likely keep it. Hay bales are at a premium this year apparently due to the wet winter so I need to scout around for some. Even a little spoiled will work since that’s really the point.

Not staying out here too long today uncovered—actually got some redness on the tender bits yesterday and I’m think the sudden burst of UVB is not so great without proper prep of earlier infrared and UVA. it was freakin 20F at sunrise so only a little of that, and not much on the bits yet.

There’s a tense prickliness here in the forum after the brief yet charged Q&A. This too shall pass.

### drezy — 2019-04-07T00:38:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-267494>

Nice basil selection. I'm growing sweet, Thai, and cinnamon basil this year. I'm also always glad to have my indestructible sage bushes and my tiny but productive oregano plants. There's something extra nice about not paying for spices. It probably has soothing to do with the fact that fresh basil costs at least $2 for even a small pack and it's nowhere near as fresh as when you picked it 2 minutes ago.

I'm sure it needn't be said but barefoot gardening and no gloves please.

### Phosphene — 2019-04-16T04:49:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-268150>

Yes, got them mostly done over the past few weeks but there was one niggling thing bothering me and I finally figured it out yesterday—to the tune of another wee trip to Mexico! One advantage of two kids in college was double the education credit as well. Too bad there’s still some debt to pay down from the past few years of chaos. Most of it will need to go to that, but I’m still getting my horse trough tub and Ethernet cable for Mother’s Day.

The cold, cloudy spring is dragging here—minor tornado even touched down last night just 15 minutes away! I’m dragging a little too and can’t quite figure it out. Actually I’m pretty sure I do know—I admit missing the chaos a bit. Life here is blessed, but....feeling so ordinary and uninspired at the moment. This empty nest shit is no joke. Still searching for my starfish spark and long term sunshine escape route. I also haven’t integrated one bit back into the community, though I did make 30 quarts of soup with my mother in law last week for her church’s charity soup kitchen. Not epi-paleo in the least unfortunately. Their mantra is cheap and filling, which means lots of noodles and beans. And rather central PA bland of course.  Sigh.

All this stuff going on with Doc is triggering me too. I simply MUST make sure I’m back here for the right reasons. I do know one of those reasons is to spend some quality time with youngest son home from college for the summer. Had a bit of emotional release with him recently over spring break—a LONG hug that turned to tears for both of us, and then an even longer embrace, making up for lost time...

Had a bit of emotional release with him recently over spring break—a LONG hug that turned to tears for both of us, and then an even longer embrace, making up for lost time...

Spend as much time in the sun as you can! Make healthy food for YOURSELF. You got this.

### Phosphene — 2019-04-18T17:42:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-268355>

Getting some oysters today. I did slack off a bit....

### Phosphene — 2019-04-19T22:26:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-268461>

Success!

Tag on the bag said 15 count Blue Points for $13, not bad but even better when it turns out to be 21 in there! Surprised that the local-yocal grocery store carries them. The name of the store is Weis (usually pronounced either rhyming with ‘rice’, or more colloquially, ‘rises’). However, New York transplant friends of mine once had me rolling with their pronunciation, We-Is. Once heard it cannot be unheard!

Experimented with opening them on the grill, which worked, but lost some precious deuterium-depleted juice and found the attachment muscle much harder to slice through after even the lightest of grilling. Still yum though.

Easter oysters—a new tradition?

### Phosphene — 2019-04-23T18:13:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-268700>

Been busy spring cleaning, in many ways. The township has a free junk drop off event next weekend and we are preparing massive amounts of stuff to GO, CHUCK, LEAVE, BE GONE! Some will be saved for a yard sale so that’s a pain, but I can’t bear to toss usable stuff. Although Goodwill is another option too.

Paused to enjoy the glorious sunrise from a new location today—my grounded front porch. Good here for another few days until the cherry leafs out.

Much contemplation with all of this tossing. 20+ years of crap, most of it for the kids. So much sports equipment!!! At least it kept them outside...

### drezy — 2019-04-23T18:26:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-268702>

Wow! Pretty tree!

You had two good boys.

I'm starting to think that past middle age and with at least one good offspring or significant influence on the future life is just one long victory lap. I think you and I should be walking around like this all day until the end of our days:

[@JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) has good grandkids so he should be doing this and be jumping too.

### Phosphene — 2019-04-26T22:43:04+0300

Yes I do have two good boys, who are now awesome young men. The younger one is a big softie for animals—came home with a baby squirrel orphaned on campus. I asked him if he would still hunt squirrels if our lives depended on it, and the answer was a quick, “Hell yeah!”

Yep, good boys. Only the younger one can rock a man-bun and this thrift store shirt.

### Phosphene — 2019-04-30T04:01:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-269295>

So this past weekend was Russian Orthidox Easter, and the feast table was laden with meats of all sorts. Ham, veal, baked pork belly, and no less than four types of kielbasa—from two states. The Pennsylvania ones edged out the New Jersey contingent, but just barely. Ignore Aunt Sandra’s rainbow jello desert in the upper left corner.

My dad as master of ceremony, for yet another year. He turned 83 on Monday.

### Sean Waters — 2019-05-03T13:52:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-269573>

> [Phosphene said:](/goto/post?id=266746)
>
> Got some tense shit going on here—husband and I are as distant as ever and the empty nest thing is just highlighting it. He’s a good (great) man...the relationship is probably worth fighting for but damn it’s hard when they don’t believe any of it.
>
> One son will be home visiting this weekend and it won’t be long till he’s back for the summer. He’s the most open minded of the bunch so maybe it’s not too late to get some of this through to him.
>
> Doc’s confession really got to me. Choosing to double down on family at the expense (or at least deferment) of dreams.
>
> Big thoughts...

Nothing a little bit of tender slow roasted Pork Belly can't handle..............

LOL I wish. And I hear you. This Spring has certainly brough about a lot emotion and relationship tension. Everyone seems to be going through this! Even Drezy is opening up.

FWIW and if it makes you feel any better I'm currently not speaking to my Mother - sounds bad to everyone else - but it is actually working out better so we don't say anything hurtful to each other. And for the same reasons everyone else is experiencing, she said "Why don't you and Jack Kruse fuck off out of my house?"

LOL.

At the time I was annoyed, but looking back it's a pretty hilarious statement. She's absolutely had it with this Light shit.

I chose to cut her off. And when I move out in the next few weeks she might still be cut off. I still think Jack had it right with that last year before all of this last few webinars.

I've tried fighting for people, and when they aren't gonna get it, and I don't have the energy, you gotta fly away.

Maybe Jack has got the energy to do it. Maybe he needs those around him to thrive and so he has to fight.

It's tough. So many of us are facing it though.... don't hesitate to reach out if you need to vent.

### caroline — 2019-05-14T03:30:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-270386>

### Phosphene — 2019-05-15T18:07:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-270538>

[@Saichi](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/23447/) , Thanks! This is exactly the kind of stuff my young dudes are into. I’ll see if they dig this. I do understand that some of it has value, and they’ve assured me that the purely misogynistic bitch/ho nonsense isn’t their thing. Thank the gods.

The older one is three months into his culturally mandated matrix job and already annoyed with the limitations of the starting 3 weeks of ‘paid time off’ (sick/vaca) which can’t be used till earned week by week, and apparently can’t be carried over to the next year either. It’s the kind of company that seems to churn employees in this tight labor market, and he’s positioned to gather experience and certifications, pay off his reasonable loans, and then work anywhere. He sleeps in my parents’ rural finished basement, surrounded by the Earth and very little nnEMF which I think bodes well for his currently excellent health. Coming home to a Baba-cooked, carb-heavy meal every day doesn’t seem to hurt yet either. He’s found a new climbing community there, and has taken up golf with fancy clubs gifted from his boss, so all systems go.

The younger one has a bit more entrepreneurial spirit and less tolerance for the mundane. He’s currently prepping for a three credit school sponsored camping trip to California—Joshua Tree and Death Valley among other places. Hope he gets Cali out of his system.

I’m doing ok but feeling a bit restless, and emerging from a bit of an anxious funk. The persistent cold rain is finally starting to clear—was really getting to me. The sun truly is my salvation. This is the longest I’ve stayed in one place for quite a few years (aside from my two week Mexico escape). Been mulling over some money earning ideas, but have made it clear that my priorities are to help support the family while I can, but then get the hell out of Dodge when I need to. I think it’s becoming clearer to everyone that this is what I NEED to do, not just what I WANT to do.

Current favorite sunny spot, partially shielded by variegated weigelia.

Life is good.

### Phosphene — 2019-05-15T18:45:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-270549>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=270300)
>
> tell me about this book ......

Will do! It’s huge (462 pages) but lots of white space and illustrations so shouldn’t take all that long. I was lured into watching her 4-episode Netflix series, which was totally worth the nnEMF. She is just delightful—knowledgeable, experienced, passionate and unpretentious. Many of the recipes are carb-focused, but I’m pretty good at subbing or just eliminating the carbs. The lessons in technique are spot on, and quite a few things I’d never considered, like poaching in oil.

Made her Moroccan Kufte yesterday—basically spicy, egg-shaped meatballs, pan fried since it was pouring yet again. Hubs declared he’d love to find those Measter eggs hidden anywhere around the house.

The sauce I came up with needs some work. I have a habit of getting a bit over-confident at times and just combining flavors and ingredients helter-skelter hoping for the best. Turns out you can’t just mix some Greek yogurt into an olive oil/herb base. Well you can, but you’ll end up with a runny (but still tasty) mess. I sort of knew this but couldn’t resist anyway.

I’m hoping following some of her classic techniques will teach me a little restraint!

### Phosphene — 2019-05-15T18:56:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-270554>

> [KrusinWitchie said:](/goto/post?id=270355)
>
> Listening to an interview with this author about the book had me sobbing! Maybe it was the nnEMF from the car radio that turned me into emotional mush... but even so there was something about her voice and experience that touched my weepy heart! Enjoy!

Don’t know much of the back story yet but she’s Iranian and that in itself makes her intriguing. Started out bussing at iconic Chez Panisse 20 years ago and apprenticed (and traveled) her way up—no formal culinary school. She weaves personal stories into the prose and recipes, which always makes both better, IMO.

### caroline — 2019-05-16T03:40:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-270585>

I NEED this book!!! we have a little turkish cafe here ....and I do mean little! little sidewalk tables only. She has 4 cookbooks now and they are fascinating when you weave her childhood memories and history thru her cookbooks.

a memoir??????? you are making me feel very old. I will have to give this all some thought .....I never think of myself as interesting - not at all. Pretty boring actually ...or maybe just too intense. That is a new word I am using about myself.

### Phosphene — 2019-06-09T23:55:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-272361>

### Phosphene — 2019-06-09T23:57:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-272362>

Artist: Mari Boine

Album: Idjagiedas (In the Hand of the Night)

Genre: Folk, World music, Sami joik, Jazz, Folk rock, Neofolk

Origin: Norwegian Sami

Year: 2006

The Sami yoiks are something very deeply connective. To Nature, to Earth, to People. Mari Boine makes them easier to understand for most people, by adding jazz and rock elements, but manages not to lose too much of that purity.

### Phosphene — 2019-06-20T21:12:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-273048>

Alright some good news to report! Through a last-minute cancellation of a beach house at the Jersey shore, my family plus my two sister-in-laws’ families will be sharing the house next week for next to nothing. Right on the bay (with a floating dock for swimming) and two blocks to the beach.

I’ve been in a bit of a slump, though nothing worth blathering about here. It’s so very obvious how the lack of sun affects me, and moreso the lack of direct interaction with likeminded community.

I intend to fully rev up my redox and dopamine next week—weather looks almost perfect so if anyone wants to join me for sunrise in Avalon just say the word. Don’t think there will be room for overnight but I could host you all day next Wed/Thurs/Fri.

Thankfully found a pet/house sitter—hope they enjoy the space—it’s certainly quiet and peaceful. Not far from farms like this. Taking some of the superb golden butter with me—$3/pound.

### Phosphene — 2019-09-06T19:40:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-277321>

Wow where did the summer go? Well it’s still technically summer for a few more weeks, but there’s just something both joyful and wistful about the start of September and kids back to school. I’ve always had awesome energy/mood this time of year and now is no different. Getting labs done in the few weeks (for the first time in years) so I’m curious about my D, hormones and other stuff.

Mexico trip is all planned for January—just waiting till the direct flight prices drop a bit to finalize it. A whole 3.5 weeks this time—woohoo! And I’m starting a part-time job next week to pay for it all, and they are fine with me taking time off. So blessed.

Now for something in the file of #youcantmakethisshitup. Look what arrived in my mailbox yesterday! Never received anything like this in all my years in this relatively fundamentalist area. Funny thing is I’m not quite so skeptical anymore.

### Christine_L — 2019-09-10T21:22:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-277586>

I have 2 pairs of Ra Optics, 1 BluBlox, 2 Uvex, 1 Dewalts and 3 PrimalHackers (2 night time and 1 day time). I really like the PrimalHacker ones even though they aren't super dark red like my Ra's. Plus if they break or scratch I dont feel as bad as the scratch on my day time Ra's because the price is significantly less. I don't use an Rx but I think the new price is a little steep. Knowing my BF his would get scratched immediately, which is how I found the other sites. Even if you are super careful, having a bunch of back ups and variety is really nice.

### Phosphene — 2019-09-10T22:18:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-277603>

I will probably get the daytime ROs as my first pair for the increased indoor exposure of winter. Plus yes my new job is indoors but I’m still looking forward to just getting the hell away from the house for a bit. My husband works from home and there definitely is such a thing as too much togetherness!

I thought hard about getting back into technical writing/editing on a contract basis but I’m still hesitant about a job where I have to stare at a screen all day. That’s what got me into this mess and even with mitigation measures I’m still leery.

Plus my new gig is something completely different, and has been quietly calling for quite some time. I’ll be helping a friend open and manage a new restaurant 15 minutes away in one of the small college towns. Yes, increased nnemf for sure, but for better or worse I don’t appear to get obvious symptoms from even extreme exposure (like a weekend in Philly) so I’ll give it a whirl. No early mornings or late nights so my sun and sleep schedule will remain intact, and my friend knows this is a leap and has already assured no hard feelings if it doesn’t work.

I’m actually rather excited about the whole thing! Oh, and I’ll get to create an employee handbook, job descriptions, and some standard operating procedures, which can be done from wherever. This is the third restaurant and the owner/friend is a bit scattered so I’m sure there will be some drama and chaos, but I’m actually embracing that concept as well. It’s been a long time since my body, mind and spirit have all been on board for creating order out of chaos. My previous trainings should serve me well!

Soft opening isn’t till early October so for now I still get to enjoy this, although it’s probably the culprit to my seasonal ragweed allergy that appears every year like clockwork when it blooms. Small price to pay.

### Phosphene — 2019-09-12T20:57:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-277730>

Holy moly! I actually have a few viable peaches on my one remaining tree—first time in the 15+ years since we planted them. I had written them off since the pollinator tree (I was told to plant two different varieties) died last year. There have been peaches in the past but they always succumbed to fungus and I would never spray.

This is the best of the bunch—flawed but scrumptious!

Wish we hadn’t placed the “dwarf” trees so close to the hemlocks behind. Seemed like plenty of room at the time.

And damn—I need to cut my fingernails again. They are hard as rocks and grow so fast.

Sorry [@drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/) perhaps your peaches teleported up here.

Yummm enjoy. That's about as good as a match as you're going to get.

### LieselK — 2019-09-13T02:45:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-277756>

> [Phosphene said:](/goto/post?id=277523)
>
> I’m committed to getting at least one pair of RO’s for now to support Matt, but it’s interesting that discount online sites are offering seemingly decent therapeutic tints now. I wonder if RO will ever offer significant bulk family discounts? They seem to be marketing to the young biohacker crowd with cash to burn, and that’s fine if the niche works.

I have both daytime and nighttime RaOptics that are prescription. But when I got them there was an option to send in cheaper frames/lenses and have them tinted so mine were significantly less, but still pricey. They've lasted at least 1.5 years with no obvious scratches. I keep them in hard cases and use clear regular prescription glasses for my outside stuff during the day when I need to see- those are VERY scratched because I'm taking them off so much! lol But the others are holding up, even with super cheap crappy frames!

BUT... my goal this fall is to tint my own because I need prescription ones for my daughter and my husband and the other boys need non-prescription. You can get glasses SO cheap online. I read Jason Lauritzen's instructions and plan to give it a go! After all... that's how Matt started. My kitchen will work fine for this.

### Phosphene — 2019-09-13T21:03:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-277792>

Zoinks!!!!

The Case of the Missing Clams...

So I went to get dinner started yesterday and realized that the canned clams that I swore were in the pantry were in fact nowhere to be found. It’s possible that DS2 has them since we both raided the pantry for his return to an off-campus apartment sans cafeteria, and he thankfully loves seafood. Or my brain-only pantry inventory system is not up to snuff. More likely the latter.

In any case I needed to come up with a quick alternative, so grilled brats, peppers and onions plus fresh tomatoes to the rescue.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Or so I thought. I’ve been all high and mighty over the fact that I (and my family) seemed to have no issues with nightshades. Is it possible (please no) that my seasonal allergy that hits every August is from the glut of raw or lightly cooked nightshades and not the ragweed? I was awake half the night sneezing and even resorted to the generic Zyrtec that I take for a few weeks when it’s really bad. And that’s not even really helping much today. I’m a sneezy drippy irritable mess! Grrrrrrrr....

That’s like almost half the damn dwarves, right?

### Saichi — 2019-09-13T21:41:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-277797>

For non-toxic relief I take two of these which gives about a 2 hour relief:

### Phosphene — 2019-09-13T21:55:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-277798>

Fuck. Of course it’s possible—I just never made the connection before. I don’t have the symptoms any other time of year but maybe just the overload triggers it. I got those luscious cherry and grape tomatoes from a neighbor’s roadside stand and have been eating them like candy with every meal...

[@Saichi](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/23447/) — Thanks. I’ve tried freeze-dried nettles before (and other natural remedies like quercetin and turmeric) and nothing would touch it except the Zyrtec. It’s my only prescription (now OTC) drug and is used sparingly for as little time as possible—some years only a week. Maybe I’ll get that brand since I haven’t tried nettles in many years.

### nonchalant — 2019-09-14T01:00:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-277807>

I have difficulty buying Brats without MSG. Kroger used to carry some, but now their store brand also includes it.

### Phosphene — 2019-09-14T19:32:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-277842>

So far so good with the nettles! I ripped the container open in the parking lot and took 2, along with a fingerful of the local raw honey recommended by the health food store dude. Yeah, I was that desperate. I had raw PA honey at home, but this stuff was more local, and who could resist Japanese Knotweed honey? It’s dark and tastes medicinal like manuka honey. They only had this brand of nettles but something seems to be working so I’m happy.

Of course now I don’t know which remedy worked, or whether just quitting the nightshades did the trick. Had a steak and a glass (okay goblet) of Malbec for dinner, and two more doses of nettles. Slept well and feel decent today. Will keep up with the nettles (and not the honey) every two hours today and report back!

### Phosphene — 2019-09-17T14:03:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-278028>

Day 1 of Sunrise Accountability

Actual sunrise 6:50, pic taken at 6:56 when he clears the ridge line.

### Phosphene — 2019-09-17T14:59:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-278033>

Copying this here where it belongs, instead of hiding it in someone else’s journal...

“Yeah, one of the reasons I delayed starting my journal is that my thoughts, behaviors and patterns were SO unoptimal I couldn’t imagine admitting to them, even behind a cloak of semi-anonynimity. I still struggle with Facebook-izing, or over-reporting the encouraging successes and conveniently leaving out the less flattering stumbles. I avoid FB for the most part but see how addictive those ‘likes’ can be by their use here. I was astonished when I recently saw how many I actually have.

What??? Me???

It makes it appear that I’m catching up with some of the pillars of the community, when in reality the ‘Like’ function was only added to the forum a few years ago. So unfair to the old timers! Can you imagine the number of likes [@Inger](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1057/) and so many others would have if that function were here from inception??

Anyway...

Accountability is a huge ongoing hurdle for me.

I’m going to be honest here and admit that I probably failed Kruse 101 this spring and early summer by missing quite a few early sunrises, especially the cold rainy ones with obscured sun. I was a forever night owl, and while I’ve mostly changed that habit there’ssome stupid mental block about getting up before 6am if I’m not obligated to do anything (besides save my own damn life).

I’m setting the intention of posting a sunrise pic every day from now on. It’s the very least, and of course the very most I can do.

One of the reasons I’m looking forward to returning to work outside the home is an enforced structure, with tangible immediate consequences.

Yikes!”

You have an amazing viewpoint of the sunrise. Is that at home or did you walk to it? Our sun takes awhile to get to us, so we're getting indirect or filtered for at least 40 min past sunrise then I have to chase it around the yard to be in it full on. But it IS worth it- every little bit!

### Saichi — 2019-09-17T20:57:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-278089>

I was only doing sunrises regularly when I thought that was one of the two times in the day I could be in the ocean without getting burned. Now I know I can do raw SG except maybe an hour around solar noon I've pretty much given up waking early. I do know it makes me feel better to see sunrise but I prioritize high UV SG. And now that I have an emfkillswitch I can sleep for 8 hours regularly which was impossible before. Agree with Inger sleep's more important than sunrise.

### Phosphene — 2019-09-17T22:39:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-278100>

Yes that is right from my backyard, there every day for the taking. I wish there were a good spot indoors to get the morning light but all bedrooms are either dark, facing north, or have unopenable windows or skylights. It’s one reason I’m looking forward to the camper, plus the power disconnect out there. My sons took their tents with him and I haven’t yet gotten a tent of my own. (Sounds like a decent book title...)

My allergies are much better—I had no idea the nettles needed to be taken so often. (Thanks again [@Saichi](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/23447/) ) Of course I’ve avoided nightshades as well so reintroducing them will be a test. Amazing how often I reach for hot sauce. I’ve read that long slow cooking inactivates the toxin so hopefully I’ll be back to my beloved pepper, tomato and eggplant dishes soon.

Just saw a huge flock of confused geese overhead flying north. WTF? This must be just a layover.

### Phosphene — 2019-09-19T15:23:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-278248>

An hour later...

My kitty Kira is joyfully joining me every morning. She is an ordinary domestic shorthair but so very special—a combination of every cat I’ve ever had. I just gaze at the myriad colors in her coat as the sun sparkles it ablaze. She blocks my sun a bit but I don’t care. It’s not long till she springload launches herself in deadly pursuit of some hapless creature.

I am truly enjoying my “ordinary” life right now.

Had a business meeting yesterday and hammered out some additional details of my new gig. Looks like the soft opening is being pushed to when the university is on Thanksgiving break to not get totally crushed. The students have over a week off then, just a few weeks before the month+ long winter break. (What in the world are their parents paying all that $$$ for??? Status.)

So I officialy start next week, 20-25 hrs/week mostly from home until opening, doing various admin that the (creative/passionate) owner despises. He even said he never even considered a role like this until I proposed it a few months ago, sitting outside his lovely cafe in the sun.

So yeah, I’m actually creating my own job—how cool is that? Redox power!

### Phosphene — 2019-09-19T22:52:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-278306>

So I’ve been thinking a lot about dreams lately, both the awake and the asleep varieties. Asleep seems easier to tackle right now so here are some random rememberings.

I’ve had a few dreams including my husband recently, which is unusual. Nothing lascivious, but not unpleasant either. Most of my dreams are about seemingly perfect strangers with the odd family member or friend tossed in on occasion. These dreams were no different, but I caught myself thinking (in the dreams), “Well that’s weird, what’s HE doing here?”

One involved me being swept along underwater in a clear blue river (underwater is never a problem because I can always breathe and there’s no real panic). I was trying to stand up (others seemed to have no problem) but couldn’t get my footing. Suddenly I caught sight of my husband’s blue and orange striped swim trunks and reached out, baffled but relieved. Then the dream ended, or morphed, or whatever they do.

Another (just last night) involved an elaborate house I’d never been in, full of windows looking out over water. Fireworks started and my husband and I both knew that the view would be much better from another room, and raced off in separate directions to get to the room. (I was of course determined to beat him and took a downstairs route I thought was surely quicker.) Nope, he got there just steps ahead of me and we both stood in awe of the most spectacular and strange fireworks we’d ever seen—like Dr. Seuss fireworks. I swear I’ve never done acid in this lifetime but that must be what it’s like.

I guess it doesn’t take an interpreter to see that these are encouraging signs! Our 24th anniversary is next week and for the first time in many years I’m not dreading it. We are still very far apart in many areas but seem to have called a truce. It’s working for now.

One final dream I want to recall is very special, involving one of my favorite long deceased cats. I forget the setting but was startled and delighted when she began softly but clearly singing the following.

I have seen ships!
I have seen ships!
On and on the waves will take us!

I listened as long as possible and then shook myself awake so I could not only write it down (which I rarely do) but also record the little tune which I didn’t want to forget. I sing it to myself every now and then and it always makes me smile.

Okay enough. I do have some actual work to do.

### Phosphene — 2019-09-20T14:25:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-278345>

I’m forever grateful for being plucked out of the anonymous abyss. A crab with a helpful (but still pinching) claw!

I can also easily sleep that long. If I get to bed by 10 or so I can go through to 7 or even 8 if I don’t have an alarm set and the room is dark enough. Usually there is one bathroom visit but nursing on demand taught me to snooze whenever I could, and fall back asleep in seconds. Now that my circadian rhythm is better I often wake naturally a bit before sunrise, but not reliably yet. Much easier outdoors of course.

Those lusciously vivid dreams on the edge of waking used to lure me back to sleep to catch a few more. Still do sometimes, but determined to break that habit.

Last night was pretty boring—another grocery store (common for me). Wandering round and round searching for pomegranates this time!

### KrusinWitchie — 2019-09-20T14:55:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-278348>

> [Phosphene said:](/goto/post?id=278345)
>
> Those lusciously vivid dreams on the edge of waking used to lure me back to sleep to catch a few more. Still do sometimes, but determined to break that habit.
>
> Last night was pretty boring—another grocery store (common for me). Wandering round and round searching for pomegranates this time!

I didn't succumb to the temptation to go back for more dreams this morning. At 5:30 I woke up and remembered my dream of Wendy Byrde sobbing on Charles Wilke's (Ozark ) chest. Signaled an emotional release for me .

Jay used to work in the UWS in Manhattan. Big jewish population there. He would order 50 cases of pomegranates a day around this time of year for Rosh Hashanah and could barely keep up with the demand! According to Gil Marks in "The Encyclopedia of Jewish Food" the abundance of seeds, nestled into a white membrane and encased in a protective and leathery skin, is associated with the 613 commandments in the Torah. They serve as symbols of righteousness and fruitfulness, as expressed in the Rosh Hashanah expression, “May we be full of merits like the pomegranate (is full of seeds).”

The High Priestess tarot card has pomegranates above her head, being full of ideas and the potential present in your life.

### Phosphene — 2019-09-20T15:13:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-278350>

I did eventually find them!

### drezy — 2019-09-20T15:47:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-278352>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=278336)
>
> You sleep for 9-10 hours a night???

I do.

Not counting my trip around the world I only had trouble sleeping about 3 nights this whole year. Maybe that's my superpower. Sleepy Man!

Better yet my wife used to sleep poorly 5 nights a week. She now sleeps pretty darn good 5-6 nights a week.

From a husband's perspective it's like getting an unexpected marriage upgrade to first class seating on a long flight.

> [Phosphene said:](/goto/post?id=278345)
>
> Those lusciously vivid dreams on the edge of waking used to lure me back to sleep to catch a few more. Still do sometimes, but determined to break that habit.

That is a fun zone.

Salvador Dali apparently mined that zone with this brilliant trick:

Take this appropriately absurd image for example: Salvador Dali, the master of surrealism, is slouching in his chair. In his right hand he holds a key. Beneath his hand is an upside-down plate. Once he falls into a deep sleep, his hand releases the key which clangs onto the plate and the painter awakes with a start, refreshed and ready to get weird.

For Dali, the time between the release of the key and the clank of the plate (coupled with the drifting off beforehand) is more than enough to throw yourself back at the canvas. As he writes in the _[50 Secrets of Magic Craftsmanship](http://books.google.com/books?id=8xAAWySuWckC&pg=PA36&lpg=PA36&dq=salvador+dali+slumber+with+a+key&source=bl&ots=ZA99dL59SS&sig=FbjpX8QoQVobfd5dn22c5hRuyvo&hl=en&ei=jMDITe37Deby0gHP3uzmBw&sa=X&oi=book_result&ct=result&resnum=4&ved=0CCsQ6AEwAw#v=onepage&q&f=false)_:

The moment the key drops from your fingers, you may be sure that the noise of its fall on the upside-down plate will awaken you, and you may be equally sure that this fugitive moment when you had barely lost consciousness and during which you cannot be assured of having really slept is totally sufficient, inasmuch as not a second more is needed for your physical and psychic being to be revivified by just the necessary amount of repose.

From - [https://www.fastcompany.com/3023078/how-dali-einstein-and-aristotle-perfected-the-power-nap](https://www.fastcompany.com/3023078/how-dali-einstein-and-aristotle-perfected-the-power-nap)

### Phosphene — 2019-09-20T19:46:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-278372>

Hypnagogia. Nice. I love learning new 50 cent words.

My college dorm walls were plastered with Dali and Escher. Were everyone’s? I don’t recall. I do recall taking far too many naps and being far too familiar with sleep inertia. My roommate and I would fairly regularly take naps after dinner and wake up around 8pm, then stay up half the night attempting to study... groan...

I’ll bet I wouldn’t have dropped calculus and my bio major if I were Krusin back then. Oh well it’s all good—I got to read tons of books and go to France instead!

I’m trying to spread Kruse to my kids and they do understand the very basics, but for better or worse they are still in bulletproof mode—never sick, breezing through classes/work, lots of good friends. All I can really do is share selected info, be a good example, and remind them how far I’ve risen from where I fell.

DS2 is an especially good friend and has rescued quite a few in need. He’s in a good space and has his head on his shoulders for the most part. He’s not coming home this weekend due to a short road trip to see, uh, Rainbow Kitten Surprise with one of his circle.

### Phosphene — 2019-09-20T22:44:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-278415>

From this day forth every time I even dare think of complaining about my lot in life I’m going to picture a can of sardines. At least I’m not one of those folks who clean, sort and pack these all day. Surely there’s no automated way to do this? It’s almost art so maybe there’s the job satisfaction.

My metric used to be garbage collectors but at least they’re outside all day.

### Phosphene — 2019-09-20T23:00:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-278416>

That’s weird. Somehow when I was uploading the sardine pic it also included a thumbnail of the Integral Yoga guru Sri Swami Satchidananda.

Generally translated as:

Sat = truth
Chid = knowledge
Ananda = bliss

Must be a big lesson in the sardine can!

### Phosphene — 2019-09-21T14:43:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-278456>

Day 5

A bit of lovely fog and 49F. Decided to meditate for half hour or so before posting. So blessed. Headed to the humongous Bloomsburg Fair today—we shall see what epi-paleo food I can find... Actually there are quite a few options but it’s hard to resist the fresh peach cobbler. We’ve been very good and my husband made his employer’s health goals yesterday so today may be a bit indulgent.

### Phosphene — 2019-09-21T23:57:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-278516>

Only spent a few hours at the fair—more than enough really. I haven’t gone for many years and this is probably my fill for a few more. I hate being judgemental but damn there are SO many large people making themselves larger with crap food. The place was packed with mobility scooters, far more than I remember in the past. Now I’m all for mobility for the disabled for sure, but just saddened by all the disability.

I found a lamb gyro for lunch (avoided most of the pita) which was pretty good for central PA gyros... And did get one of the scrumptious peach dumplings (not cobbler) later on. A whole peach, cored and stuffed with cinnamon-sugar butter and wrapped in pie dough, baked to perfection. Sorry I just had to. Sue me!!

The highlight was this delightful find for $12:

I was looking for something like this in Mexico and of course they were everywhere but I only found heavy pottery and didn’t want to lug it home. Who knew that this cheery metal sculpture would find me all the way from Haiti, via the Bloomsburg fair. Oh wow, a bee just landed on it and bumbled around confused for a few seconds. Love it!

### Phosphene — 2019-09-22T00:51:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-278529>

Oh, and happy (soon to be) Autumn Equinox!

Boogie down!

Day 6

This is almost getting boring—clear and a balmy 55F.

I’m out here with my part-time dog Gus today. Kira is somewhere...probably stalking something. I may put a bell on her to give the critters a chance. No birds deposited on the doorstep thankfully, but several mice.

### Phosphene — 2019-09-23T01:17:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-278628>

Deep truth that I am reminded of often and need to absorb and reabsorb, to thorough saturation:

In a world where things constantly and inexorably change, we suffer because we attach and cling to that which can never last...

Yet how to reconcile this with the concept of commitment/investment, which involves attaching and clinging to things/situations and hoping they will last?

It comes down to non-attachment to the outcome: being content with whatever is, after we’ve done our very best.

### LieselK — 2019-09-23T02:53:52+0300

Yes! This! Such peace in that place.
