Day 8

I feel like this is the world’s most awesome sobriety check. When do I get my token?

### Phosphene — 2019-09-24T15:31:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-278738>

Something quite breathtaking just happened. I was startled by some creature buzzing by my neck from behind and got all flustered for a second, and then realized it was just an annual cicada. He landed quite deftly on the edge of the large clipboard that I use to hold a notebook, reading material and yes, often my phone. (Keeps it away from my body and hands and I now carry it with me whenever I choose to take my phone. Learned this trick from [@shah78](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1152/) — thanks!)

He perched there quite calmly and as I got a closer look I realized he only had half of his six legs! (Hopefully not due to my cat...)

The two right front appendages were down to little black stumps. The large back left leg was gone as well, along with half of one antenna. How in the world was he managing? Quite well it seems. He sat with me for about 15 minutes and when Kira approached (perhaps to finish the job) he escaped and blended right in to a nearby tree.

So inspiring how we, and all nature, can adapt.

### Phosphene — 2019-09-25T14:43:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-278829>

Day 9

Clear again and 44F.

As I bound out of bed this morning a dark thought came over me. “Yeah it’s easy NOW but just you wait Missy till it’s 0F and miserable out there. Then whatcha gonna do about leaving this comfy bed?”

It’s a valid thought but I pushed it away. I reminded myself that I’ll have the camper and on those really hideous days the morning light will already be coming through the open window and I can just bask in it there if necessary, and my sun spot will literally be just a few steps away instead an acre.

Plus...most of January in Mexico! Woo-hoo!

Must add 0 degree sleeping bag to my list. I used to have one but it disappeared with all the packing and unpacking. Or maybe one of the boys lost it. Not sure the down comforter and wool blankets will suffice on the coldest nights. We don’t get below zero all that often but really anything under 20F counts as miserable to me. I’m MUCH more cold adapted than I used to be, but can’t sacrifice my sleep. Good sleep is just vital for every aspect of my health. Wish I’d known 40 years ago that good waking is just as vital.

### ElectricUniverse — 2019-09-25T21:55:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-278887>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=278336)
>
> You sleep for 9-10 hours a night???

Not unusual, especially for someone under age of 65. For some reason seniors sleep less than younger or middle aged adults. I remember needing 9-10 hours of sleep as a teenager to feel fully rested. **Is Drezy still a teenager?**

**Siim Land**, noted hacker, has an interesting podcast where he claims we really need less sleep than customary approx. 7-9 hours most folks spend in slumberland.

By his estimation, roughly 2 hours of our sleep time is a light sleep that does not contribute to repair/restorative body processes found in deep and REM sleep stages at night. And he gives some tips and hacks for achieving an incredible goal of gaining perhaps several waking hours from all that time lying in bed. He even discusses _polyphasic_ sleep approach (not recommended!).

### Phosphene — 2019-09-26T14:25:59+0300

Day 10

55F and partly cloudy. Love it when the clouds get all lavender glowy for those few fleeting minutes. Just beyond precious, the every changing art museum.

I need to start rationing my forum time a bit better. 20 hours a week working doesn’t seem like much but getting back on some type of schedule is going to be essential for not falling back into my old traps.

### ElectricUniverse — 2019-09-27T18:20:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-279059>

> [Phosphene said:](/goto/post?id=278664)
>
> Ah, now that’s more up my alley. Stars and Stripes forever! Love when Ma Nature turns all Jackson Pollock-y.
>
> [View attachment 13174](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/13174/)

Like you, I like skies with character: lots of large, dramatic cloud formations changing into chimeras according to wind and sun angles. The subtle and often nuanced colors and shades are truly some of God's most beautiful artwork.

However,absolutely cloudless clear blue skies from horizon to horizon are rare and very auspicious and something to be savored-- boding well for health and happiness.

### Phosphene — 2019-09-28T14:45:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-279126>

Day 12

Clear and 58F. Amother auspicious one? I’ll take it. Changing the perspective a bit today—or whatever that thing is when you switch from landscape to portrait...variety, ya know?

I spent the afternoon yesterday with two friends I don’t see very often, in the small town where I’ll be working. The weather was SO fabulous everyone was in a banner mood—there was no way you couldn’t be. It’s parent’s weekend at the uni and there were tons of people out and about, quite a few not glued to their phones even! We grounded flat on our backs at the park for a bit, staring up at the brilliant, boundless sky.

I bought a free-range duck at a tiny pop-up farmer’s market in a parking lot, and a gorgeous magenta dinner plate dahlia like I used to grow. The duck was $20 so I’d better not screw it up. I’m pretty frugal and usually only splurge on seafood so I can’t remember the last time I roasted one. Any suggestions?

### Phosphene — 2019-09-28T15:49:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-279133>

WTF is this creature that just landed on my boob? (Yes I have some clothing on today—boys are home! )

### Jenelle — 2019-09-28T16:47:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-279135>

[https://www.colonialpest.com/scary-bug-looks-like-dinosaur/](https://www.colonialpest.com/scary-bug-looks-like-dinosaur/)

### Phosphene — 2019-09-29T03:11:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-279172>

Thanks! My quick search of ‘grasshopper with buzzsaw on back’ was fruitless. Assassin bug! How...non-auspicious. Thankfully the day turned out fab—whole family went for a hike on a new trail and it was awesome, though it indeed could’ve been a killer—nearly 1000 foot elevation change over about 1.5 miles, and temp in the 80’s. It was like hiking up a wooded ski slope. Phewwww!! We had no idea going into it—well-intentioned recommendation from mother-in-law who saw it in a magazine, and only hikes out to her mailbox. We picked the vista trail thinking how bad can it be? Uhhhhhh... Somehow I’m not in quite as good shape as I remember, and hubs is much worse, needing to stop quite often. DS1 and 2 could have sprinted up but stayed back with us, sometimes out of genuine concern for their dad, who was struggling and still 100 pounds heavier than us, though slowly losing it.

We are going to hike more often to rectify this. We are within easy drive of parks with trails of all sorts so there’s absolutely no excuse.

### Phosphene — 2019-09-29T14:12:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-279180>

Day 13

A few proper clouds and bit of wind today but warm—64F. Red light morning. Clearing up soon though.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

I’m out with Gus and he’s a good dog but definitely a plaything. No work done by that looker but I’ll bet he’d be adept at agility training, if I had the patience for that. There was a dog show and sale at the fair last week and sons were drooling over the Siberian husky puppies, but I’m not sure. Not a great time to get another dog with me starting work, though I guess other folks manage it. Plus part-time Gus was free. We didn’t dare ask the price of the pups—prefer to get rescues.

The dachshunds were adorable too though... and they at least were bred to be vermin hunters. Brave and keen watchdogs too. My little girl would try to defend the family against anything, and once found herself in the jaws of a neighbor’s pit bull. Very lucky that didn’t end badly.

We definitely love our animals, and they give us so much in return.

### drezy — 2019-09-29T22:55:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-279213>

> [Phosphene said:](/goto/post?id=279200)
>
> In other news, what is it with me and wildlife? Almost stepped on this little dude in my yard, and I rarely see snakes here.

My wild lizards of different species watch me very regularly.

I can't help but get the vibe that they negotiated an ability amongst themselves to ditch territoriality in order to get close and in the same zone to see what the "big lizard" was doing. since he apparently got so big and size (usually) correlates with success in the lizard world.

I have to imagine that seeing a live human basking in the sun is a rare event for an animal and even with animal brains there must be some concept of novelty. What is nature if not a novelty generator and appreciator?

### Jenelle — 2019-09-30T16:12:16+0300

### Phosphene — 2019-10-01T14:52:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-279284>

Day 15

Third day of cloudy but still warm-ish sunrise. He’s there somewhere. Kira is enjoying it with me again and I’m so tempted to get another one. The only thing better than one cat is two.

She’s not too keen on the new collar but seems to be wandering farther away during the day and should have something since I refused to get her microchipped when she got “fixed”. (I did that reluctantly as well since I’m sure she’d be a wonderfiul mom, but there are way more than enough cats already.) Plus with the boys mostly gone she really is our substitute kid, and we are a bit more doting and protective than with the others. Would be great if it didn’t have that tiny metal ring by her throat—have another on order without that.

Went to a yoga class yesterday for the first time in ages. I’ve been making excuses and saying I’m fine doing a bit on my own, but there is NOTHING like a good yoga class with a proper deep guided relaxation at the end, like the Integral style offers. Such great energy in the room when done with others, and that cannot be discounted. It was even strong enough to overcome the downside of the class being in a new state of the art blue-lit fitness facility, especially since the instructor turned almost all of them off.

There was some sadness as well, since this is my first class not held in the old repurposed church opened by my guru Sudharman. He was murdered in that studio about 10 years ago by a schizophrenic former student off his meds, and the place never fully recovered. Finally closed the doors a few months ago despite valiant efforts by devoted students to keep it open.

Yeah there’s a long story there.

### Jenelle — 2019-10-01T15:22:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-279291>

Holy shit ... I like your sunrise cat picture. The murder — not so much!

### Phosphene — 2019-10-01T19:18:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-279313>

Yeah it was a mind fuck circus for sure. Happened at night in the living quarters of the studio/church, durning the small town’s Independence Day fireworks display so nobody would think twice about a few more loud bangs. Truly chilling, and well planned.

The locals were of course gobsmacked and I’m surprised the story didn’t reach national news. This account from the perpetrator’s hometown actually gives the best summary.

This certainly contributed to my downward spiral.

[https://www.stltoday.com/news/local...cle\_c1ad8a9c-8b6b-11df-9f6b-00127992bc8b.html](https://www.stltoday.com/news/local/crime-and-courts/yoga-instructor-from-creve-coeur-is-charged-in-bizarre-murder/article_c1ad8a9c-8b6b-11df-9f6b-00127992bc8b.html)

### Phosphene — 2019-10-01T20:07:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-279316>

Copying this here for further contemplation. I still focus too much on my perceived weaknesses and often use the peg/hole analogy to describe my troubles fitting in. In today’s environment this is a strength. Yes of course!

“You must leverage your strengths, and not just focus on repairing/fixing your weaknesses. If you obsess too much on working on your opportunity areas it can grow tiresome--because it's tiring work. Doing the easy work matters too; that is, leveraging your natural strengths to make progress on things that matter. MOST. This includes being self-aware of both your strengths and your weaknesses and having self-compassion to be patient with the latter.

We can all too easily fall into the trap of lasering in on our weaknesses, ones exacerbated in our mind when we compare ourself to others. We’ll also find when we switch gears to leveraging our strengths, it's an effortless task because it renews the energy depleted in striving to get better at something we’re not so good at.” —JK

### Phosphene — 2019-10-02T14:44:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-279375>

Day 16

Ah that’s better! Sunny and 63F with a touch of mist. Even three crows if you look closely.

Love it when the angle and the mist causes those little green glowing orbs to appear in pics—like ghosts.

### Phosphene — 2019-10-02T15:10:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-279376>

Is it possible that my mood took a dive from just three days of clouds? No I don’t think so —I’m more resilient than that now. One of my circle is in town and she’s still dealing with major grief over the loss of her son to an opioid overdose. It’s the 11 year anniversary and his birthday is this month. Her aching mother’s heart still hasn’t healed and I guess probably never completely will. She’s the one who got me into yoga and gently encouraged the deep dive. I was compelled to take the teacher training after meeting Sudharman and taking just two yoga classes ever. Absurd, right? We took the training together. Her son’s memorial was held at the yoga center in the old church, which recently closed. It was her idea to take the class Monday night and I felt so close to her lying there on our mats, stretching our bodies and freeing our emotions. I’ll be working at her son-in-law’s restaurant...we are so entangled.

I’m still dealing with grief as well—my guru, my still rocky (but on the mend) marriage, the detachment from my paramour, who I did indeed love very much, and part of me always will.

Still, I’m handling it all well. Glad it’s not December... I’m aching a bit from the class—that good ache from stretching things that needed to been stretched and releasing things that needed to be released. I did collapse into a nap yesterday afternoon (first in ages) after a spat with hubs over our perpetual bugaboo—money, and my sleep was off last night. I’m finally going back to work and he’s still giving me shit over how much I ‘owe’ him? Fuck that.

Ok enough—it’s an awesome day. Move on.

### Jenelle — 2019-10-02T15:16:47+0300

Anyone feel free to analyze.

My initial thought is, “You aren’t getting enough action.”

But it was toads ... not cobwebs ... lmao.

That’s pretty fucked up.

### LieselK — 2019-10-02T19:32:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-279420>

> [Phosphene said:](/goto/post?id=279376)
>
> I’m finally going back to work and he’s still giving me shit over how much I ‘owe’ him? Fuck that.

The thing that bothers me about this line is the mentality behind it regardless of why he might have said it. Granted this is one instance and I have no other real context to put around it. But as someone who has been studying abuse A LOT it is always about power and control. A comment like this definitely deserves a "fuck that". If this is one of many then it's clearly destructive, if not abusive and that makes me super sad. You have said your marriage is healing and I hope you are safe to speak up and call him to account for his careless... destructive... or abusive words. Because words, backed by the mentality of power and control, really do hurt. A lot. It doesn't mean we give up, but it's a lot of work to stay well and move forward in the same direction. Thinking of you and sending love your way!

### Jenelle — 2019-10-02T20:35:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-279426>

I'm glad some other people can respond with serious posts ~ while I was just here this morning, wanting to provide a much-needed laugh.

The truth is: There are some things I hear/read that make me cringe internally _so much_. And a rush of advice comes to my mind. A knee-jerk reaction.

And my next thought is: "Keep your mouth shut. This is not your battle. And they are not asking for your advice."

But [@Phosphene](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19648/) ... I want you to know I'm here for you. If you ever do need someone to talk to.

Nice to some degree (ooo—I forgot about that nice thing in my closet—lets see if it still fits...) but I’m kinda hoping we get uniforms so I don’t have to think too much about it. My brain will be taxed enough—I can tell already.

Years ago I was told about an article that followed a woman who gave herself a uniform and how it made her a better employee. Guys have a uniform (shirt and tie) and while they can mix and match, it's still SO much easier. She basically did black pants, white shirt and wore the same damn thing day in and out. Totally easy, less mental energy involved and she was highly successful. So feel free to give yourself one!

### Phosphene — 2019-10-03T15:14:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-279474>

Day17

Cloudy, light rain, 58F. Not so great, but I was out. Had a chat with another forum member as the sun was doing his thing behind the clouds, and it was great to connect. Need to do this more often—actual words coming out of mouth.

### Phosphene — 2019-10-03T17:35:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-279487>

Meeting went well and I slept like the dead last night from 9:30-6:00. Went into it with head held high, knowing I can bring a lot to his tables. We actually started late because he was still cleaning up—owner’s job is never done. I pitched in by putting away plates and cups, taste-testing a yummy spicy coconut milk-based veggie soup (a bunch of shrimp would make it perfect), plating up and storing freshly baked scones for the morning, and plunging my arms into greasy water to clean the floor mats. First time in that tiny cafe kitchen (not the new one where I’ll be working) and I held my own.

He liked the employee handbook template I found and we started making revisions. At one point when discussing employee roles I said, “Would it help you to see a diagram of all your employees? It would help me.” He agreed and I banged out a rough organizational chart that I now need to finalize. We were both kind of amazed when we realized that he’ll have almost 25 employees when the three restaurants are fully staffed! He exclaimed, in his endearing Italian accent, “Wow it’s like I’m actually becoming a restauranteur!” Yes my friend you are.

I still worry that we are both taking on a bit too much, but I’m confident we can handle it. He seems to have good intuition about employees because many have been with him for years and there have been relatively few HR problems. He said he works long hours but has no problem switching off when home with his wife and two kids. May it continue.

I’m not avoiding the comments above—still pondering. I have the whole weekend with the house to myself with plenty of time to reply, and perhaps talk. Hubs and DS1 are headed to philly for a concert, and son wants to watch an Eagles game at an actual Philly bar for the first time (cringe, but yeah kinda fun at his age I guess).

Many thoughts are surfacing about my dear paramour. We haven’t seen each other for almost 2 years now, and very little other contact, but the 5 year connection is still strong and I will always care about him. I’ve been avoiding posting details about our time together but I think some of it needs to come out to help the purge, and the ongoing detachment. I do miss him, and Portugal, and the ashram. It was both a magical and miserable experience...

Look what I picked back up again for re-reading:

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

I misplaced my original copy and borrowed this from my one friend I convinced to buy it. She actually didn’t read it... but her husband did.  He’s a recently retired 35 year family physician who is not entirely on board yet but has modified his diet a bit and gets out in nature barefoot every chance he gets. (And probably now uses this as an excuse for not answering his phone...)

He said it took him awhile to get through (and was not a fan of the style) but he certainly highlighted a bunch. It’s interesting to see which parts, and also interesting that I actually understand so much more this time around. Hooray I’m learning!

### Phosphene — 2019-10-06T01:26:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-279663>

So I sent this to ‘A’ today (paramour) with no other words. None needed—says it all with beauty and grace. We used to constantly communicate thoughts and feelings via music, and this will ring loud and clear.

There have been relatively few words exchanged the past two years but the connection is still so electric. He pops into my thoughts several times a day no matter how hard I try to suppress them. I had requested a divorce before we met, and had gotten eyeballs deep into a radically new life with him.

But my life here fought and fought for me, tooth and nail, tear and smile, love and forgiveness. And the other life was being shoddily constructed on a foundation of fantasy, lies, and pity, and the mortar of deep love just wasn’t enough to fill the cracks.

Fly on dear Cloudwalker. I’ll still tell your story.

(Sorry for the Saturday night downer. It is what it is.)

### Phosphene — 2019-10-06T04:22:01+0300

> [Phosphene said:](/goto/post?id=279663)
>
> And the other life was being shoddily constructed on a foundation of fantasy, lies, and pity, and the mortar of deep love just wasn’t enough to fill the cracks.

No, it wasn’t pity—it was compassion.

Day 20

Cloudy, light spritzing drizzle, 58F

Kira did not come in for the night (not unusual—she often stays out and sleeps in the garage at some point). But she’s not around this morning either, which IS unusual. Need to investigate.

\[edit\] False alarm! Curled up in a new spot on the back porch where we watched the sunset yesterday.

I’m so attached to this little love bug.

### Phosphene — 2019-10-06T15:42:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-279682>

Of course ‘A’ showed up in my dreams last night for the first time in a long while. He’s determined, and not a quitter. I hesitate to give specifics because MS is a cruel and humiliating disease and he suffers enough already, but I think he’d be ok with it. It’s just the honest truth, which I’m determined to stick with now.

It was a brief encounter, in no specific place, but we were in bed sleeping and his arm was draped around me (as it always was until he overheated and needed to separate). I woke up in the dream and was thrilled that the bed wasn’t wet, and I didn’t have to inch away from from the urine that formed a warm, damp spot most nights. I thought, “Maybe the Coimbra Protocol actually is working!?!”

And then it ended.

I spent endless time researching MS cures before stumbling onto Kruse. ‘A’ is a big fan of supplements, and I was at the time as well, but he wasn’t sold on any of it. He had spent so much time, money and hope chasing cures and was just sick of it. I finally convinced him to consult a Coimbra-trained practitioner because people were (and still are) having remarkable success with extremely high-dose vitamin D, with other cofactor supplements (several forms of magnesium and a B vitamin, I think maybe B3.

I know [@Theka](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/17862/) has mentioned trying the protocol, but I haven’t reached out to her about it yet because I wasn’t ready to talk about ‘A’ and get myself all wrapped up in it again.

I’m still confused why Coimbra works for many autoimmune diseases, especially if caught in the early stages. Perhaps I’ll venture a question at the next Q&A.

‘A’ definitely was not in the early stages, and has suffered for nearly 20 years. He has reported improvements in energy level and urine retention, but no change to his walking, eyesight, and temperature regulation—the major debilitations. It’s enough to keep him sticking with it, despite my current concerns about ruining the feedback loop with supplements.

I wish him health, strength, peace and new love.

(Don’t worry—no violence here!)

### Phosphene — 2019-10-08T21:15:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-279854>

Oh man, why do I always feel like writing when I’ve got other stuff to do? Couldn’t this desire/need to spew intimate details of my life have come when I didn’t have a job???? No of course not.

Just need to manage my time and not get anxious and sleep-deprived.

When ‘A’ noticed me getting anxious and worked up and irritated and hyper about the complicated mess I’d made of my life, he always reminded that there are no lions and tigers here anymore. Nothing’s chasing us. Nothing’s chasing us. Nothing’s chasing us...

The marriage had been rocky and tedious for about 15 years, and I couldn’t take any more of it, or of counseling. My husband refused the divorce and said he’d make it long, expensive and painful if I filed.

So I didn’t.

Instead I ran away and left the boys, then 14 and 17, in his (and our families’) very capable hands. He had been doing most of the serious parenting anyway as I got more and more sick and unable to cope.

I told everyone (including myself) that I needed time away to heal—that I DESERVED time away after burning the candle at both ends for so long.

Would I ever have guessed that the time away would involve falling in love with a partially disabled British yogi, living with and assisting him for months at a time (both in Portugal and near the ashram in Virginia) and ultimately coming back to my marriage for yet another shot at it?

No of course not.

They were two very kind, strong and persistent men dealing with one very fucked up woman, and I’m grateful to both for the gifts and lessons I’ve received both large and small.

It’s definitely time to write some of this down before I forget. Whether it’s wise to do it publicly is anyone’s guess, but none of this is a secret anymore, so it feels ok.

It feels more than ok—it feels liberating.

Ok back to work.

### LieselK — 2019-10-08T21:20:50+0300

Verbalizing the truth sheds much-needed light. Hard sometimes, but necessary and with it comes so much freedom.

### Phosphene — 2019-10-13T14:38:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-280306>

Day 27

Mostly clear, 34F, a few wispy crinkled clouds. Some of them look like Ma Nature dug out her crimping iron (remember those hideous things from the 80’s?) and went to town on her wispy locks.

I have a picture somewhere of me sitting on the bathroom sink of my college dorm with my roommate, after just having crimped our hair, and shotgunning a beer. Somehow at the time all three of those things seemed fun and reasonable. I haven’t crimped my hair or shotgunned a beer since, so I guess I learned a bit. I still sit on my sink sometimes. You don’t have to know why!

Day 28

Clear 38F with spooky, beguiling fog rolling up the valley making Senior Sun even more magestic with each passing minute.

It’s like Kira is staring down that green mystery dot like it’s her next prey.

### Phosphene — 2019-10-14T19:42:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-280423>

I occasionally check up on about eight people on FB and this is one of them. Former CDC microbiologist, cosmologist, and physicist, turned Swami Murugananda. His current career includes Ashram archivist, film critic, and standup comedian.  God I miss that place.

[https://m.facebook.com/story.php?story\_fbid=10215679583282867&id=1240748484](https://m.facebook.com/story.php?story_fbid=10215679583282867&id=1240748484)

### Phosphene — 2019-10-14T20:10:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-280424>

Ok one more bit then I have to do some work that can’t be done out here.

My hawks are back! They’ve been gone for a few months and just showed up again, with family. This one is so huge I thought it was a vulture or eagle at first—sitting there for over an hour. Then three more joined and were screeching happily among themselves in those treetops high above my house. I’m almost a bit concerned for Kira since she can’t be more than 10 pounds. She’s a scrappy fighter though, and no dummy.

### Phosphene — 2019-10-14T21:00:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-280426>

Okay, indoor plan aborted. It’s just too gorgeous out today and this is the last UV 5 I’ll see till spring.

I brought some work outside.

I need to sell (or scrap) a car and I’ve actually never done this before. It can’t be that hard but it’s a bit emotional and I think it’s worth more than it probably is...

It was my escape pod a few years ago. A gift from my uncle that was well taken care of and worked perfectly. Until it didn’t. Over and over. And nobody in this family inherited the car tinkering gene.

It’s a somewhat hobby collectible 1990 Honda Civic SI hatchback that now has a blown engine, but otherwise is in pretty good shape for her age, (like me ). More faded after sitting outside for two years, and tires probably ruined too.

I don’t have a current picture of my lovely Cinnamon Rose, but I’ll grab a few tomorrow. It’s sitting behind the family business that is still up for sale and FIL wants it gone.

Craig’s List? Local paper? It’s half an hour away and doesn’t run so I don’t want a ton of hassle. Should probably just donate it.

### Phosphene — 2019-10-15T15:13:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-280463>

Day 29

Clear and cool again 36F. Won’t be as warm today as yesterday but this whole summer/fall has been just gorgeous with sun it seems. (Or perhaps I’m just not minding the clouds quite as much as I used to?) Either way I’ll take it.

When I stepped out the door the clouds looked like feathered wings and immediately reminded me of yet another hairstyle. I made desperate attempts in sixth through tenth grades to achieve the Farrah Faucet feathered look, which mostly resulted in goofy hairspray lacquered plates on either side of the head. Sigh. My friend Lori usually got it just right, and I was so jealous. But it took her literally two hours to get ready in the morning, and even back then I valued sleep over style so my lacquered plates had to do.

If the clouds are perfect curly corkscrews tomorrow I will get down on nearly naked knees and pray. Oh wait, I already do that. What odd and humbly, gratefully celebratory thing shall I do tomorrow if said miracle occurs??

### Phosphene — 2019-10-15T20:45:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-280494>

So I was wrong about it being colder today. Well it IS actually 15 degrees colder than yesterday, but in full sun with a light south wind 57F feels just delightful. I’m still so surprised how I can tolerate colder temps on bare skin as long as the sun is out. I didn’t go hardcore with CT this summer but I did get in natural water outdoors several times a week—oceans, bays, lakes, streams, and most convenient—the river.

This is the view from the boat launch where I like to wade in. It’s clear and shallow this time of year and not much trouble to keep the current from taking you.

I had a quite mystical experience recently. At least that’s how I chose to label it—might seem ordinary to most. I was out about halfway just treading water and floating, gazing upstream when a tiny leaf whizzed by. Out of the corner of my eye I noticed an odd bump and grabbed it before it slipped away.

To my great surprise a honeybee was clinging.

I held it up for a close look and sure enough it was still alive, and my lifeguard instinct kicked in compelling me to swim back to shore with this creature and leaf held high out of the water. I gave zero shits about the curious stares from those on shore, who clearly think I’m loony for swimming in October anyway.

I set the leaf gently down on a bench and sat with it as recovery slowly set in.

It’s fascinating to watch a bee dry itself off with the forelegs, just like a cat. After about 20 minutes it did indeed fly off, circling me twice.

Some redeeming karma points for sure right? If not, just a really special afternoon.

### KrusinWitchie — 2019-10-15T21:32:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-280497>

### Phosphene — 2019-10-15T22:18:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-280500>

You have no idea. I have a bunch of pics from this season that I haven’t had a chance to post. Various bugs just land on me and hang out for awhile. As long as they don’t bite they’re welcome to stay.

Although I thought if you attracted bugs that means you’re leaking light? Whatever, it’s cool no matter.

### Phosphene — 2019-10-16T14:32:00+0300

Day 30

Day 30!!!!! Don’t care if rain is on the way—this is an accomplishment! Not only getting out for the sunrise everyday, but also just being here at home day after day after day. Aside from a few short bouts of wanderlust I was perfectly content to be right here, and that is indeed a first.

48F and more ash gray clouds. Charcoal tinged cotton puffs, not leaking at least. Rained all day yesterday but got needed indoor stuff done. Winds picking up to 20 MPH later—my outdoor nemesis.

### Phosphene — 2019-10-18T15:04:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-280717>

Day 32

48F, breezy and clearing. Should be a gorgeous weekend.

That bug was some kind of leaf hopper I think. I remember when it finally got sick of hanging with me it parted that bushy sky blue tail exactly in two and propelled itself way over my head and left me grinning in amazement.

I oddly don’t care that much about identifying exactly what these bugs are. I’m just honored they chose to spend part of their brief and brutal lives with me.

54F, sunny, topless, UV4 and working on a spreadsheet!

I’ll have the camera ready for any lewd bugs but no promises folks.

Oooh, I did just spy a red (yes RED) dragonfly, but he’s too quick for me.

\[edit\] Paramour’s stepfather just died yesterday. Wonder if he fluttered over the pond to finally meet me? If so, enjoy the show!

### Phosphene — 2019-10-19T14:59:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-280779>

Day 33

31F and mostly clear. No frost yet but a bit of fog. Love how it amplifies the appearance of the sun.

### Phosphene — 2019-10-20T14:54:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-280823>

Day 34

46F an mostly cloudy. Rain rolling in soon. Red light day...

Boys are both home again and we had a fab outing at the aptly named Worlds End State Park about 1.5 hours from here in the boonies of Sullivan County, not far from where my maternal grandparents spent their youth. Toured the campground where i spent much wondrous time as a child, hiked (or shall I say crawled) High Rock Run trail, and took a dip in the bracing Loyalsock creek. Yeah I was the only loonie doing that, and could only take about 5 minutes. No thermometer but water must’ve been in the 40’s. Could’ve stayed longer if I had diving booties and gloves. Another addition to the list.

### Phosphene — 2019-10-21T14:50:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-280868>

Day 35

48, cloudy and a bit blah. (Like me 2.5 years ago! )

To liven things up I’ll add the CT proof from the weekend. Blurry pic but the thought was there. Silly dog gets all the focus.

### drezy — 2019-10-21T16:59:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-280883>

> [Phosphene said:](/goto/post?id=280868)
>
> To liven things up I’ll add the CT proof from the weekend. Blurry pic but the thought was there. Silly dog gets all the focus.

The more blurry the picture the younger I look!

I used this trick on my Father in law who is an extreme camera picture geek. He kept admiring the pictures I took of him. I eventually admitted that I adjusted my canon dslr slightly out of focus for the same reason I'd help any 80yo guy with heavy packages. That picture was printed and on his wall last trip we made to his place.

### Phosphene — 2019-10-22T14:55:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-280944>

> [ElectricUniverse said:](/goto/post?id=280900)
>
> Beautiful scenery, and nary a cell tower (phone mast) in sight. Yet.

Unfortunately they are here, you just can’t see them in the distance. There are two visible from where I watch the sunrise, 5-10 miles away.

This one however is about .8 mile away, and visible from another part of the yard which is now rather lonesome...

Day 36

Cloudy, 58 and more rain on the way after a sunny afternoon yesterday. Good a day as any to show the closer tower I guess. Refuses to even come into focus, and evergreens and another house block it from my sight, but not my mind.

### Phosphene — 2019-10-22T15:00:23+0300

### ElectricUniverse — 2019-10-22T18:57:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-280964>

> [drezy said:](/goto/post?id=280883)
>
> The more blurry the picture the younger I look!
>
> I used this trick on my Father in law who is an extreme camera picture geek. He kept admiring the pictures I took of him. I eventually admitted that I adjusted my canon dslr slightly out of focus for the same reason I'd help any 80yo guy with heavy packages. That picture was printed and on his wall last trip we made to his place.

No need to blur the photo-- with today's camera/image apps you can make yourself look younger or older on a whim, LOL. A lot of things are fake these days and it ain't just the news.

Now, I figure if I appear older in photos younger women will see their father image in me and I am in like Flynn.

### drezy — 2019-10-22T19:28:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-280965>

> [ElectricUniverse said:](/goto/post?id=280964)
>
> No need to blur the photo-- with today's camera/image apps you can make yourself look younger or older on a whim, LOL. A lot of things are fake these days and it ain't just the news.
>
> Now, I figure if I appear older in photos younger women will see their father image in me and I am in like Flynn.

When I was in China I sat behind a woman on a train that spent 40 minutes tweaking a 2 second social media video of herself. She used the filters you mentioned along with settling on a virtual flower petal fountain. 40 minutes!

I know that westerners do all the same things. I just got to witness 40 minutes of it in this instance. The irony is that we were passing by the Wudang mountains considered the Sacred Mountains of Taoism. I'm the last guy that'll ever be wowed by foreign (or any) mystical traditions and styles, but I couldn't escape the thought of how many people worked hard to get to sit with stillness and contemplate nature the in this place we were passing through at 180mph as this lady fidgeted seemingly endlessly with her 2 second video.

For all I know she was making the video to cheer up someone ill or depressed or she was trying to lure a mate. I had no pejorative thoughts. The past and present contrast just left with one of those head scratching moments.

### Phosphene — 2019-10-23T15:00:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-281026>

Day 36

Hot damn—it’s an auspicious one! Not hot unfortunately but 45 and brilliant. Light mist burning off now.

### Phosphene — 2019-10-23T15:37:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-281030>

Slept well last night and nearly galloped outside to greet the dawn, grinning like the enchanted ape that I am. This is such a gift it brings me to tears some days. Of course these days with the sunrise at nearly 7:30 I’m waking earlier and getting out pre-dawn which is even more miraculous when creatures are just starting to stir.

I had work to do yesterday (and today) so won’t be on here much, but can I just reiterate how grateful I am for this forum? So fucking lucky to have found it. I watched the Bruce Lipton video recommended by others, and have started listening to one of Matty’s podcasts with Luke Story (#234 - one of his best yet I think). He’s such a brilliant and ballsy young man and getting more and more articulate with these concepts by the day. Way to go [@Matty\_M](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/11546/) ! I don’t think he’s on the forum much anymore, but his journal is well worth a glimpse to see how far he’s come.

I’m really glad I watched the Bruce Lipton video (in Iris red mode) instead of just listening. Although I already understood the basic biology something clicked with his visuals and I put it all together that the environment is the SIGNAL that prompts cellular function and gene expression. Mind expanded yet again! I’d read part of one book years ago and it wasn’t resonating then. Well it is fucking now.

### Phosphene — 2019-10-24T20:18:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-281191>

Lunch break!

64F and UV4 for two more hours. Exactly one month till the restaurant opens (if all goes well) so I’m enjoying my time outdoors now. The prep room has a huge window that can be opened, and there will be no problem subbing out lights if needed. My tentative hours will be 10-4 three or four days a week, plus some office work from home and pitching in where needed.

### Phosphene — 2019-10-25T00:16:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-281213>

View of the oft neglected back screened sunset porch. I’ve avoided this area in the past because the (thankfully still analog) electric meter is two walls and about 12 feet away. A meter that I recent borrowed showed negligible EMF hazard though, so I’m going with it for now. FEELS good out here, and that counts for a lot in my book.

### Phosphene — 2019-10-25T14:40:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-281239>

Day 38

38F and partly cloudy. Clouding up more throughout the day and looks like a rainy weekend. Oh well. Can’t have perfection all the time. I actually got a bit rosy outside yesterday afternoon!

The clouds today look very much like the flocks of birds that pepper the sky this time of year. Love October for that.

### Phosphene — 2019-10-26T14:46:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-281280>

Day 39

41 and mostly clear for now. Another gift! Although are those streaks the dreaded chemtrails? They’re starting to dissipate into wispy nothingness now, but it bothers me that every time the clouds look odd I wonder about any nefarious origins instead of just enjoying the beauty. Thus is the downside of the red pill I suppose.

I like these streaks better.

### Saichi — 2019-10-26T17:47:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-281286>

Welcome to reality Phosph.

The white line coming off the sun's X in the bottom pic is a plane at the head of it laying a fresh trail. The first pic are also dissipated trails. It's pretty much a non-stop operation these days.

### Phosphene — 2019-10-26T18:40:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-281287>

But there are no visible aircraft. Are you saying this is coming from farther up in the atmosphere?

### Phosphene — 2019-10-28T14:18:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-281372>

Day 41

48 and mostly clear. Another beauty on tap.

A little late posting but I was out. Got interrupted by sons leaving this morning. They both were home for the weekend again—hiking and (for them) college/NFL football. I watched a bit of the Penn State game but for the most part I couldn’t care less. About 0.1 shits given. It is great to have them home though. Now I understand why my parents were always asking when I’d get back to visit.

I’m still glowing a bit from finally calling into a Q&A for the first time. I’m pretty sure I just babbled on for a few minutes and he graciously allowed it. I hope I adequately expressed my gratitude. More coherent questions next time.

### Phosphene — 2019-10-28T15:29:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-281380>

Oh! And I was just gleeful at the answer to one question. Someone asked about investment opportunities for mitochondriacs and his reply was community...build a COMMUNITY!!!

As I’ve been harping all along, everything is better in a supportive community.

Let’s get our brains going with this folks—maybe a 5-10 year plan? I don’t think it needs to be nearly as expensive as he suggested, but I’ve been known to be naive...

\[edit\] There is at least this to learn what NOT to do...

[https://www.google.com/amp/s/www.vice.com/amp/en\_us/article/bn53b3/atlas-mugged-922-v21n10](https://www.google.com/amp/s/www.vice.com/amp/en_us/article/bn53b3/atlas-mugged-922-v21n10)

### drezy — 2019-10-28T16:35:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-281393>

> [Phosphene said:](/goto/post?id=281380)
>
> I don’t think it needs to be nearly as expensive as he suggested, but I’ve been known to be naive...

Initial unfiltered thoughts

Worth considering:
He may be right.
Or
He may have neurosurgeon level tastes. (I'm pretty sure my malbec isn't the same as Doc's)
Or
both statements may be true

This could be built from the ground up or some angel with big bucks could throw money at it.
Ground up efforts can be touch and go and many iterations deserve to fail in order to learn.
"Angels" with big buck projects may have strings attached as well as run overbudget.

Some regions have unofficial government (aka corruption) that needs to be taken into account. It may be wise to just pay up to corruption in the assumption that it's just one revolution, coup, or strategy away from being official anyways best to have some street cred with it.

The running engineering half-joke is to do the best thorough job you can of cost or time estimation and then multiply by 2.

### Phosphene — 2019-10-28T22:58:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-281412>

Worked outside topless all day in the dwindling October UV4. Felt fantastic and just like summer with no wind and nearly 70F. Soaked it all up—every sight, sound, smell and sensation—since this will likely be the final day like this here till Spring.

Sunny brunch of my usual local eggs and garlic, homegrown purple onions, andouille sausage, sardines, avocado, turmeric, hot sauce.

Got good and bad news. Bad first.

The chef went to quit his job and the institution (for which both I and the restaurant owner used to work) fought back to keep him, nearly doubling his salary to do so. With two kids he decided he needs to stay and sacrifice his freedom in the matrix. Sad for all of us, but the right person will come along.

The good news is that with this setback it’s unlikely the restaurant will open on time, which may give me the opportunity to sneak in a quick trip to Florida with my parents. They have a timeshare booked somewhere on the east coast and want to drive down, not fly. We used to live in Florida, Georgia and Mississippi so driving down to Florida is usually a familiar cakewalk. My 75yo mom however no longer trusts my 83yo dad to drive anywhere but locally, so she hopes I can come along to help drive and keep my sometimes depressed and stubborn dad in line. It may indeed be the last time he can make this trip, so I do hope it works out that I can go.

I would go to Florida in a boat, with a goat, in the rain, in the dark, on a train, in a car, in a tree, in a box, with a fox, in a house, with a mouse.

ENJOY!

### Phosphene — 2019-10-29T02:35:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-281422>

I’m totally leaning towards going to Florida no matter what, but don’t want to stress him out by asking about it yet. We will probably meet on Wednesday to regroup and I may broach it then.

Feeling nostalgic about ‘A’ again. We met a little over five years ago at the ashram. I was separated from my husband but not officially moved out. The tension in the house was horrific and I just had to get out, so enrolled in a weeklong course at the ashram—Yoga of Recovery. It was an emotionally chaotic week for so many reasons, and at the tail end ‘A’ appeared, sitting all alone at one of the tables in the dining hall, eyes reflecting the baby blue of his shirt in a shaft of afternoon sunlight.

It wasn’t quite love at first sight, but very close. I noticed the aluminum crutches jutting out into the aisle and asked if he needed any help. I returned his dishes to the kitchen and came back for a brief chat. I was rushing somewhere for something, but made time for this. It was Friday and he’d already gotten a tour of the campus lined up for the evening with another kind soul. But tomorrow, yes tomorrow we could meet up at lunch, and could I show him my favorite parts? Why yes indeed I’d be delighted to show you my favorite parts!

He was well versed in yoga-speak and offered a sweet and sincere ‘Santosha’ on my way out. I got a few steps away then spun around and admitted I’d forgotten what that means. Would you tell me? “Of course,” he replied in his charming British accent. “Contentment.”

I couldn’t get him off my mind all evening and practically tripped over myself getting to lunch the next day. We spent a delightful day together, me supporting his arm as he lurched awkwardly and slowly—too proud for a wheelchair and convinced his muscles would start to waste away if he gave in and used one. His Multiple Sclerosis had progressed rapidly over the past few years, but he fought his way from being bedbound after his divorce, to wheelchair-bound, to finally walking well enough with the crutches to take a yoga teacher training course and get back to independent traveling. He was truly inspirational and every bit of his words and actions reflected fierce determination to overcome that damn disease.

I was smitten and bludgeoned with an Armani-scented chambray sledgehammer. I certainly wasn’t looking, but boy did I find.

### Phosphene — 2019-10-30T06:00:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-281521>

I’m up too late—bad girl I know. But it’s been quite awhile and not a habit so I may as well spill this out.

I probably should cut communication off with ‘A’ altogether (as most marriage advice recommends) since he still has a strong but waning grip on part of my heart. I’ve tried many times and just can’t. I care too much, and I still want to help him heal in any way I can. I know I’m not responsible for that, or for him in general, but I will always, always love him deeply as a dear friend. Sorry—simply no changing that.

Messages are few and far between these days (and generally in the friend zone) but he’s an outrageous flirt and does slay me with an odd love song now and then. I suppose I should put the kibosh on that.

I really think finally journaling will help get it all out of my system. It was a grand, romantic, strange, daring and hurtful adventure, begun during a time of extreme emotional fragility when I should’ve been focused on just me. I get that now, and I’m sorry for the pain it caused everyone, but I really don’t regret a moment of it. It was a necessary part of my messed up but still perfect journey that I’ll always treasure for its lessons good and bad.

Perhaps the story is old and cliched and I’ll bore you all to tears, but this is all for me. And the few people to whom I’ve spilled parts have mostly been like,

WHOA!! You went WHERE? With WHO? And did WHAT? HOW many times??

So it must be fairly entertaining. I hope I can do it justice.

This classic song arrived the other day. Sigh. Something about that 80’s sax...

Dire Straits - Romeo and Juliet

A lovestruck Romeo sings a streetsuss serenade
Laying everybody low with a lovesong that he made
Finds a convenient streetlight steps out of the shade
Says something like you and me babe how about it?

Juliet says hey it's Romeo you nearly gimme me a heart attack
He's underneath the window she's singing hey la my boyfriend's back
You shouldn't come around here singing up at people like that
Anyway what you gonna do about it?

Juliet the dice were loaded from the start
And I bet and you exploded in my heart
And I forget I forget the movie song
When you gonna realise it was just that the time was wrong Juliet?

Come up on different streets they both were streets of shame
Both dirty both mean yes and the dream was just the same
And I dreamed your dream for you and now your dream is real
How can you look at me as if I was just another one of your deals?

Well you can fall for chains of silver you can fall for chains of gold
You can fall for pretty strangers and the promises they hold
You promised me everything you promised me thick and thin
Now you just say oh Romeo yeah you know I used to have a scene with him

Juliet when we made love you used to cry
You said I love you like the stars above I'll love you till I die
There's a place for us you know the movie song
When you gonna realise it was just that the time was wrong Juliet?

I can't do the talk like they talk on tv
And I can't do a love song like the way it's meant to be
I can't do everything but I'd do anything for you
I can't do anything except be in love with you

And all I do is miss you and the way we used to be
All I do is keep the beat and bad company
All I do is kiss you through the bars of a rhyme
Julie I'd do the stars with you any time...

### Phosphene — 2019-10-30T17:41:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-281559>

My boys got me this for Christmas last year as a gag gift, but there are actually a few decent recipes.

Tried this recently and it was lip-smackin good. Tripled the recipe but subbed out the ribs for chunks of pork shoulder, the peanuts for almonds and the brown sugar for just one tablespoon of coconut sugar for the whole shebang. The sauce is phenomenal.

This was great too (sans buns) with just caramelized onions, gruyere cheese and a bit of truffle oil, which is what I had. This is just a simple burger recipe but I kinda like his ‘Albanian immigrant in Brooklyn’ flair.

I mentioned to hubs that I need to get o the farmer's market for more oysters and raw cream, and this soon blasted from the stereo.

> All we have to do is store some spunk in the permafrost and wait the worst of it out to form a new civilization, right?

Well, if the future is really unkind to us and disaster is staring humanity in the face, perhaps a better choice is to put a contingent of the healthiest known and high IQ young volunteers in a cyrogenic chamber buried underground (powered by a small nuke power plant), with their wake time programmed in distant future.

_**Garden of Eden**_ can be replayed all over again, this time hopefully with a happier ending.

Listen and observe his emotional reaction rather than the words themselves.

If your NASA friend is willing to speak frankly off the cuff you might be surprised. Depending on his scope and expertise in climate science he may shed some light on what the gubmint really knows. So for me there is no doubt we are headed soon for challenging times with the sun and climate-- too many free thinking researchers and scientists have hard core evidence that something unsavory is headed for planet earth. NASA and NOAA and IPCC and mainstream media will deny global cooling, etc. and possible magnetic reversal until the last minute, or until it is at last a politically convenient truth.

As for me, I don't worry much about the future. The future will take care of itself with or without me.

### Phosphene — 2019-10-30T21:48:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-281592>

Copying here from another journal. CT success!

“I also no longer have a problem being in cold water. I like observing the sensations. Last time I was in a very cold creek a few weeks ago I noticed how it actually felt prickly hot at first, in a weirdly good way. It’s getting out and the warmup that puts me off.

You know what? You’ve inspired me to get in my tub right now and try out my new heat lamp if needed for the warmup!

Did it—45 minutes in mostly submerged in 50F well water! (Hands, feet and upper chest out.) The simple one bulb heat lamp was on a few feet away and I just pretended it was the sun. Shivering a bit now but not too bad. This is definitely getting easier.”

### Phosphene — 2019-10-30T22:14:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-281593>

Reminds me of this obscure song by an obscure band. Not sure about the album cover though—nothing particularly evil going on here. Nice one for Halloween I guess.

Afghan Whigs - Birdland

I was a child
An open letter
To be read aloud to the throne
Caught in the spell of stormy weather
Mnemonic lies to behold
Whatever it is that's kept us together
I look to the sky and it's gone
Save one for me and I'll never forget you
Comin' alive in the cold
Comin' alive in the cold
Comin' alive in the cold
(And I say)
So in a haze of feverish lights
Satyr arrives to the throne
We'll come together when the feeling's right
Comin' alive in the cold
Comin' alive in the cold
We're comin' alive in the cold

I’m a bit concerned about Jason. I checked in on him yesterday and woke up to a message asking me to post something in his journal, which I will now do. He’s consumed by deep darkness over something (a girl?). Let’s hope he sees the light soon.

### Phosphene — 2019-10-31T17:48:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-281637>

Five years ago today I was with ‘A’ rummaging through a vintage shop in Charlottesville Virginia in search of costumes. We had made the hour drive from the ashram, as we occasionally did, to get a dose of the ‘real world’ and eat some meat. Even back well before Kruse we both had a visceral understanding that our bodies needed more protein than tofu and beans, and we searched out restaurants recommended by our favorite foodie Swami. I think that day it was brunch at a funky little coffee shop, probably a smoked salmon omelette.

‘A’ had reintroduced me to Dr. Who, which I’d watched on public TV as a child. He had several recent seasons saved on his laptop and we spent many evenings snuggled in bed watching them in our cozy rented cottage, tucked back in the woods across the road from the ashram.

I’d taken quite a shine to heroine/villain/lover River Song, and my curly hair could be coaxed curlier by a hairdresser for a passable resemblance. ‘A’ was the spitting image (or close enough) of the 11th Doctor, and the only costume he needed to transform into the Time Lord was a $5 tweed blazer and bow tie, along with a slightly new haircut.

There was a Halloween party at the ashram that evening, and we intended to make quite a splash. I found an elegant vintage dress, got my hair done into wild curls via Bantu knots under a hooded dryer, and practiced my “Hello Sweetie...” all afternoon.

### Phosphene — 2019-11-01T14:01:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-281664>

Day 45

The sun returns as he always does. 42 and windy. Torrents of leaves came down along with the rain and tornado warnings, but mostly clear skies ahead.

### Phosphene — 2019-11-01T17:52:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-281673>

Another memory surfaced and I just can’t stop writing about them. When I was on my Magical Mystery Tour this summer visiting so many friends, I entangled with this couple in a restaurant.

At the time I was with [@Inna](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20770/) but there was a long wait for our fabulous seafood lunch and she had skedaddled outside for awhile to keep harvesting sun, like the hardcore she is.

So I was quietly observing this couple—how they were relishing their clams, lobster and onion rings, and each other. I finally asked them for their secret, and got big smiles and lovely answers. They were both successful business people who had lost their spouses and found each other. They said one secret was keeping their households and finances separate. In summer they live in their own apartments across town from each other, and meet all over the Boston area for fun and frolics. In winter they share a place in Florida. At one point the man asked me to guess their ages! Always a tricky proposition so I guessed a bit on the young side to be safe. 72? Maybe 75? They both screeched with laughter and proudly announced that she was 87 and he 90!! I was so floored I didn’t dare admonish him about the sunglasses. Obviously they are doing something right.

Talk about life goals there. Check out those heels and that ankle bracelet! Not that I would bother with such things, but perhaps I would on special occasion.

### Phosphene — 2019-11-02T13:50:49+0200

(No I won’t eat it yet...)

### Phosphene — 2019-11-03T16:06:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-281755>

I need to jumpstart my yoga practice again. I do bits her and there, but not enough. I miss the regular stretching, strengthening, breath work, chanting, meditation, deep relaxation. A full Integral yoga class is 1.5 hours and includes all of it.

One of my favorite instructors (and friends) teaches on Monday evenings, and I’m committing to go. $10 a class and well worth it. Then more structured practice on my own throughout the week. I have a simple 36 minute sun salutation chanting series that is challenging and satisfying to complete. Just like CT!

Not entirely sure why I let my practice slide. I could blame the Kruse immersion (and it’s partly true) but I think it’s more to do with grief over the closing of the yoga center, and finally letting go of ‘A’.

It was once a dream to help revitalize the center by offering classes and workshops in adaptive yoga (for disabilities) with ‘A’. This would be in between traveling the world doing the same, of course. It wasn’t an entirely far fetched dream, but many obstacles intervened, and not all obstacles are meant to be overcome.

I posted this to Jason’s journal and copying it here for good measure. I held some of the darkness myself—finally letting go of that as well. We are all transforming—every single second.

“I sent him this yesterday—one of my favorite kirtanists (chant leader for Bhakti yoga of devotion). Best wishes for his ongoing transformation.

This guy is special. See him or (better yet) take a workshop. He and his family opened up my voice in many ways. Looks like he’s in Toronto in a few weeks.

He Maha Lakshmi He Saraswati He Mata Kali Jagatambe Jai Jai Ma
He Ma Durga He Ma Durga He Ma Durga Jagatambe Jai Jai Ma...

We call out to the great Divine Mother in the forms of creator, preserver and transformer
We offer our salutations to She who embodies the highest reaches of Love, the Mother of all”

Isn’t Jagatambe a fun word to say? Yes it is!

Not sure I’ll get to ask my life and death climate questions this weekend. He may be traveling. I can’t get a straight answer.

No worry folks—all is well for now.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-281787>

Quite awhile it seems. And the weather is saying it’s supposed to be clear right now. It’s far from clear.

It was about time for me to head in and warm up anyway, but after I took this last one a distinct, acrid smell permeated the air which was certainly not wood smoke, and I freaked and ran inside.

Just asked hubs to go outside and take a sniff, and he confirmed it smells weird, “Like a citrusy chemical.”

Not a great way to start the week. First time I’ve witnessed this, or paid attention at least. Paranoia?

### Saichi — 2019-11-04T18:13:59+0200

### Phosphene — 2019-11-05T13:55:30+0200

I’ll send the whole series of pics to my NASA friend this weekend after we speak. Timings were not right, but I will definitely talk to him. He at least used to take the pulse of the clouds—spent years camping out at remote high altitude testing stations gathering data.

### Phosphene — 2019-11-05T14:26:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-281831>

I went to yoga yesterday with my good friend from Maine, who happened to be in town. We took teacher training together and the yogic name she was given upon completion is Kali, a fearsome and often misunderstood goddess. Names are assigned after long study of the student by the teacher, based on qualities the student embodies.

“Of all the forms of Devi, She is the most compassionate because She provides _moksha_ or liberation to Her children. She is the counterpart of Shiva the destroyer. They are the destroyers of unreality. The ego sees Mother Kali and trembles with fear because the ego sees in Her its own eventual demise. A person who is attached to his or her ego will not be receptive to Mother Kali and she will appear in a fearsome form. A mature soul who engages in spiritual practice to remove the illusion of the ego sees Mother Kali as very sweet, affectionate, and overflowing with incomprehensible love for Her children.”

[http://www.goddess.ws/kali.html](http://www.goddess.ws/kali.html)

She has three living daughters with chaotic lives, and one son who died from his chaos. She tries to help the daughters manage their chaos, but she is still trapped within her own.

We joyfully chanted on the way to class, before devolving back down into the details of our lives. Chanting is sweet escape into higher vibration, especially when a group combines their energies.

### ElectricUniverse — 2019-11-05T17:08:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-281840>

> [Phosphene said:](/goto/post?id=281673)
>
> Another memory surfaced and I just can’t stop writing about them. When I was on my Magical Mystery Tour this summer visiting so many friends, I entangled with this couple in a restaurant.
>

>
> At the time I was with [@Inna](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20770/) but there was a long wait for our fabulous seafood lunch and she had skedaddled outside for awhile to keep harvesting sun, like the hardcore she is.
>
> So I was quietly observing this couple—how they were relishing their clams, lobster and onion rings, and each other. I finally asked them for their secret, and got big smiles and lovely answers. They were both successful business people who had lost their spouses and found each other. They said one secret was keeping their households and finances separate. In summer they live in their own apartments across town from each other, and meet all over the Boston area for fun and frolics. In winter they share a place in Florida. At one point the man asked me to guess their ages! Always a tricky proposition so I guessed a bit on the young side to be safe. 72? Maybe 75? They both screeched with laughter and proudly announced that she was 87 and he 90!! I was so floored I didn’t dare admonish him about the sunglasses. Obviously they are doing something right.
>
> Talk about life goals there. Check out those heels and that ankle bracelet! Not that I would bother with such things, but perhaps I would on special occasion.

What a lovely golden years couple.

I would have guessed their ages as 80ish. but they are indeed well preserved.

He wears sunglasses, but that obviously hasn't been detrimental to his longevity. The fancy bracelet he wears is intriguing and I wonder if it is for a purpose. She is wearing a gemstone bracelet.

Of course you can't know their real health status (degenerative diseases like heart disease, arthritis, etc) but at first glance they have an aura of wellness.

Peter Attia, MD has opined that we all will get cancer, heart disease, etc. eventually if we are fortunate to live long enough. He believes the secret to successful aging is to postpone the inevitable diseases of old age out far later in life than the average person.

For example, by time people are in their 80s incidence of degenerative diseases is already rising dramatically for most. But if you can push onset of disease and debility off to around 100 or so you have won the health game of life.

### Phosphene — 2019-11-10T21:43:17+0200

Hipsters, but I still like The Decemberists:

Let's commence to coordinate our sights
Get them square to rights
Get them square to rights
Condescend to calm this riot in your mind
Find yourself in time
Find yourself in time
I am waiting, should I be waiting?
And I am wanting, should I be wanting?
And all around me, all around me
Document the world inside your skin
The tenor of your shins
The timbre of your limbs
Now commence to kick each brick apart
To center on your heart
Starting with your heart right heart
I am waiting, should I be waiting?
And I am wanting, should I be wanting?
And all around me, all around me
All around me, all around me
All around me
I am waiting, should I be waiting?
I am wanting, should I be wanting?
I am hopeful, should I be hopeful, all around me
All around me
It's the sunlight, it's the shadows
It's the quiet, it's the word
It's the beating heart
It's the ocean, it's the boys
It's you, my sweet love
Oh, my love
And the light, bright light
And the light, bright light
Bright light, bright light
It's all around me
It's all around me

### Phosphene — 2019-11-11T14:32:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-282092>

Back at the old homestead. 35F and hazy sun. Getting a bit miffed at my NASA friend who hasn’t returned my call yet.

### Alyce Miles OD — 2019-11-11T19:41:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-282115>

I also have this love/hate relationship with cold! I am the only one in my family who does it. My 8 year old turns the water "cool" at the end of his showers, but I am the one submersing myself outside in a rainwater collected plastic tub, and have eliminated warm/hot showers. I think I have had a dozen hot showers in the last 18 months. In my outside tub sometimes I have to get some water out and boil it as it has been 40 degrees just to warm it up. I have Raynaud's (cold hands/cold feet) and the first time I did cold it hurt so bad. I kept at it and now it doesn't hurt it is just cold. Maybe for winter cold instead of doing 50 degrees one could do 58-60 degrees which is still cold but not bone chilling....

So what do you do? and I gather that you live in Pennsylvania. Me... Memphis. Have an opportunity I am waiting for in Alabama (South) near the FL/AL border about 45 min from Pensacola to get to a higher "quantum yield" as mentioned in many blogs here and to help my SAD and history of depression.

Could also private message here if you want...

### Phosphene — 2019-11-12T14:16:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-282138>

First inch of snow up here today. Mom wanted to leave last night to avoid it.  It’s quite obvious where I got my anxiety tendencies. Not anymore though—cool as a cucumber.

I was mistaken about going all the way to Myrtle Beach today. Stopping somewhere in southern Virginia today—7-8 hours in the car is enough for them.

At least someone sorta fixed the weathervane and bird house.

### LieselK — 2019-11-12T16:09:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-282145>

Enjoy the trip! The weather down here in FL is up and down so I hope you get a good stretch! Yesterday and today are 80's and sunny, but we're going to get cooler and then back to 80's again.

### Phosphene — 2019-11-13T03:06:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphene-%E2%80%94-she-wants-to-do-right.21506/post-282179>

Anything above 40F is warm to me!

I’m having a ball with my parents. Dad is nearly deaf and can be a bit ornery, but he’s on his best joking behavior since I’m along. Mom is the planning engine, and quite a powerhouse, determined to keep her husband alive and happy for as long as possible.

It’s all quite a lesson.

We have CDs and NPR in the car. Today was classical, Johnny Cash, Neil Diamond, and one of my dad’s heros—Willie Nelson.

### Phosphene — 2019-11-15T14:54:47+0200
