### KalosKaiAgathos — 2018-07-17T20:24:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hormesis-autophagy-mitochondrial-health-fasting-melatonin-and-circadian-biology.21509/post-246084>

Posted this one on Jack's dr's page. Jack posted about how mitochondria have their own circadian rhythm as well:

[https://www.facebook.com/drjackkrus...883926075969/2223028911094784/?type=3&theater](https://www.facebook.com/drjackkruse/photos/a.568822376515454.1073741825.239883926075969/2223028911094784/?type=3&theater)

I then posted this study:

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5187924/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5187924/)

(emphasis mine again):
**Melatonin: A Mitochondrial Targeting Molecule Involving Mitochondrial Protection and Dynamics**

**Abstract:**

Melatonin has been speculated to be mainly synthesized by mitochondria. This speculation is supported by the recent discovery that aralkylamine _N_\-acetyltransferase/serotonin _N_\-acetyltransferase (AANAT/SNAT) is localized in mitochondria of oocytes and the isolated mitochondria generate melatonin. We have also speculated that melatonin is a mitochondria-targeted antioxidant. It accumulates in mitochondria with high concentration against a concentration gradient. This is probably achieved by an active transportation via mitochondrial melatonin transporter(s). Melatonin protects mitochondria by scavenging reactive oxygen species (ROS), inhibiting the mitochondrial permeability transition pore (MPTP), and activating uncoupling proteins (UCPs). **Thus, melatonin maintains the optimal mitochondrial membrane potential and preserves mitochondrial functions. In addition, mitochondrial biogenesis and dynamics is also regulated by melatonin. In most cases, melatonin reduces mitochondrial fission and elevates their fusion. Mitochondrial dynamics exhibit an oscillatory pattern which matches the melatonin circadian secretory rhythm in pinealeocytes and probably in other cells. Recently, melatonin has been found to promote mitophagy and improve homeostasis of mitochondria.**

**Conclusion:**
Mitochondria are important organelles. They not only provide the chemical energy to power the cell, but also regulate cellular homeostasis of calcium, apoptosis, and cellular metabolism. Preservation of the structural and functional integrity of mitochondria is essential for a healthy cell. One of the mitochondrial-targeted molecules is melatonin. **Melatonin may be synthesized by mitochondria, a capacity that was inherited from bacteria, the precursors of mitochondria.** **As a result, all cells with mitochondria likely have the capacity to produce melatonin.** This is strongly supported by the observations that the products of AANAT are exclusively located in mitochondria of pinealocytes, the AANAT/SNAT has been identified in the mitochondria of oocytes and the suitable substrate (acetyl CoA) availability for AANAT in mitochondria. In addition, the high level of melatonin is detected in the medium of cultured mitochondria. The protective effects of melatonin on mitochondria depend on its accumulation in these organelles. To achieve this, it requires an active melatonin transport against a concentration gradient. Melatonin mitochondrial carriers have been reported recently, and their levels in mitochondria were positively associated with mitochondrial melatonin concentration. An important protective mechanism of melatonin on mitochondria is that melatonin influences the mitochondrial membrane potential (Δψ). Melatonin blocks MPTP to preserve the Δψ under stressful conditions and activates the UCPs to slightly reduce the Δψ in normal condition. These activities are not in conflict with each other. Blockage of MPTP prevents the Δψ collapse and cellular apoptosis. Activation of UCPs reduces ROS formation because a slight lowering of Δψ accelerates the electron transportation and reduces electron leakage. Activation of UCPs seems not to reduce ATP production as expected. A potential mechanism is that the fewer leaked electrons under the UCP activation contribute their energy to ATP production. A balanced Δψ is ideal for the function of mitochondria. The detailed information as to the mechanisms is summarized in [Figure 4](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5187924/figure/ijms-17-02124-f004/). In addition to mitochondrial protection, melatonin also influences mitochondrial dynamics. The daily oscillations of mitochondrial functions as well as the morphology seem to fit well with the melatonin circadian rhythm. Melatonin reduces mitochondrial fission and increases their fusion, thereby preserving their normal function. Recently, it has been reported that melatonin modified mitophagy by either the enhancement or the reduction of this process, depending on conditions and cell types. The exact mechanisms require further investigation.

### KalosKaiAgathos — 2018-07-17T20:49:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hormesis-autophagy-mitochondrial-health-fasting-melatonin-and-circadian-biology.21509/post-246089>

Another one:

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/28864909](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/28864909)

(emphasis mine):

**Melatonin as a mitochondria-targeted antioxidant: one of evolution's best ideas.**
Melatonin is an ancient antioxidant. After its initial development in bacteria, it has been retained throughout evolution such that it may be or may have been present in every species that have existed. **Even though it has been maintained throughout evolution during the diversification of species, melatonin's chemical structure has never changed; thus, the melatonin present in currently living humans is identical to that present in cyanobacteria that have existed on Earth for billions of years.** Melatonin in the systemic circulation of mammals quickly disappears from the blood presumably due to its uptake by cells, particularly when they are under high oxidative stress conditions. The measurement of the subcellular distribution of melatonin has shown that the concentration of this indole in the mitochondria greatly exceeds that in the blood. Melatonin presumably enters mitochondria through oligopeptide transporters, PEPT1, and PEPT2. Thus, melatonin is specifically targeted to the mitochondria where it seems to function as an apex antioxidant. **In addition to being taken up from the circulation, melatonin may be produced in the mitochondria as well. During evolution, mitochondria likely originated when melatonin-forming bacteria were engulfed as food by ancestral prokaryotes. Over time, engulfed bacteria evolved into mitochondria; this is known as the endosymbiotic theory of the origin of mitochondria. When they did so, the mitochondria retained the ability to synthesize melatonin. Thus, melatonin is not only taken up by mitochondria but these organelles, in addition to many other functions, also probably produce melatonin as well. Melatonin's high concentrations and multiple actions as an antioxidant provide potent antioxidant protection to these organelles which are exposed to abundant free radicals.**

### Jack Kruse — 2018-06-23T16:30:09+0300

I want to say this here not to upset you, but to teach others who will learn from this thread. Your situation really resonates with me because so many people who have come here looking for help and then left too soon without understanding all moving parts to make informed choices and decisions that will move your needle.

People don't like the thought of being an accomplice in their own health issues. It's more agreeable to think that it is something that has occurred by chance and they neglect to be proactive.

Hello Everyone,

I plan on moving to Tucson, Arizona next year. With that being said, I wanted to know if anyone out there knows of the best spots in terms of limited non native emf in Tucson? I'm currently in Mesa (just moved here from Philly trying to get more sun, but now getting more emf) and see that the entire city is bombarded with cellphone towers, usually disguised as palm trees. I have one .10 miles from my apartment that I didn't notice when leasing and another about the same distance on the other side disguised as a flag pole. It's absurd!

Thanks much,
Eric

Okay, cool. Yeah, I'm taking it step by step, Jack! Glad to hear I made an improvement--progress. However, I'm willing to do what it takes to get healthy by surrounding myself within a functionable environment. My mother had been diagnosed with progressive MS in her early twenties (I was 2 at the time) and her mother has had two different types of cancer and a heart attack mainly from smoking. She's still alive and kicking, however. My wife is from a small city in Peru called Arequipa. I lived there for over two years in 80 degree sunshine where I really started to feel the benefits of the sun. It wasn't until listening to your podcast with Adam Lowrey that I really began to understand the why and made an even more conscious effort to follow the protocol. I thought I was doing myself a huge service by coming to Arizona and then finally settling in a small suburb around Tucson. But after being here and feeling it, and then you say this, it appears it won't be.

Getting the place right is the most essential. Then my wife and I can work on our career paths and business endeavors from a sane mental state with enough energy sprouting from our mitochondria. Therefore, I want to reiterate follow up questions regarding living destinations. Tucson is definitely out of the picture, correct? Or is there a way to make it work? How about Destin, Florida? I see there are some members living there and feeling the benefits. I also see some potential for work at least in tourism. What about any places in Peru? If anyone is familiar with there.

Thanks for your time and help, Doc! It's amazing! I love spreading the good word to my fellow working companions, ect. even though they think it's crazy. haha

The thing to consider about Peru is that, despite being densely populated, the cell technology is probably not even 4 G yet. I couldn't say for sure - I have never done any testing outside of the US.

# Thread: Another paper destroying CICO.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-paper-destroying-cico.21514/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2018-06-23T14:54:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-paper-destroying-cico.21514/post-244137>

When you eat the steak tells you more about things than the macro's in a steak.

For GNG enthusiasts:

"After fasting overnight, they ingested a steak (120 g).

(...) fractional gluconeogenesis increased from 68.2 {plus minus} 7.2% before the meal to 75.5 {plus minus} 5.8% 8 h after the meal, the latter corresponding to 22 h without a glucose supply."

[https://www.physiology.org/doi/abs/10.1152/ajpendo.00157.2018](https://www.physiology.org/doi/abs/10.1152/ajpendo.00157.2018)

### Jason Coates — 2018-06-26T13:11:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-paper-destroying-cico.21514/post-244346>

In the postprandial state, glucose homeostasis is challenged by macronutrient intake, including proteins that trigger insulin secretion and provide glucose precursors. However, little is known about the postprandial response of gluconeogenesis to a protein meal. We aimed to quantify the evolution of fractional gluconeogenesis after a meat meal. Thirteen healthy subjects received oral doses of D2O. After fasting overnight, they ingested a steak (120 g). Glycemia, insulinemia and 2H enrichments in glucose and plasma water were measured for 8 h after the meal. Fractional gluconeogenesis was assessed using the average method. Glucose was stable for 5 h and then decreased. There was a slight increase of insulin 1 h after the meal. 2H enrichment in C5 increased after 2 h, whereas it decreased in plasma water. Consequently, fractional gluconeogenesis increased from 68.2 {plus minus} 7.2% before the meal to 75.5 {plus minus} 5.8% 8 h after the meal, the latter corresponding to 22 h without a glucose supply. These values are consistent with the exhaustion of glycogen stores after 24 h, but represents the highest among values in the literature. The impact of methodological conditions is discussed.

### Jack Kruse — 2019-01-26T00:18:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-paper-destroying-cico.21514/post-261386>

If CICO was true.........no way this result is possible: Did you know fast food restaurant meal servings on average have about 30% less calories than meals at full service restaurants - across the globe????

[https://www.bmj.com/content/363/bmj.k4864](https://www.bmj.com/content/363/bmj.k4864)

# Thread: EMFIS - filtering the BS, gaining new understanding
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emfis-filtering-the-bs-gaining-new-understanding.21516/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### James Lech — 2018-06-24T14:31:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emfis-filtering-the-bs-gaining-new-understanding.21516/post-244226>

Born location: Pretoria, South Africa (Lat: 25, South) Alt: 4,400 ft
Current location: Cape Town, South Africa (Lat: 33, South) Alt: sea level
Born 1984, current age: 34.

**Parents**:
Both are still alive, father (75), mother (73). nnEMF is kicking their butts in their environment in their later years. They are Polish immigrants who escaped the communists, I was conceived in South Africa after they arrived.

**Health history**:

1.  Age 2: my mother would give me a ton of fish since I was born, then I recall at age 2 I had a bad reaction and developed a block to the taste or smell of anything fishy made me want to puke.
2.  Starting speaking at the age of 4.
3.  3 or 4 years old, raped by my parents female domestic worker. My parents didn’t know about it until I was 32.
4.  Was an active sporty kid, excelled in sports, but performed poorly in primary school. Was switched to a private school in grade 7, I then began to excel in my subjects except for language, I always had trouble and still do. My brain seems to have a wiring problem.
5.  School conducted an IQ test. My parents were informed, that I basically failed it, even though you can’t fail one technically but my scores were really poor.
6.  Minor cosmetic surgery, was told I went flatline during the anesthesia.

**High School**

1.  Early high school, got our first computer. I developed a reaction to the CRT screen where I would get severe headaches, pain in the eyes.
2.  Visited an eye specialist who said I needed computer glasses (didn’t make a difference). Also said I had scar tissue at the back of my retina, probably from a parasite and that was the cause (later evaluation a decade later, revealed no such evidence).
3.  Had another cosmetic surgery, again was told I went flatline during the anesthesia. Further surgeries to date, no repeat issues.
4.  In math class, when the fluorescent lights would be switched on with overcast weather, I would zone out for 20 mins, time would go on by without knowing what was happening. When it was off, I was sharp as a fiddle and top in the class.
5.  When the weather would change to a low pressure system with overcast and rain, my nnEMF intolerance would be greatly effected.
6.  Borrow my mom’s Nokia 5110 for the first time, in science class it rang, I went into a zone out, my nose started to bleed, the teacher shouting at me to answer the phone but I didn’t register until a classmate shook me.
7.  Graduated with very good grades except for languages, and received sports awards.

**Undergraduate University (2001-2005)**

1.  Became vegetarian and then later Vegan. Went back meat 2 years after finishing university.
2.  Had mercury fillings replaced. Non-biodentist.
3.  Was in a student town. I biked everywhere. I was however, always skinny and would have a leaky gut where I would lose 2-4 kgs within 2 days, therefore, I would always try and eat. This lasted until I got married.
4.  Majors, Computer Science, Geography and Information Systems. Not a great combo a person with my situation but social currency didn’t accept not being in the nnEMF game.
5.  Got first pair of Bluetooth gaming headphones, after 5 min, I got a terrible headache and never used them again.
6.  Was unable to use any of the CRT screens, had a laptop with LCD screen which I used instead.
7.  In my third year, I hit chronic fatigue and was sent to a retreat center for 2 weeks, felt better but no solutions to the problem experienced.
8.  For years, when I would close my eyes hard, and then open, when I blinked I would see coloured spots. I was told it was because of problems with my liver, and that I probably had hepatitis when I was young (never did).
9.  In honours year (final year), I was in a dedicated computer lab. I would have the fluorescent lights in my section covered with paper to diffuse lights. Had an LCD screen, but would zone out in lectures and feel fatigue when they were in rooms with fluorescent lights.
10.  Graduated with top academic grades and national awards. I was smart like the other kids, because I couldn’t keep up, I looked for ways to strategize innovative means.

**First permanent employment**

1.  My nnEMF exposure continued, WiMAX internet, WiFi, Cellphone, etc… but kept pushing myself. Left after a year, went into private business.
2.  Developed inflammatory fat.
3.  I gravitated to work that was more outdoor, but I didn’t address nnEMF exposure, as I was young and wanted to feel important and connected.

**Tipping point trauma**

1.  I had developed a successful skill and business being called South Africa’s Dog Whisperer. I had a radio show, TV appearances and was developing a domestic TV show.
2.  Before that I had registered myself an EHS service dog in court but, due to my full lack of understanding with EHS, he was reacting to be protective with me and therefore I couldn’t use him as anticipated. He naturally reacted to be protective of my space by barking at people when they would come to close to me in certain environments. Only older in age and after investigation, do I realize and understand everything.
3.  While I was training a foster dog to become a diabetic alert dog, the dog grabbed a young girl. Because of me being a public figure, this went viral on the web and front pages of newspapers with details changed by the papers to add more sales. As a result I would get death threats and people parking outside my front gate.
4.  I saw a psychiatrist who said I have PTSD, but I get stipulated I was having exacerbated issues especially around nnEMF but they were dismissed.

**First major hospitalization**

1.  My health continued to decline and in early 2012 I was admitted to hospital. Issues with absent mindedness, having trouble recalling conversations, sharp stabbing pain in a specific part of my brain, blotches of blurred vision etc…
2.  Total time in hospital (4 weeks broken up as follows):
    1.  Specialist physician sent me for every test she could think of including sleep tests.
    2.  I was often questioned for taking steroids or cocaine (because of the symptoms, and kids in my town having such a large problem of cases admitted). I had for neither, also asked about the steroids, because I unusually defined and strong arms muscles like popeye. Answers were no, confirmed by tests.
    3.  Psychiatric medication made no difference to symptoms.
    4.  Cardiologists did a number of tests, include theatre scope. No issues. Performed a TILT test in ICU. Clinical assistance said I had some abnormal spikes during the test, but the cardiologist said on average it was normal.
    5.  Prostate test, normal.
    6.  Testicular exam. My testicals were much larger then the measurement device samples, was referred for a sonar, but everything was clear.
    7.  MRI results summary (still have digital files):
        1.  Temporal lobes, asymmetry is noted regarding hippocampus is present.
        2.  Left hypo campus appears oedematous with feint high signal intensity changes on T2 weighted images noted.
        3.  Surrounding temporal cortex is also involved.
        4.  Head of hippocampus is also enlarged.
        5.  On post-contrast study, no obvious enhancement of the hippocampus is present.
        6.  Prominent sub-arachnoidal spaces are present in the right posterior fossa. This is a normal anatomical variant with normal fluid signal on all the series seen. Conclusions:
        7.  Asymmetry in noted regarding the temporal lobes and the hippocampi.
        8.  The left hippocampus head is oedematous with high signal intensity changes on T2 weighted FLAIR series seen.
        9.  No obvious diffusion restriction or contrast enhancement is noted.
        10.  Post-ictal changes, mesial temporal sclerosis as well as localized encephalitis should be considered.
    8.  Lumbar puncture showed abnormal protein levels.
    9.  Physician reviewed blood results and said levels were below but felt my diagnosis was Lupus, but sent me to another hospital with a specialist Neurologist in MS to check if I had MS.
    10.  Was also sent to City Eye Hospital, everything came back 100% in the clear, I had good vision and no scar tissue in retina.
    11.  At new hospital, Neurologist disagreed with my MRI images and report and said everything was normal but scheduled a follow-up scan 3 months later.
    12.  I spoke about nnEMF issues and gave literature at the time but he dismissed it and said no such thing exists.
    13.  Put me on a 2 week drip for viral encephalitis as a precaution, even though my tests came back negative for the virus.
    14.  3 Months later, I had a follow-up MRI and lumbar puncture. Still showed abnormal protein levels (indicative of compromised BBB). but Neurologist dismissed me.
    15.  Send me for another MRI, I had a stroke during the procedure, (started to feel ill while in the waiting cue outside the machine, where I was weak and was carried in, then during the procedure, lost functions and the medics had to carry me out. My left eye wouldn’t stop tearing for 4 hours.
    16.  Additionally, I was continually yawning constantly.
    17.  Left side of body was unresponsive, I lost the ability to speak. I was told it was psycho schematic and wanted to send me in for another MRI to confirm a stroke, my wife pulled the plug and got me out of there ASAP and back to Cape Town, to find alternative help. The second MRI report by the Neurologist said everything was normal.

**Long road to recovery**

1.  Took 3 weeks before my speech started to come back.
2.  After a couple of months my wife managed to find me another neurologist who is my current one now.
3.  I reviewed literature by Prof. Dominique Belpomme. With it my Neurologist diagnosed me with EMFIS - electromagnetic field intolerance syndrome. I concur with him, I found the word EHS deceiving, a long history of Sweden lost in translation, and the new term was a better medical definition based on his work. Further ratified in my thesis when applying the methodology of scientific-technical analysis and statistical environmental modelling.
4.  I tried to self medicate with a protocol of high dose of methylcobalamin injections (B12) for 8 weeks, thinking it would repair my damaged nervous system.
5.  Home environment was changed to no wifi, no cordless home, wired internet and LAN cable, no energy saving bulbs, but blue light was not addressed.
6.  When in environments of high nnEMF, I would get the following symptoms:
    1.  Wake up with blood from my ear.
    2.  Abscess form on my gums.
    3.  Had 3 dental implants because of gum infections.
    4.  Needed to be wheelchaired because my body would go into shutdown, continually yawning.
    5.  Uncontrollable twitches and shaking of leg or hand.
    6.  Would urinate completely, then take a few steps and there would be a excess trickle down my pants.
    7.  Gait problems.
    8.  Stutter with speak.
    9.  Trouble with concentration and understanding communication.
    10.  Tinnitus.
    11.  Miss-hearing words, i.e. person would say one word and I would completely think they said something else.
    12.  Blurred vision.
    13.  Etc…..
    14.  Had a 6 hour psychiatric evaluation by the State, was found to have no psychiatric condition but evidence of neurological degeneration.
7.  Had developed several benign tumours over my body. Had them removed, not a single regrowth for years until after my second stroke incident.
8.  Was unemployed for years, then in 2016 I was fortunate to have a received a scholarship from the South African Govt. to model the potential effects nnEMF has on the population (LF and RF nnEMF). Was discouraged including blue light.
9.  Scholarship included equipment disability grant in which I was able to purchase voltage transient filters and Aaronia shielding canopy and grounding sheet for sleeping. Low EMF infrared sauna. They helped along with other changes but still not perfect.
10.  I still get swelling pain in my right ankle when my nnEMF intolerance flare up too much.
11.  I was taking 400mg of ibuprohen a day, sometimes 2-3 times a day. When I started drinking Jun Kombucha, my need dropped dramatically.

**Second stroke and dramatic shift in my approach and understanding**

1.  Late, 2017, moved to a new place.
2.  Installed UV EMF shielding on the windows, position and configurations showed green levels on the meter and had external company come and do measurements, all levels were very low.
3.  Had negotiated before had no SMART meter installed. Developer, still had one installed, configuration of levels went off the charts of my meter.
4.  Next morning, there was blood in the toilet bowl.
5.  Went to High Court the next day, to get an order for the SMART meter removed. A week later system was converted.
6.  3 months later, the developer retaliated by laying false criminal charges with the police saying I was running around like a crazy person ripping out HVAC power cables.
7.  Police came to arrest me, wouldn’t review my High Court disability files for EMFIS, or any other documentation, said they had to take me to the station.
8.  At station my condition quickly deteriorated, my body went into shutdown, speech slow and slurred, gums started to bleed, hearing was only functionable on the one side.
9.  I was constantly yawning, and water tears flow from my left eye only.
10.  Detective called the medics (two separate teams came), even though I was stationary, my blood pressure was 168 or 106.
11.  Detective dismissed the charges, as after review, it saw they were malicious. I was then rushed to hospital. The medic said I had a stroke upon admission.
12.  Parts of left left was unresponsive.
13.  For months after my wife said that when talking or typing emails it was like I was conversing in a different language as it was unrecognizable with pieces missing and what I thought was written or said was different to what came out.
14.  Tinnitus got, to the point were I get more irritable because I find it so difficult trying to filter the outside noise to that in my ears, with me being unable to properly understand what is communicated to me. Volume, isn’t the issue, it is the distortion that is.
15.  My one leg when walking would just lose the signal from my brain and collapse from under me. Currently it is much better but has periodically switched to the other leg
16.  I also had an uncontrollable twitch in my index finger but that stopped 3 months after my stroke.

**Current protocol**

1.  The stroke was a downer but also a blessing. The failure had me revisit my neurologist which had learnt more since I saw him years ago. He was more open to the effects of nnEMF and put me onto some literature to read.
2.  I first started with the Wim Hof Method. That showed very promising results but something was missing and I was encountering certain issues.
3.  Then I came across JK and that caused a dramatic turn around in understanding and set me onto the path to change my PhD pursuit to quantum biology (biophysics), in order to better understand my situation and design mitigation options for policy makers.
4.  Had home lights to red or pink. Computer and TV with flux, but found the default setting is total crap and heavily refined it. It is amazing now how I fall asleep around 8pm with the changes. Something never possible before.
5.  Drink spring water, that sits in copper pots overnight on the earth.
6.  Our unit is on the ground floor, mattress on the floor.
7.  Power at night is turned off at the main DB board.
8.  Some electrical cables are disconnected permanently due to bad wiring my developer. We are awaiting to more before the end of the year, but awaiting pro-bono attorney to assist with damages claim after the stroke, as currently unemployed while awaiting next scholarship award.
9.  Wear blue blocking gunnar glasses in day in nnEMF blue light environments and with computer despite flux custom.
10.  Loads of CT, with as much in the ocean. Water temperature is around 50 F.
11.  Since my stroke with the CT, I have followed JK's protocol and test hack, by not doing any exercise, I have gone from 98kg down to 82kg (36 lbs), I have retained muscle (not possible before), and have a 4 or 6 pack depending on the day of the week. Something I have not had since early university days. Although I do appreciate the body benefits, the main dysfuntion in my mitochondria is in my head.
12.  Stop drinking home-made honey kombucha. It helped in the beginning, it worked great for my liver and kidney, but then found the truth bomb from JK that it is a deuterium bomb, and disaster for self-medication with EHS persons. Since I stopped, along with more CT, including sleeping, I am drinking water and urinating a lot more.
13.  This makes sense with the CT in it depleting deuterium.
14.  For years, my wife says at night I am like a furnace, I give off so much heat that she has to get on the other side of the bed. From JK, I understand this as my body producing the heat to shrink my respiratory proteins to help heal me with my disease. When I don’t drink enough water during the night (because I get tired of getting to go pee 2-3 times a night), I awake with a horrible headache. My wife says when I am cold at night, I have a headache in the morning, but with a hot fire effect of my body, I am fine.
15.  With the Kombucha I didn’t need to pee during the night.
16.  I get my morning sun. grounding and have a homemade grounding to a rod in the earth for the computer by my feet, the bed and sauna (this made a big big diff for the sauna hack).
17.  I have switched to eating raw seaweed morning and night. A few weeks ago I forced myself to eat shellfish. Oysters I enjoyed, but cost, more, shrimp was next and now mussels because of the financial challenges at the moment but it all works out. Pictures attached.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

1.  Almost daily now my combined protocol is in the ocean for sunrise, this is CT at the same time, and I harvest mussels. I then steam them at home and eat them with my service puppy, she goes mad for them. With the shell fish, I get this incredible hot heat in my head.
2.  I love the CT as it forces me to take bigger breaths with more oxygen, for my mitochondria.
3.  With my next scholarship I will be able to get a magnetico.

**Bottom line**
I am incredible debt to my failures, very supportive wife, two children, and the resonance I have to JK’s work on the literature and insight, as it enables me to learn with understanding instead of fumbling. Still a very long learning curve, but I am blessed.

### Jason Coates — 2018-07-03T17:13:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emfis-filtering-the-bs-gaining-new-understanding.21516/post-244870>

Oysters are about the only seafood besides squid/calamari that I can stomach. My wife is now buying me salmon and if you freeze it and cut off small frozen pieces and swallow those then you do not get that fishy taste at all. In Johannesburg the cheapest fresh oysters available come in at R12 at my local fishmonger which is still expensive. There are door-to-door oyster deliveries in Johannesburg and I found one in Cape Town for you - sometimes these guys have seasonal specials. [https://oysters-online.shopstar.co.za/products](https://oysters-online.shopstar.co.za/products) R8 is cheap!

### James Lech — 2018-07-03T17:38:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emfis-filtering-the-bs-gaining-new-understanding.21516/post-244876>

Hi Jason

Thank you for the thread response. Any fish will be fine for the DHA but the shellfish have the higher concentrations. Regarding oysters online, I am acutally looking at building a promotion and health network for my show \[ [http://jameslech.co.za/](http://jameslech.co.za/) \] on Niche Radio. You will find it under podcasts. I recently interviewed Jack for it and that will be broadcast in a couple of weeks.

For stomaching the seafood, I recommend a garlic, butter and ginger sauce. It makes a great difference. Let me know if you would like me to give you a walk-through on it. The freezing of the salmon is a good idea with the swallowing. However, I assume you have already done the other environmental hacks to your space? Your CT must be really easy in JHB now with the winter. I was there recently and being in the pool for an hour with the close to zero temperatures was wonderful after my flight.

For the oysters, you don't have to eat a great deal every day, even if you have it a couple of times a week that will make a difference. I recommend rather buying the biggest ones they have instead of the smaller R8, it will be better value for money. You can also get DHA supplementation from the Irene Dairy or other Organic Diary in JHB. The butter and unpasteurized milk helps. It is not as much as the seafood but still helpful.

# Thread: Smart meters vs a phone radiation?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smart-meters-vs-a-phone-radiation.21525/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2018-06-27T15:23:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smart-meters-vs-a-phone-radiation.21525/post-244417>

Smart meters vs a phone radiation is 100 times worse? Here is the link to the research paper published based on a small study of self-reported symptoms after installation of smart meters on homes in Victoria, Australia: [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/25478801](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/25478801)

### OptimalSkr — 2018-06-27T21:17:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smart-meters-vs-a-phone-radiation.21525/post-244454>

Interesting. I just got an RF meter and my cell phone sends the meter off crazy high. But the smart meter outside sits quiet and then sends out a quick burst every few minutes. Plus I can only pick that up from outside the house. [@Scompy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/17718/) I have heard that each smart meter is different with respect to signal strength and time of output - have you found this as well with your tests?

### Eric Waldron — 2018-06-27T21:49:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smart-meters-vs-a-phone-radiation.21525/post-244456>

what rf meter did you purchase?

### OptimalSkr — 2018-06-27T22:23:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smart-meters-vs-a-phone-radiation.21525/post-244459>

Safe living technology's safe and sound rf meter. It's pretty much the same as an acousticom2 meter.

### Scompy — 2018-06-28T03:37:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smart-meters-vs-a-phone-radiation.21525/post-244474>

> [OptimalSkr said:](/goto/post?id=244454)
>
> Interesting. I just got an RF meter and my cell phone sends the meter off crazy high. But the smart meter outside sits quiet and then sends out a quick burst every few minutes. Plus I can only pick that up from outside the house. [@Scompy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/17718/) I have heard that each smart meter is different with respect to signal strength and time of output - have you found this as well with your tests?

Each company and even specific location will setup their smart meters differently. In some neighborhoods, one home on the street will act more like a 'hub' to gather the data, and that home is struck with more RF/Microwave than other homes. But in some systems, they'll blast out certain times of the day periodically. Others are more 24/7. I've see all of these scenarios. When testing anything, we need to do it over the course of several days and weeks to collect the data points to know at what times of day various emissions happen.

### Eric Waldron — 2018-06-28T08:42:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smart-meters-vs-a-phone-radiation.21525/post-244483>

> [OptimalSkr said:](/goto/post?id=244459)
>
> Safe living technology's safe and sound rf meter. It's pretty much the same as an acousticom2 meter.

What about this one? Would you happen to know anything about it?
[https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B01AWRNVA6/ref=ox\_sc\_act\_title\_1?smid=A17YV0793ULSSE&psc=1](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B01AWRNVA6/ref=ox_sc_act_title_1?smid=A17YV0793ULSSE&psc=1)

### Mike David — 2018-06-27T16:28:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oysters.21526/post-244429>

steamed 10 oysters. 2 didn’t open and the grocer doesn’t want anyone getting sick so he says toss em. Should I just boil them longer till they open and eat them or open them myself and eat them or toss em? Does the dha and other positive benefits decrease dramatically when steaming? Is 10 a day a good amount? Are wild better than farmed? Should I buy Chesapeake because their the closest? Are there positive benefits from the salt water juice there in and if so what are they?

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/page-80#post-240559](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/page-80#post-240559)

### JanSz — 2018-06-27T17:19:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oysters.21526/post-244433>

There are two types of oysters.

Eastern oyster (Crassostrea virginica)
Invasive Oyster Crassostrea gigas

We usually see the Eastern type.

At my Asian supermarket NJ 07054 I can get both types.
The Gigas are from Pacific but in the previous post you can see analysis of Gigas from France, latter that analysis is referenced and used for evaluation of Eastern types.

Fresh meat from Gigas I can get in one pound containers, $10/pound

That amount of meat from Eastern oysters would cost $100/pound and up.

....
Per the analysis
I concluded that oysters are the only seafood that I know of that have less EPA and more DHA.

////////////

In stores or restaurants, further classifications are often made, by place of origins.
But eventually all of them are small (Eastern Oysters), that is small in physical size, when comparing to gigas.

////

Toss them, the ones that did not open after cooked. Not a good sign.
You can eat them, but be prepaired to get sick in your belly . Might happen might not

> Does the dha and other positive benefits decrease dramatically when steaming? ?

Raw fresh oysters are the best IMO. Of course . You get all the viruses and bacteria too. Raw seafood has great benefits! But steamed are good too of course.. I only eat mine raw, I did not find them as delicious cooked.

> Is 10 a day a good amount? Are wild better than farmed? ?

I think 10 is a great amount daily, about what I eat on average too. I guess farmed are as great as wild ones, not any big difference there. If you pick yourself, be careful to not pick in polluted waters.

> Should I buy Chesapeake because their the closest? ?

IDK

> Are there positive benefits from the salt water juice there in and if so what are they?

Sure! The water is awesome! The salt in it.... and I bet there were other thing that I cant remember - I always drink the water too.

Somewhere around 94% of every dinner for the last year my wife has eaten 9-12 raw oysters. I've had way more oysters than ever but she insists them regularly. She's never gotten ill from them.

The funny things about being middle aged and in reasonable health in the first world is that it helps one realize that pulling through a little food poisoning or stomach bug eventually comes with the benefit of effortless weight loss. The last time my wife had a stomach bug she finished by saying "Hey look! I lost 4 pounds!" I'm not sure you're supposed to do that in one go, but hey, she seemed pretty happy.

# Thread: 21 y/o newbie from Canada
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/21-y-o-newbie-from-canada.21527/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Ossa — 2018-06-27T23:48:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/21-y-o-newbie-from-canada.21527/post-244466>

Hows it going everyone, Im Alex. Just wanted to introduce myself and tell my story.

The path that lead me here all started back in highschool when i started to get into health and fitness. I grew up an overweight kid and had a friend that was always in amazing shape. So one day when i was around 15 he started taking me on long distance runs. 3km to start which eventually turned into 10km. This lasted for a few months over that summer and i lost a little bit of weight. Eventually i started getting into weight lifting and this is where the problems started happening. I got into it seriously and not knowing really what i was doing decided to push myself as hard as i could because i was told and thought this was how you got results. I would take excersized sush as pushups, pullups, squats, all the way to complete failure for multiple sets and i did this for about a year until i completely collapsed from exhaustion. What started happening over time was that i became more and more exhausted. Started having to take multiple hour naps everysingle day. My libido completely vanished and i became really depressed. I stopped when i was about 18 and recovered a little bit.

Eventually i started learning about calories and the diet side to fitness and how to workout properly but unfortunately still had to take it easy because of how exhausted i was. I was able to squeeze through highschool barely. Ive always been a very curious person so eventually started learning about intermittent fasting and circadian rhythms. Started listening to scientists such as Dr. Satchin Panda which eventually led me here. Dr . Kruse’s work makes more sense to me by far than anything else ive ever stumbled upon.

One thing i havent mentioned yet is that my whole life i have had the terrible habit of staying up on computers/ tvs playing video games up until 12:30-1am for my whole life up until recently. I think this explains the reason i have had mental health issues my whole life, most came in the form of mainly severe anxiety and suicidal thoughts. Ive been on three different medications over the years and had huge struggle getting off all of them. Ive struggled with lots of daytime sleepiness and fatigue for as long as i can remember. Since discovering Dr. Kruse i have made huge changes to my lifestyle such as; lights out at sunset, wearing blue blockers at night and while looking at electronics, started the leptin rx (as i have suffered from binge eating disorders), and getting sun everymorning as well as grounding. Ive always had the problem of not feeling sleepy at all at night and ive started to notice small improvements. I understand it will take a while to get well again.

Ive went to my family doctor a few times in the past to try and get help for my fatigue problmes and all my lab results came back fine except for the fact that i was on the very low end for normal testosterone levels.

Im hoping by applying all that i learn here and from Dr. Kruse i will finally be able to feel and be healhy once and for all. Thanks for reading. ~ Alex

Two things stand out to me: 1) You are so fortunate to have come across Dr. Kruse's work earlier in life, and most importantly 2) You are smart and intuitive enough to see the value in it.

Keep up the great work!

# Thread: My Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.21529/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 264
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### KrusinWitchie — 2018-06-28T17:06:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.21529/post-244503>

I am beginning my journal with the intent of record keeping so that as I read more of the blogs (and the webinars one day when I can afford them) I can go back in time and make connections. There are many anecdotes from my past that have become relevant to me again when applying the context of the knowledge Jack presents us with.

For instance, when I was around 12 I partially and completely tore some tendons and ligaments in my left elbow while performing in a cheerleading competition at the Mohegan Sun casino. The injury happened doing something I had done maybe 500 times already (a roundoff backhandspring), however I had never done it in Mohegan Sun Arena. Prior to that the only competitions I had performed in were in school gymnasiums, and had never injured myself like that. I enjoyed making that connection.

I’m on page 10 of Inger’s journal and I am blown away. I am inspired by her openness and the support she extends towards others and that others reach out to offer her in return. It is a beautiful thing and I am learning so much. I feel like I am reading a book! It will be hard put my phone down and to stick to reading the blogs chronologically when I have such a page turner at my fingertips. Maybe I can print Inger’s journal too...

Inger, thank you. Also, thank you Jack. You have both given me courage in seemingly small ways. Inger, by having the courage to share in your journal that you have drank your urine and put it on your face, I have no shame in saying I’ve been using my own urine, too! Since February of this year I began using my fresh urine as a toner first thing in the morning. It has made my previously clogged and dry problem skin very radiant. If I do not use it everyday, I start noticing more “plugs” on the skin on my nose. A week ago I also began using a homemade grass fed tallow/aloe/honey cream all over afterwards and now I feel like a moisturized goddess. Years ago, I always knew that I could be beautiful underneath my painful cystic acne and eczema ridden skin, however I had no idea what that beauty would look or feel like. Now I know! Also, yesterday I experimented with using my urine to cleanse my hair and scalp. I did not rinse it out. I let it air dry and asked my fiancé to smell my head and give me his impressions. He did not know I used the golden goodness and told me my hair smelled like an herbal flower garden!!!! I was floored! He likes to joke around a bit so I had to ask him about ten times to make sure he was serious.. he was. My hair dried very curly and shiny.

And Jack, thank you for wearing outrageous purple suits and bright shirts and the like. I am settling into a comfortable place of self expression. I reflect how I see myself, not how others see me. Having been bulimic, this is huge for me. I have some wild thrifted pants that I keep meaning to tailor...

I also have found the courage to tell my mother than I am declining her invitation to travel to Barcelona this summer. She is working there, and I’d be flying from New Hampshire to Spain. Pre-Kruse, I agreed even though my heart sunk just imagining the travel burden the moment she asked me to come. Last year I traveled overseas for the first time to London and had a horrible eczema outbreak. I don’t feel it is right to harm myself to make others happy. Especially when I am working so hard to get my mitochondria happy! I feel so much better after informing my family that I would not be traveling with them.

The other day I was wading through the lake with my lil’ scrappy dog when I saw a small dragonfly struggling to stay afloat. I picked him up, gave him some time to get his wings dry, and he stayed on my hand for close to an hour! I also saw a small bird sing his song, he put his whole body into it! I have never seen that before. On the lake are two loons who have been hanging out close by our cottage. My mother said that last year they did not come close by at all. All spring they have been making appearances! Yesterday we saw them diving for fish in the shallow part of the water... they are SO fast. It is raining now and I just ran out into the lake because I felt like it. Minutes after I returned inside, who shows up in the exact spot that I was in but the loon!

I am inspired to know that this spring has been so eventful for myself and my little family. The changes we have welcomed into our lives came at the perfect time. I am thankful for the connection I have with the earth that has invited all of these changes during a season of transition:

\-I found Dr.Kruse in April as the ice was melting... one week prior I intuitively sat in the snow looking towards the sun with the intent of kicking the lingering cold I had to the curb. It worked!
\-My fiancé all of the sudden looks like some sculpture. He has reminded me of the gay figure drawing teacher that I had many years ago who would get so excited when we had a chiseled male model. All those lines and contours! He gets bashful when I oogle over him and I find that endearing.
\-We decided to go for the juggler and make moves towards gaining physical custody of my fiancé’s 6y/o daughter. Where she lives is getting 5G this fall, she is sometimes fed rice and ketchup 3x/day and does not have reading time with her mother who keeps her away from her own father.
\-We moved from a 5G area to rural New Hampshire before we even knew what 5G was.
\-we both made great strides in learning to love our families from afar.
\-huge changes in my fiancé’s job that most of his peers are anxious about but that he is more than well equipped to adapt to.

Overall, I am grateful. I am also really humbled by how informed most people on the forums are. I have a ways to go and am excited for the ride!

### KrusinWitchie — 2018-06-29T21:34:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.21529/post-244582>

> [Phosphene said:](/goto/post?id=244580)
>
> Hi Hannah! I’m pretty sure you’ve found your coven.

I think you are right, Phosphene!

This morning I finally made lamb heart carpaccio, thanks to this forum! Mine and my fiancé’s tongues were wagging! My dad was over and i gave him a bite too, he loved it! I used lime juice, ginger, Celtic salt, some balsamic vinegar, red onion and parsley, black pepper and EVOO. He knew I was going to make something with the lamb heart, but he had no idea it was going to be as good as it was. I’m so glad I had no problems eating it. I think that preparing the food and handling it directly helps with being able to eat new and unusual things. Two years ago he cooked me liver and onions and I was a big baby about eating it. Look at me now! The lamb hearts were previously frozen so now I am keen on finding an optimal source for raw organ meats before I start raising my own.

[@Inger](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1057/) I was planning on getting Nigerian Dwarf goats as soon as my little farm is ready for them, but your journal has changed my mind. You paint such an idyllic picture with you and your cuties. My “lamby” stuffed animal was given to me when I was born and I had it for 25 years until last year when I accidentally threw it out. Time to get a real one! I think that before I do get goats, if I do, I will get sheep. I have 15 acres of pasture. I would want them for milk and also meat. Have you ever had fresh sheep milk? I have not, and am curious about the taste.

I haven’t moved into the farm yet, but here is my plan. I want to start ASAP with farming Black Soldier Fly larvae. They eat anything that you can compost, and their “compost tea” deters other insects from colonizing the area it’s applied to. They are loaded with omega-3’s and protein. I have a complete fatty acid analysis for them that I think might tickle @Jansz’ fancy. I will be feeding that to my ducks and my dog if she’ll eat them. I think she will because she has been known to kill and eat 5 horseflies at a time. I want ducks for meat and eggs, a rabbit colony (on ground) to feed my dog and my family, followed by the sheep.

A few weeks ago I gave my family a heart attack when I told them I ate two mussels from the lake we live on. My fiancé had 1. It is one of the cleanest lakes in New Hampshire. They told me that I’m not supposed to eat freshwater shellfish. A little salt sprinkled on top and it was just as tasty as anything store bought I’ve had. That night my fiancé and I ended up on another planet in bed. Although they did scare me to the point I haven’t had another since. I checked the New Hampshire fish and game website and they said that there are no additional risks eating freshwater shellfish, just to be sure the water is clean as always. This weekend I’ll wade back out and grab a few more to freak them out during July 4th festivities.

### Phosphene — 2018-06-29T23:51:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.21529/post-244587>

Hannah, absolutely everything about your journal is making me smile. It’s so encouraging to see younger folks finding this site and taking it to heart. I still consider myself a newbie even though I’ve been following quite awhile—have many bad habits to undo and relearn to right my ship. I also dove in here by obsessively reading [@Inger](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1057/) ’s journal. Get ready for a wild and wonderful ride there. I’ll bet she could seriously turn that into a best-selling memoir...

I’d love to hear more about your cottage on the lake. In college I took a trip to such a lake in New Hampshire with the man who became my husband (and still is, just barely). The simple, rustic cabin belonged to the family of a friend we went with, and we all had such a fabulous time—seven of us. The memories are still so vivid. It was off the grid—small gas stove, outhouse, kerosene lanterns (which did give headaches but we were certainly up too late).

I also remember gathering freshwater mussels from the lake and being so excited to make something with them. Of course we were all inexperienced cooks then with very limited supplies. The dish involved potatoes and eggs and was probably supposed to be some sort of frittata. It turned out nasty (was dubbed Potato Nasty) but we were hungry and funds were limited so it was consumed anyway. With way too much cheap beer.

What did we do way back then without TV and internet and phones? Oh yeah—we played games—poker, gin rummy, scrabble, charades. And hiking (unfortunatly doused in DEET for the mobs of mosquitos. Maybe that’s what the headaches were from?) And hours out on the canoes and swimming in the clear, frigid water, then sunbaking on the shore. And creative cooking. And books. And music. And some hard labor—we were tasked with clearing a fallen tree in exchange for the stay.

I don’t recall any astral travel in bed, but there were probably a few Earthly excursions.

Oh yeah....

Thanks for the stroll down memory lane! Who knew optimal was in reach way back then? Don’t remember the name of the lake—I’m sure there are hundreds. I’ll find out.

### KrusinWitchie — 2018-07-03T14:11:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.21529/post-244841>

[@Phosphene](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19648/) that sounds like a precious memory! As I was outside by the lake this past weekend I found myself wondering what might be different about the lake now versus a few decades ago. Definitely more sunscreen floating on the surface in its oily, rainbow reflecting fashion from all the gringos. It’s called Kolelemook Lake. All winter there was barely a soul here, save for the occasional ice fisher on Saturday mornings. This past weekend it was in the 90s and crowded enough that I felt like I needed to “get away”. I am not the biggest fan of fireworks, and there were TONS going off. I wonder how that affected the fishies, and me too!

I won’t be asking for your Potato Nasty recipe anytime soon. I am not so daring to attempt anything other than steaming them and then down the hatch with salt and lime. Apparently, though, there are a lot of endangered freshwater mussel species so I’ll have to drive to the shore pick fresh shellfish. Haven’t been yet, so I’m looking forward to that.

There’s no cable internet service here, so we have a little “mifi” device from verizon that is off most of the time. Usually I have 1-2 bars of LTE service and sometimes only 3G. I have been wondering if it’s worse that there’s poor service because my phone works harder to find a connection. Now that there’s more people here, there’s more WiFi signals too. All of the nnEMF business is new to me, but yesterday I turned off the breakers here for the first time and it was great! I am sure a few decades ago there was less “buzz”, more quiet. I used to feel I needed that white noise of the “buzz”. The dishwasher running at night used to be like a lullabye to me, and now I don’t even want to be in the house when it’s on. The fridge gives me anxiety lol. It’s worse that all of the appliances my parents put into the house are unnecessarily huge. Even the sink has a motion sensor. They like to keep up with the Joneses in a big way.

We have red lights in the cottage, much to my mothers horror that the neighbors will think it’s a “whorehouse”. Usually my fiancé and I just talk and are in bed by 10. We have the most beautiful discussions, disagreements, and therapy sessions. I am grateful that we do talk so much because he talks all day at work (he’s a manager for Whole Foods). We also bought a library membership from Dartmouth College and have been getting good use of it so far. My favorite books are the ones that find me in the library stacks.

Yesterday I was misbehaving and jumping around on the blogs again instead of reading the ones I have printed. Ran into the importance of oxy 2x/day and shared that with my fiancé. As if he needed any more encouragement  I do get a little scared of the unknown now and then in regards to my health. Less so since I’ve found this site, and will be close to zero when I can afford to be a member and have a consult with labs and everything. But what keeps my worries at bay is that we are optimally in cycle with the moon (ovulate during full moon) and are both getting lots of oxy.

### KrusinWitchie — 2018-07-03T16:12:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.21529/post-244856>

I used to “lay out” every summer in high school. I am very pale without the sun, almost translucent. This was in the mid 2000’s and it wasn’t cool then to wear sunscreen or care about the health of your liver or your thoughts. I didn’t know what anxiety or depression were but I knew I was some sort of fucked up. What kept me alive was the sun, I think. Getting some sort of color was my job during the summers, and as I was hungover most of the time it was really all I could muster. I was deep in an ever evolving eating disorder that left me cranky and insecure. Fast forward to two years after high school when I had dropped out of college and returned home. I began working at Whole Foods to pass the time as all my friends were still out of state and in school. I somehow got to thinking it would be a great idea to get really, really pale andminimize the appearance of any freckles. Oh, and to become vegan. I stayed out of the sun for 5 years. That first year of sun avoidance, I got a MRSA infection on my leg and mononucleosis that wiped me out for weeks.

I was raised to be jealous of my siblings when they were sick. I wanted to be sick so that I could get some attention from my working, BlackBerry-poweredmother. I was excited to get hip surgeries in high school, and got a lot of attention for it. Eventually, I clung to being sick as part of my identity because I had no idea what it was like to not be sick. I couldn’t imagine being me without all of the layers I had caked on top of myself. I think that there are a lot of people who will not accept improvements in health because it will consequently mean that there is less “stuff” todistract them from the fact that they don’t know who they are.

As an adult, real nurturing is something that you have to do for yourself with the help of Mother Nature. Once I let go of wanting someone else to regulate my state of health and well-being, I wasn’t sick anymore. I was still lost as fuck in the world and not quite okay with it, but that was a process and at this point I am okay with it. And I never truly feel lost because I am so connected to the earth and everyone else that is also connected.

I also find comfort in knowing that “who I am” can and does change everyday.

### KrusinWitchie — 2018-07-04T18:49:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.21529/post-244964>

My fiancé is such a great experiment subject. We have been together for 5 years. I know his body like I know my body. Our bodies talk.

4 years ago he pulled a muscle in his back doing pull ups. At the time we were living in the Bronx and were both vegan. Needless to say redox was a bit slow. It took him over two weeks to recover and it was painful to watch as he likes and needs to be very active at work.

This past weekend he was pulling the paddle boat up onto the shore from the lake. It was a lot heavier than we realized and I should have helped him. He pulled it using a really wide and weird grip and.. \*bop\* pulled a muscle in his upper back/shoulder. That was on Saturday. Headache, eye pain and neck pain ensued but we didn’t associate it with the boat until Monday.

Monday he was uncomfortable and was worst during his drive home. That afternoon I gave him a massage with magnesium, frankincense and geranium and iced it combined with sun exposure. I never really know what the heck I’m doing but ended up doing this. We haven’t done any CT besides air exposure in late winter. He got cold urticaria on his neck for about 20 minutes and had a runny nose for 10 minutes after it was iced.

Monday night he accidentally kicks our scrappy terrier in the dark and she growls at him. He reaches down (big mistake) and she bites him. I wash it immediately and put iodine on it. Tuesday morning the wound has already closed.

Tuesday he was still in pain, and was getting chills. His temperature was 101 something. Now I’m worried the dog bite is gonna mess him up in a bad way. I gave him around 2tsp Celtic salt in water and put heat on his shoulder. He fell asleep immediately which was nice because Sunday and Monday night he had tossed and turned and didn’t sleep well. He got up at 1 and I had him gargle with baking soda and take a sip of Lugols in water. He woke up today (weds) and had so much less pain and was able to sleep through the night. No fever this morning. Only thing is he still has eye strain and has slight pain in his neck/upper back but has full range of motion.

I had no idea what I was doing as I took care of him. But it is good to know that there is a chance that what I intuitively decided to do helped. But i know it was mostly his own body and the improved redox he has experienced since April of this year when we began the epi-paleo diet and modified our light exposure. It is night and day compared to 4 years ago. I also find it interesting that the healing intuition kinda kicked in after I had written the above post yesterday morning.

I also visited my mom yesterday afternoon to discover that she had a stabbing pain in her lower back, supposedly because she was straddling a stool and “people shouldn’t sit like that”. The post above was really written for me to come to terms with my mother’s resistance to self-nurture and nurturing others. I told her to take a bath with epsom salt and baking soda. She was limping/waddling around and afterwards was just waddling and in a brighter mood.

My mom suggested that I am blindly following another sensational fad or figure with the whole Kruse thing. What is different about this is that I am following ME. I am learning my body’s own response to my environment with the support of some serious scientific backup from Kruse. And it all intuitively makes sense to me on a birds eye level, even if I cannot understand yet the quantum cellular mechanics of everything I’m reading. Mother Nature, man. It feels so good to have confidence in my decisions despite internal and external sources of doubt.

As far as mosquitos go it works like a charm - it makes you smell a little like creosote but not bad.

### KrusinWitchie — 2018-08-17T06:11:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.21529/post-248663>

Well I’m not a member yet, so I haven’t tapped into webinars/podcasts at all (besides seeing Jack at VT 2018 ). That being said I wanted my journal to start out just as a way for me to keep track of myself, and not ask lots of Q’s and whatnot. I find it kind of overwhelming how damn helpful so many of you are in your communication with others on the forums. This is a cool place.

I didn’t want to ask any questions before I’ve soaked up all the info...

BUT..

I am four days late and have suspected something was amiss weeks ago. If I am pregnant I’m almost 5 weeks. My mind has been all over the place. I’m 26, in a relationship where kids were in our future anyways, but my concerns are about the health of myself and this possible brewing babe.

I don’t have insurance (will get married soon to get on future husband’s insurance, swoon). I need to get a blood hCG test to confirm pregnancy but what other test should I get that zeroes in on what I need? What should I do, like, NOW to react to this possibility? I know that’s a loaded question entirely dependent upon my environment. I impulse bought pastured chicken liver today but don’t know what to do with it.

\-Urine tests are negative. I’m super regular and for the past three years begin my cycle within three days of the new moon. I like to think that means Mother Nature looks out for me.
\-Left 5g city in December of this year, now live in the boonies of NH. Rarely spend time in shitty emf environments (2x/ month) but was blue light toxic 6 months ago. Of course idk what exactly my environments emf is now but we got rid of our WiFi jet pack device because it sucked and verizon said it was cause we are in the boonies surrounded by trees and a lake.
\-future husband commutes 3hr/day poor guy into MA 35 mi outside of Boston at a retail supermarket. This is what is stressing me out the most. He’s waiting to transfer to NH.
\-I eat lots of cooked mussels, bacon, grass fed butter oil, pastured lamb heart, grass fed tallow, shrimp, fish, canned sardines and canned oysters. This began in April of this year. Doing BAB and eating again before sundown. Before then diet was pretty much fish heavy Autoimmune Paleo (eczema) but with Taco Bell hiccups here and there. For a two month period last fall I ate Taco Bell and jack in the box nearly everyday while I worked 3am-11am full-time in Dallas. But I also ate sardines by the case. This is why I wanted to wait a bit more before having babies.
\-no significant CT strides made yet
\-no artificial light, wear blue blockers pm, phones always red, I get lots of sun (NH sun) and don’t burn anymore(started in April). I get morning sun consistently, nude sometimes.
\-smoke marijuana out of a bowl (small pipe) pretty much daily for the past 5 years. Stopped this past week thanks to future husband, as I was trying to convince myself that it was okay.
\-weirdly stopped caffeine (green tea) the week before the possible conception. I have a very addictive personality. I can’t even have coffee in the house or I’ll drink it. I love coffee.

Can I blame kruse for all of this? The day before the full moon (ovulation time) we got a heat lamp and hooked it up to the bed (before reading much later that it should only be used with UV and while grounded). Fun times that night except I don’t remember falling asleep or what even happened before then. Control and memory were lost on us. No drinking involved. Infrared FTW. Apparently we both were very affected by the red light, I don’t know why. It was a few days before he reminded me what happened because he thought I knew since he had told me right then and there. That’s how it happened.

Hopefully this is a wash and I don’t need to fret. My goodness, thank you for reading all that and thank you for your time! I know how valuable it is.

Thank you  I feel more at ease.

### KrusinWitchie — 2018-08-19T16:41:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.21529/post-248802>

In my first post on this forum, I detailed my experiences while overnight dog sitting a dying dog at la case del jefe de my future husband. I like the word jefe. I don’t like that house. (Post since deleted because I had posted that under an account using my real name as my name which I don’t know why exactly that bothers me but it did. I can repost it sometime.)

I was freaked out enough by the experience that I realized the depth and significance of what I had only just begun to see.

Tottering between both worlds is more taxing than doing the grueling work of submitting to the fact that the reality proves there are just bad places and bad people that you don’t want to mess with and therefore must always work around.

I say grueling because it sounds visceral like you’re pulling an object out of a puncture wound. Or like CT. And with everything I learn here, I continually pull myself away from a comfortable reality that I must still wish was warm, fuzzy and true. I wish that I didn’t have to feel that, and that there was no threat of something endangering my environment and well-being. (Insert quote about how wishes ain’t ish.) None of that is as grueling as admitting that you knowingly put yourself in a damaging environment. That’s heavy and is what I must be diligent about avoiding moving forward.

The depth of the negativity I can sense in my environment is clear again. I find myself this morning in that same house. I can’t declare causal links to any of this because I don’t have a meter. Last time we stayed over a week taking care of a senior dog on tons of antidepressants that was a sheer horror energetically to be around unless we were outside. Outside she would dig a hole and lay in it. Me, her, and my own dog just sat outside all day mostly for that week and I still developed an espresso addiction and a hemorrhoid.

The dog since was put down. This time house sitting consists of plant watering if it hasn’t rained. I stayed just one night with future husband so I could visit his store and go the Asian supermarket. We set up the tent outside with intentions of sleeping there. Rookie mistake leading to wet tent. So we slept on the couch. I had shitty sleep, and dreamt that I knew I wasn’t pregnant despite future husbands excitement over a positive test. Woke up to what seems like a beginning of a cycle and mucus in the back of my throat like last time. I can’t explain that but I know I just can’t wait for him to get out of work so we can go home. Only we’re going straight to Sunday dinner with my racist grandmother and shitty uncle, both grossly outspoken and about to meet my brown partner for the first time. I usually avoid them if I can help it.

Positives: 3lb box of dried anchovies for 25$ and lesson learned. I do still worry about future husband working here though. Not more than a month ago, his store was upgraded for data speed something or another, I’ll have to ask what exactly they did. But I’m sure as hell it had something to do with 5G in Boston. Around the same time future husbands chiropractor confirmed a calcified lymph node under his right ear. We need cold, dark energy. We are excited for winter to dive into CT, alone together, in the woods.

In these moments I tell myself that I can enjoy life on earth. I breathe in the suffering of those who have more obstacles than I do and breathe out peace to them.

### KrusinWitchie — 2018-08-23T16:51:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.21529/post-249194>

First post on forum (April ‘18)

Hi All,

I’ve read for hours and hours on the blog and on the forum and still feel like a complete noob, but the beauty of forever being a noob is that you never cease to throw yourself into new situations and experiences to level up into a new noob level.

I’m 26, female, pale and freckly, and live in New Hampshire, US. It’s barely spring here but I did get pink the other day laying out in 58F!

Family History:

Both grandfathers were in the Navy, died in their 90s. Maternal- respiratory disease, paternal I’m not sure. Both had skin cancer.

Maternal grandmother has rheumatoid arthritis

Paternal grandmother had dementia

Father has dementia and is pre diabetic and obese, has skin cancer.

Mother has high inflammation, is obese, teeth falling out but “has no time for health until she retires.” Narcissistic tendencies. Currently she’s living in Barcelona and travels to NH frequently.

Medical History:

Antibiotic use since childhood to treat staph

Steroid asthma inhalers in childhood

Heavy antihistamine and nasal decongestant use from childhood through late teens due to seasonal and pet allergies

Eating disorder (bulimia and purging type AN) from early teens through 22

Depression, Depersonalisation, social anxiety and suicidal from early teens through early 20s

Tendon and ligament tears in elbow from sports injury at 14

Arthroscopic hip surgeries (r+l) at 15 and 16 with general anesthesia for 7 hours each time

Mononucleosis (ebv) at 19

MRSA at 19

Binge drinker from 15-24

Acne, eczema, rosacea

Digestive symptoms (undigested food in stool, extreme bloating, pain)

Thankfully, most of this is behind me at 26 years old. At 20 I became vegan to try and “be healthy.” Lasted 3 years and finally realized it wasn’t the answer to my health woes. Ever since then I have been obsessed with questioning nearly everything I’ve been led to believe about health and disease. After stopping vegan, I just started being conscious about where my meat was coming from and making sure it was treated well to be less inflammatory. Cut out wheat. Lots of different seeds, veggies and nuts are undigested in stool so I avoid them.

Now i’ve been eating epi-paleo for the past four weeks since finding this community and my poops are fabulous so I know Dr. Kruse is for real.

My “skin in the game” realizations:

Even after a week and a half, sex w/ BF was markedly improved. Quantifiably improved. (Oh yeah, I’ve got him eating epi-paleo too)

We live currently in a non-WiFi lake house with a metal roof in the boonies of NH, and have been mindful of light cycles and emf exposure (although I have been glued to my phone reading the blogs, forums etc). Last week, we stayed at BF’s boss’ house while they were on vacation to watch their dog and for BF to benefit from a shorter commute. Holy shh. The place is maybe 1,000 sq ft and it is lit the f up with electronics. I noticed it when we arrived but figured we’d be fine. We still ate our epi-paleo diet for the first few days. By day 3 I was insatiable and had to rummage through their cupboard and binged on oatmeal bars, popsicles and espresso. Not to mention the senior dog paces at night so we barely slept. By day 5 I DEVELOPED A HEMORRHOID. I was horrified. Never happened to me in my life. This happened even though I spent five hours on day four and five outside grounded in the sun with the dog to try to help her symptoms. Mind you, the dog drinks an ungodly amount of water, and BF noticed too he was increasingly thirsty. The BF remarked he is more rested after sleeping 5 hours and commuting 3 hours/day from the lakehouse than when he commuted 20 minutes a day at the “Lit” house and attempted to sleep for 9 hours. So, yeah. Environment is very important, just ask my butt. He is feeling and looking tons better since we’re back in the lakehouse. I am still nurturing my butt.

Have been cold air adapting for the past four weeks. Haven’t had the heat on inside at all. People come over and say I’m crazy but I don’t feel it. My whole life I’ve always been shivering even when it’s warm, so this is exciting to me. Face dunks feel like I’m drowning for some reason but I aim to be more consistent with them. Once the lake isn’t frozen I will do CT in it, assuming it isn’t too cold when it first melts.

I get a heavy feeling when we use GPS navigation in the car now. Never noticed before. Getting a road map ASAP.

We’re fixing up an old farmhouse. What a humbling experience to realize that I can’t just agree to everything my dad says re materials because “this isn’t his first time at the rodeo”. Got secondhand cabinets that’ve already offgassed, using shiplap instead of drywall throughout the house and am saying no to interior plywood. I’m not too techie so I am struggling to understand what we can do re internet and wiring. The walls are exposed now so it’s high time for me to get a grip on this.

I hope to be active on the forums once I absorb some more nuggets from the blog posts. Nice to meet you all. I feel like I already know many of you since I’ve read so many of your enlightening words. But all I know is that I know nothing.

Take it easy,

Hannah
