### KrusinWitchie — 2019-02-22T13:44:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.21529/post-263791>

FH’s employer of now 10 years just sent out an email to all managers detailing protocols for handling employees who come into work high. They’re not just smoking weed. The same day they sent out the email, FH’s store had to call the police to pick up one of their employees who was acting erratically. I listened in on the recap of this situation and my jaw was on the floor the entire time. He wouldn’t leave with his grandma who came to Pick him up because he didn’t trust her, so when given the choice of leave with Grandma or leave with the police, he chose the police. The manager who was telling this to FH made note of the “dead look in his eyes”.

He was also caught the other day throwing away hundreds of dollars of perfectly good seabass. That had my eyes bulging more than the circumstances of his separation.

Just kidding. What frightened me the most was that this kid said that FH was “scary”. Young 5g opiate addicted dopamine depleted Zombies and fear of black men don’t sit well with me.

I don’t think this will be the last young kid being separated for this. I wouldn’t be surprised if the company starts storing Narcan at the stores.

[https://www.google.com/amp/s/www.wl...r-flu-diagnosis-school-district-says/26437756](https://www.google.com/amp/s/www.wlwt.com/amp/article/mason-fourth-grader-dies-day-after-flu-diagnosis-school-district-says/26437756)

Greater Cincinnati 5g death.

[https://www.yahoo.com/gma/bus-drive...ed-heroin-104205389--abc-news-topstories.html](https://www.yahoo.com/gma/bus-driver-arrested-she-needed-narcan-revived-heroin-104205389--abc-news-topstories.html)

NJ bus driver ODs and crashes school bus carrying 12 special needs children.

### KrusinWitchie — 2019-02-22T14:06:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.21529/post-263795>

In other news, for the first time since early elementary school, I actually have ankles vs cankles and I can see my kneecap without stretching my skin over it. And the cellulite that I’ve carried with me on the backs of my thighs since middle school is long gone.

### Phosphene — 2019-04-06T06:49:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.21529/post-267455>

How are you doing my dear Witchie?

Do tell.

### KrusinWitchie — 2019-04-07T18:46:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.21529/post-267549>

> [Phosphene said:](/goto/post?id=267455)
>
> How are you doing my dear Witchie?
>
> Do tell.

Thank you for asking! Swell is the answer. Family law attorney fees have postponed our travel to Mexico. Like you said to me last year, things are happening as they should. We could not be more pleased with the outcome and with our representation.

We were in Kissimmee in March for a week getting sun, visiting the little ones and handling some court stuff. The kids and I spent our days outside. They are so intuitive and receptive to mitochondriac knowledge.
I hope this place is somewhat shielded from 5G.

A week before we went down there we moved into the renovated farm house. Now that the ski season is coming to a close, we are hoping for less obstructed skies and more birdsong. It can be discouraging for me to sit on the porch only to watch a thick grey blanket of clouds proceed in a perfectly straight line to obscure the morning sun and blue sky, as the windmills spin ominously four miles away in the hills. If the birds didn’t go quiet it wouldn’t feel so bad, I think. Other times the same sight motivates me to get the fuck out of here. Also found out there’s a radio telescope in Westford, Mass where FH works. LOVELY.

Dad got cancer chiseled out of his face, when I went to see him he greeted me from the couch, watching the news. I’m grateful I have my mother’s mitochondria.

Oxy when circadian rhythm is disturbed has still been successfully deterring any nonsensical shit storms between my partner and I. I need to hold myself accountable to CT. I have not done cold water immersion since the lake froze over and can’t afford to be lazy with my dopamine harvesting.

### KrusinWitchie — 2019-05-04T03:31:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.21529/post-269636>

My maternal grandmother is the last G-parent standing. Mito stands strong. I felt close to my roots being with her, and whenever I was with her I was her silent shadow. I have a lot of sweet memories with her as a kid, all of them either in the kitchen or outside in the gardens.

Then in my teens I guess I was grown enough for my mom to completely shit on any good feelings I had about Oma. This broke my heart and I began to resent Oma for making life hard for my mom. I don't know specifically what issues my mom has, but I'm sure she got off by turning me against my own grandmother. I am also sure that the things my mother told me about her were true, however I don't think she should have told me so young. Now I'm a young adult and I have a lot of guilt about this and I know no one else is to blame but myself.

My paternal grandmother had pretty severe dementia and tried calling me when she was essentially on her deathbed to say goodbye when I was in my late teens. I didn't pick up and I didn't call her back. I've made a good deal of poor decisions but nothing feels as shitty as that when I think back on it. Hanging out with Betty and helping her in her gardens has caused some grandma feelings to bubble up. I am offloading the feels here so that they don't spill out into my phone call with Oma.

Don't ever, for even 1 second, think that you are the only one who makes stupid, selfish, childish, and really hurtful and poor decisions.

Move over sister .....you have lots and lots of company!

Never, ever forget...... even after lots of regrets.....

Now that you know better ....you MUST and WILL do better.

The sun has come out for us ........don't ever let anyone block our shine!

### KrusinWitchie — 2019-05-21T18:43:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.21529/post-270992>

Oma is good. Dealt with my guilt and just connected with her.

Stepdaughter is here next week for two weeks... can’t wait. I wasn’t expecting one of the preparations for her visit to be putting our dog down. She bit my finger drawing blood last night when I reached down to close her crate. I can’t accept the possibility of her hurting a child.

I tend to overthink these things given the time and opportunity to do so. It’s like CT. If I mull over how to submerge, when to submerge, to wait for that one cloud to pass... I’ll never get in. Whatever the internal switch is to turn off ruminating thoughts, I do have it, and having a solid goal is essential to pulling the trigger. In this case, quite literally lol.

I’ll remember her wily ways and how big she smiles when she’s running in a field or at the beach. How she wiggles her butt in excitement when she sees me get her leash for a walk. I’ll also remember the tension I felt whenever anyone came to the house as she barked incessantly at them. She was/is causing me some social hermit behaviors and I suppose I can thank her for giving me a definitive reason to grow beyond that now rather than wait for her to die in her old age.

### Phosphene — 2019-06-02T19:06:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.21529/post-271832>

That must have been really difficult with your dog. I’m not sure I could do it but I understand why you chose to, especially if there are potential custody issues with the stepdaughter. My dachshund was a bit of a nipper and very protective of the family around other dogs. She was a rescue and not well-socialized so we were always a little hesitant to take her out and about. Come to think of it, I’ll bet this contributed a bit to our social isolation. Easier just to stay home than having to worry what to do about the dog.

We have a nice compromise now—sister-in-law got a Maltese puppy a few months ago and we watch him three days a week while she’s at work. It was almost my son’s but since he got a job and moved out, that would make him ours, and we’re not ready for the commitment and expense of another dog yet. Plus this dog is just a total wuss. Cowers at everything and his sole purpose is to look cute, which he admittedly does quite well. Having a part time dog is nice—kind of like a grandkid I suppose—enjoy him for a bit then hand him back!

How are things going?

### KrusinWitchie — 2019-06-06T04:20:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.21529/post-272115>

My dachshund was a bit of a nipper and very protective of the family around other dogs. She was a rescue and not well-socialized so we were always a little hesitant to take her out and about. Come to think of it, I’ll bet this contributed a bit to our social isolation. Easier just to stay home than having to worry what to do about the dog.
>
> We have a nice compromise now—sister-in-law got a Maltese puppy a few months ago and we watch him three days a week while she’s at work. It was almost my son’s but since he got a job and moved out, that would make him ours, and we’re not ready for the commitment and expense of another dog yet. Plus this dog is just a total wuss. Cowers at everything and his sole purpose is to look cute, which he admittedly does quite well. Having a part time dog is nice—kind of like a grandkid I suppose—enjoy him for a bit then hand him back!
>
> How are things going?

You and I both have part time dogs. My sister has a goldendoodle puppy that she drops off for a few days a week when she’s working her 12 hour shifts at the hospital. I appreciate the cuddles but sometimes she is just too damn cute and needy—just in time to hand her back like you said  My dog was scrappy and scruffy. I have been missing her a lot although I haven’t had much time to ruminate over it since the stepdaughter is here. I feel tightness in my throat when I think about her, but nothing in comparison to how I felt looking into her eyes those few days between her biting me and us saying goodbye.

I used kordon methylene blue topically on the bite and it healed nicely. The first two days i just did soap and water and alcohol/antibiotic ointment but it was still draining suspiciously and looking red and puffy. The MB immediately dried it up and things started moving. I also put it under a heat lamp several times and exposed it to the sun. I had a puncture wound on the underside of my finger and three long wounds on top of my finger either from me pulling my hand out of her mouth or her biting and dragging. Now all that is left is a bruise under my finger nail.

Interestingly, as a result of this, I learned that my maternal grandmother and my bf’s mother both used methylene blue back in the day.

Thank you for checking in. I received a kind letter in the mail from the vet the other day that let some tears flow and your note did the same. I appreciate it

Short answer to your question is I’ve been drinking a bit too much coffee and wine.

In other news, our well has run dry and in the past two months we have not had a drop of water for over three weeks total. I’ll bite my tongue here cause I could go on and on about how annoying that has been and my journal is already a downer as it is.

### Phosphene — 2019-06-07T21:42:14+0300

That’s interesting about the MB—something I haven’t explored yet for some reason. Going to pick some up!

I hear you on the coffee and wine... At least mine is usually decaf—just love the taste with a little heavy cream.

Plus why is there a coffee emoji here if we aren’t supposed to drink it?

### KrusinWitchie — 2019-06-12T16:08:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.21529/post-272511>

BF is interviewing for a job in NH closer to home, finally, a year and a half after commuting 3-4hrs/day into 5G Boston suburbs. We will find out Thursday whether or not he gets the job... fingers crossed!

Our well water was soooo tasty before these shenanigans started... On Monday the well was hydrofracked to get more water into the well. Chlorine was added to sanitize the well and today I begin the flushing process to flush all of the chlorine out. A few weeks ago when the pump was replaced, the same process took a week and a half until the chlorine was gone. I am hoping this time it’s not so bad.

The fill rate for the well pump is now 1.5 gallons per minute. My little lambs won’t be able to drink from this well so I’ve got to figure out what to with them now.

Light, water, and magnetism... the electricity in this house is fucked, there’s no water... grumble grumble.

Recently found out that my dad was institutionalized by his family for a short time in his early 20s for taking the end of times in the Bible too seriously. My mom told me if she had known she wouldn’t have married him... lol well I’m glad she didn’t know!

We were in Long Island eek over the weekend for BFs sister’s baby shower/gender reveal . She has two boys, 8 and 5 that both have autism. They were conceived and raised in a Bronx apartment literally a few yards away from the 6 train station platform. They moved to LI a few years ago. I guessed (and hoped) that due to a better emf environment (better but still shit) that she would have a girl. It’s a girl! BF is not so convinced that means she won’t have autism, too, because he said he saw an alarming amount of young girls who may have been on the spectrum at a shopping mall in NH recently. We will see. She hasn’t listened to her brother’s warnings about the WiFi or screen time, and she has eaten nothing but fruit throughout her pregnancy due to nausea and vomiting.

Always in the back of my mind I am pondering what it will be like when I am pregnant and raising children. Fuck all the noise, the only thing I care about is what light environment I am in and if my partner will be with me. Still looking forward to becoming a member here and having a consult for this reason.

No one in my family with my mitochondria has had children at this high of a latitude before. Most of them were born in Maryland, including myself, besides my brother and sister who were born in Lowell, Mass and my second cousin’s daughter who was born prematurely in NYC. I will ask where my mother conceived my brother and sister... i wonder if they were also conceived in Massachusetts.

### Phosphene — 2019-06-12T18:58:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.21529/post-272530>

I must be getting all your rain, though it’s finally letting up now. Are you guys planning to stay there long-term? Maybe there is something like this that could help? This of course wouldn’t work in the winter, but it’s still awesome...

[http://www.goodshomedesign.com/a-ba...dXU-B-YswmwGB51bhsq2vcgFkIBRBhUNn8fNQWC9dy\_z0](http://www.goodshomedesign.com/a-bamboo-tower-that-produces-up-to-25-gallons-of-water-in-a-day-by-capturing-condensation/?fbclid=IwAR2mBHYICNjG8SdXU-B-YswmwGB51bhsq2vcgFkIBRBhUNn8fNQWC9dy_z0)

My father was briefly institutionalized for severe depression when I was 2. Something else you and I have in common.

### KrusinWitchie — 2019-06-12T18:58:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.21529/post-272531>

The little one is 6 turning 7. One morning she instinctively found the only real glass window in the house and stood in front of it and said, “Ah, this is the spot!” It faces east and is in an old dusty workroom that we have barely cleaned out from the previous owners... but that didn’t matter to this girl.

She said that she loves bacon more than her mom...

She also said that she loves mackerel more than bacon.

She ate some salmon caviar without knowing what it was and said it tasted like salmon.

On her own, she knows what’s good for her and what’s not. Very cool to witness.

### KrusinWitchie — 2019-07-02T23:02:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.21529/post-273620>

Prepared lamb liver masala yesterday and noticed I could hit much lower notes while singing in the shower. I assume that’s a vit A thing

### KrusinWitchie — 2019-08-09T16:24:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.21529/post-275735>

My priorities as of late have been directed towards my stepdaughter and not so much on "optimal health". This shift hasn't cost me any mitochondriac efforts, except financially (no MX trips yet) because of attorneys fees. Instead, it has strengthened our family's mito lifestyle in a way that is fluid and not strained at all. I have time after this has blown over to dive back into blogs and be active on the forums. I do sometimes have trouble caring for myself when I am alone, but when my stepdaughter is here not only do I nurture her, but myself as well. When she recently headed back to FL it hit me that she would intuitively know if I was not taking good care of myself, so I have been holding myself accountable to my needs despite her physical absence.

We have always known she was not under the best care and that her mom is a nightmare, but we had no idea how bad it truly was until this week. I could get all woo-woo with the backstory of how we got this information, but I'll keep it simple. She is being neglected. Dental neglect. She hadn't been taken to the dentist for over 3 years. For a mito-kid maybe that is okay, but this child eats nothing but fast food, juice, candy and rice w/ ketchup. She has 8 cavities that need fillings and has lost the root of one of her baby teeth.

When her Dad found out and immediately asked her mom to make an appointment to get the fillings done, she refused, accused him of harrassment, and claimed the dentist told her to wait til they fall out. This dental neglect will be the clear and convincing evidence needed to petition for primary physical custody to be transferred to Dad.

Sometime earlier this year, I had a dream that me and my stepdaughter were in Mexico for half the year. The same night, my partner had a dream that his daughter was somewhere she'd never been before and he didn't know who she was with, she wasn't with him or her mom. In his dream he was frightened that it was a kidnapping. I wasn't going to tell him my dream, because I had been thinking about going to Mexico solo for months already and felt unsure of being away from him for a long time. I didn't know what he would think and I didn't even know what I thought about it. When he told me his dream about the kidnapping, I laughed and assured him he had nothing to worry about. It was me in his dream with her, and we were chillin at the beach in Mexico! We had a good laugh.

This manifestation is underway and optimal health is on the horizon for everybody, big and small, in our family.

### KrusinWitchie — 2019-08-09T16:42:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.21529/post-275739>

Oh, and my partner finally got transferred to work at a Whole Foods in New Hampshire! Less commute time, no 5G, and no income taxes! He starts end of August

### Saichi — 2019-08-09T19:08:27+0300

### KrusinWitchie — 2019-08-09T19:37:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.21529/post-275750>

> [Saichi said:](/goto/post?id=275744)
>
> What makes you believe this?

I used to be subject to the paranoia that 5g was everywhere, including the extremely rural and mountaineous area that I live in. As of now, I am not immediately concerned despite the fact that I have not confirmed the absence of 5g with a meter.

My understanding is that fiber optic networks are necessary within the 5G network somewhere along the line. Regular cable is barely offered in this area of NH.

New Hampshire thankfully is one of two states, I believe, to have passed legislation to create a commission to study the environmental and health effects of 5G technology as of July 2019. See NH HB522.

My excitement is that his new work environment will be better than his existing work environment. His existing location was very close to a massive radio telescope and military base and was close enough to downtown Boston that he was most likely subjected to 5G exposure.

### KrusinWitchie — 2019-08-18T15:05:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.21529/post-276189>

> [KrusinWitchie said:](/goto/post?id=275735)
>
> I do sometimes have trouble caring for myself when I am alone, but when my stepdaughter is here not only do I nurture her, but myself as well. When she recently headed back to FL it hit me that she would intuitively know if I was not taking good care of myself, so I have been holding myself accountable to my needs despite her physical absence.

Well, this isn’t fair because:

1\. It’s not my stepdaughters job to maintain my level of self care and...
2\. Im admitting I will not take care of myself if no ones looking.

I picked up a book at the dump the other day. “The Dance of Intimacy” by Harriet Goldhor Lerner. Here’s what triggered my “oh shit” moment:

“Without a life plan, our intimate relationships carry too much weight. We begin to look to others to provide us with meaning or happiness, which is not their job. We want a partner who will provide self-esteem, which cannot be bestowed by another. We set up a situation in which we are bound to get over involved and overfocuse on the other person’s ups and downs because we are underfocused on the self.”

### KrusinWitchie — 2019-08-18T15:11:14+0300

it seems pretty damn obvious after having read that quote.

I feel very uncomfortable reading my own journal and being confronted with how often I am dishonest with myself in order to avoid tough realities and anxieties.

One year old "us" used to crap our pants and puke all over ourselves. Pretty embarrassing if you think about it. But we cut one year old us a little slack, don't we. Stretch that a bit. There's no amount of work or frustration that fixes past us anyway. There's now us and hopefully future us. Invest energy there like a tycoon.

### KrusinWitchie — 2019-09-05T23:52:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.21529/post-277260>

A cousin I truly never talk to asked me for my opinion on IUDs. So I offered it but without much context. A few hours later she made a sly comment on the subject that I didn’t want to believe was as rude as I know it was in hindsight.

I sent her a message to say if she does go the IUD route, to eat plenty of oysters to offset high copper levels in the blood. Her response? “I wish there was an unsubscribe button.” You fucking asked me??? Good Lord.

I am so grateful I have analyzed my own issues with my upbringing and mother so that those issues do not make me a miserable person. Even if they ask for it... I must trust my gut. I had a reluctant feeling when I was first asked and I will listen closely to those feelings next time.

### KrusinWitchie — 2019-09-06T02:00:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.21529/post-277273>

LOL she probably meant unsubscribe from her cycle.

I must be coming up on mine considering how I interpreted that message

### KrusinWitchie — 2019-09-08T17:48:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.21529/post-277410>

Quite a strange night two nights ago... I passed out in the shower. J was in the bathroom with me and caught me before I fell. He splashed cold water on the back of my neck and I regained consciousness after about a minute.

I hadn’t eaten oysters in about two weeks, was eating (homemade with love) crap and drinking tequila last weekend and was short on sleep. That night I had just smoked weed for about the first time in two months and had two glasses of not Malbec red earlier in the evening. I was zoned out in the shower and didn’t realize how hot the water had gotten while we were talking about favorite snacks. Mine was oysters, to the reply of “damn you’re expensive”. I took that as a compliment. We happened to have had a fresh bag of 20 or so oysters in the fridge for the next morning.

Im feeling fine now, even the minor sprain in my ankle is better. Apparently a 5’8 155lb wet noodley dead weight body is hard to maneuver out of the shower even by a hunky man’s standards.

A brief search on the forums led to a post from Dr. K about dandruff being a lack of selenium and sunlight and in the same post, syncope as a sign of poor heteroplasmy. Clearly I can’t afford to be fucking around as much as I have been.

I still sleep within 12-15 feet of the smart meter. I have noticed that I am not nearly as tanned as I was last summer.

### Phosphene — 2019-09-08T20:56:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.21529/post-277423>

That will teach you for taking hot showers!

Glad you’re ok. That would be scary all alone.

Are you prone to fainting? I don’t think I’ve ever fainted from anything. Lightheaded yes, but never passed out.

### KrusinWitchie — 2019-09-11T18:34:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.21529/post-277658>

> [Saichi said:](/goto/post?id=277426)
>
> Hope you have a canopy and some shielding on the meter.

I followed up with the utility company. They can't replace it with an analog, but they can remove it from the house and onto the barn. Right now it is encased in a metal box, but I still pick up the readings with the trimeter. In the meantime I will look into shielding the meter on the house.

I did decide to sleep in the bed of my SUV the past two nights. I drive it out to the pasture. I have screen cloth attached to the car with magnets so that I can open the windows of the car and keep mosquitos out. Although I'm not grounded, there are no RF readings from my meter. I will next be looking to get some of that braided copper wire to ground myself during sleep.

I felt at my best when sleeping on the ground in a tent last year. It was a bitch to maintain long term from the elements, so I moved back inside for the winter. In the car, I have already felt more bright eyed and bushy tailed these past two mornings.

I slept alone, since my parter prefers something more comfortable. I don't have anything like a mattress pad for the car at the moment, I only sleep on top of a cotton comforter. I prefer sleeping on hard surfaces. The fact that I knew he wouldn't sleep with me was a major deterrent to me sleeping in the car for the past few months. I was fearful it would be a source of conflict, but that hasn't been the case. He is supportive of it and for his sake, I hope he joins me. For now, it may work in my favor that he may miss me a little.

### KrusinWitchie — 2019-09-12T04:33:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.21529/post-277691>

I’m borrowing nuggets from @seanwater’s journal. Last name Waters is pretty dope...

> [MITpowered26 said:](/goto/post?id=277649)
>
> As someone that would rather journal than act, someone told me something yesterday that is a gamechanger for me.
>
> "Set your intention every morning and act on one thing"
>
> [@Josh Rosenthal](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/15366/)

Tomorrow morning I intend to lose myself (in as little clothes as possible) in my neighborhood’s country roads until I find my way back home.

I used to venture out into the woods on our property with my dog. The last time I did that, my little terrier ran off and wouldn’t come back. After about 45 minutes she did return. She bit me that night. I examined her and found a small wound on her neck. I suspect she was bitten by some animal that put her on guard and exacerbated her aggressive tendencies. We euthanized her since my stepdaughter was coming days later. That’s been on my mind nearly every day since then. I feel entirely responsible for her aggression and death.

I miss her a lot. I’m sleeping in the same pasture I buried her in. My sister’s dog is a big wuss but will be a sweet companion for my walk tomorrow.

### Phosphene — 2019-09-16T20:45:45+0300

How was this Sunday’s family dinner? Any product testing going on, or won’t they touch it?

### MITpowered26 — 2019-09-16T20:54:52+0300

Late reply.. I relate. I say a lot of things in my journal, especially things I"m going to DO in the future.. that I fail to do. Its embarrassing, and makes me feel like my words have no meaning. ### Phosphene — 2019-09-16T21:40:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.21529/post-277975>

Yeah, one of the reasons I delayed starting my journal is that my thoughts, behaviors and patterns were SO unoptimal I couldn’t imagine admitting to them, even behind a cloak of semi-anonynimity. I still struggle with Facebook-izing, or over-reporting the encouraging successes and conveniently leaving out the less flattering stumbles. I avoid FB for the most part but see how addictive those ‘likes’ can be by their use here. I was astonished when I recently saw how many I actually have.

What??? Me???

It makes it appear that I’m catching up with some of the pillars of the community, when in reality the ‘Like’ function was only added to the forum a few years ago. So unfair to the old timers! Can you imagine the number of likes [@Inger](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1057/) and so many others would have if that function were here from inception??

Anyway...

Accountability is a huge ongoing hurdle for me.

I’m going to be honest here and admit that I probably failed Kruse 101 this spring and early summer by missing quite a few early sunrises, especially the cold rainy ones with obscured sun. I was a forever night owl, and while I’ve mostly changed that habit there’s some stupid mental block about getting up before 6am if I’m not obligated to do anything (besides save my own damn life).

I’m setting the intention of posting a sunrise pic every day from now on. It’s the very least, and of course the very most I can do.

One of the reasons I’m looking forward to returning to work outside the home is an enforced structure, with tangible immediate consequences.

Yikes!

BRILL [@Phosphene](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19648/)

For me, some things that run through my mind as I read your post is this:

On paper, I've thrown a lot of money, really quickly, at the Jack Kruse experience. On the outside, sometimes I wonder what people think and how it appears? "Wait, he's been to the farm, two vips, etc, but in yet he's still super lazy to implement any change and action? What does that say about Jack? And the truth and the response is that Jack will lead you to the water. He can move mountains, and take you through miles of desert, time travel even... but once you arrive at that beautiful fountain with water trickling down in that serene place... and you neglect to taste the water... well.. that is on YOU, that is on ME, and not on Jack. .. Oh, these thing take time.

### KrusinWitchie — 2019-09-18T00:40:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.21529/post-278113>

> [Phosphene said:](/goto/post?id=277960)
>
> How long till you found your way back home?
>
> I’m sure your pup is weighing on you but you must forgive yourself and move on. You made the best decision you could in a pressured and emotional situation involving the well-being of a child. That’s all we can do day to day—make our best decisions and keep moving forward.
>
> How was this Sunday’s family dinner? Any product testing going on, or won’t they touch it?

About an hour.. theres an uphill wooded trail that leads to an open meadow. I wanted it to keep going but it leads into someone’s backyard. Short but sweet

You’re right about moving on and forgiving myself. She was loud AF. If she wasn’t barking she was making all sorts of noises expressing herself to get what she wanted from us. I figure since I tend towards timid and probably have blockages in my throat chakra, she more than made up for that. I can do myself a lot of good by being a little bit more like her.

Pot roast with carrots and parsnips and mushroom grits made by my mom. She used brown sugar. My teeth hurt for two days afterward. I wonder how I’d react to the same meal without the high RF exposure at their house. Sunsets are quickly arriving sooner and sooner. I will begin to host Saturday brunches this winter to avoid a poor environment on fall and winter Sunday nights.

I haven’t made them any samples yet... at the moment I am an excellent sloth.

### KrusinWitchie — 2019-09-18T01:18:06+0300

> [Phosphene said:](/goto/post?id=277975)

> I’m going to be honest here and admit that I probably failed Kruse 101 this spring and early summer by missing quite a few early sunrises, especially the cold rainy ones with obscured sun. I was a forever night owl, and while I’ve mostly changed that habit there’s some stupid mental block about getting up before 6am if I’m not obligated to do anything (besides save my own damn life).

Since sleeping in the car, I have no choice but to see the morning sun and sunrise when I get up and out. That's been helping me. I struggle with accountability too, so we have that in common. I have plenty of dreams about missing deadlines, being late for work, showing up to places naked lol. Each morning I wake up (right now its about 5:15) and tell myself it's still 3AM. I fall back asleep for about another hour and twenty minutes and have the most vivid dreams. Today I began journaling my dreams, I feel it will be helpful for me to connect with myself. The lesson in this morning's dream was to get my shit together and tell the haters to fuck off! I was peeing in a toilet where plenty of people could see me (this is recurring) and instead of being anxious like I usually am in these dreams, I spoke to the man invading my privacy. Then I went and sat down at a table with two women. One was named Dawn. I asked her if she spelled it "Daun" or "Dawn" and she was nasty and made fun of my interest in linguistics. I took no offense and just explained my interest. According to the internet, "To see the dawn in your dream signifies rejuvenation, enlightenment, and vitality. You are emerging out from a new stage in life and have a new understanding or a new start in life."

I am excited for you to start your new gig. Hope you can sample some meatballs here and there from the kitchen ​

My dreams have always been vivid so I hope that says something good about my redox and sleeping environment. And I totally relate to the anxiety and shame-related dreams, not lately though, so that’s good as well. I had much to work out apparently.

Good for you on moving the weekly family gathering to your space, under your control. I must say that I do admire your parents for enforcing that tradition, and I was honored to participate. As flawed as they (we) are they all still love you tremendously.

The restaurant owner is open to some paleo/Keto-friendly dishes so I hope that will save me.

### KrusinWitchie — 2019-10-11T17:32:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.21529/post-280103>

I began writing in a physical journal. I became aware that my voice is fragmented, which is no surprise because my thoughts are too. I am not putting too much pressure on this area because in time, plus effort, it will improve.

I have been learning a lot about abuse in the past year. It is comforting to know there are others on the forum that have been through similar experiences and achieved growth. I think that my fragmented thinking may have something to do with this. I learned how to be abusive from my environment and am giving myself the space to learn not to be. As unwise as it may seem I am also giving that space to my SO to do the same for himself and in turn for us.

I am currently overcome with guilt. Despite this I still smile at the sun and imagine roots enveloping me when I ground outside. I am a work in progress. We had a trip to Florida planned for this week to see the little one. An attempt on my part to express my emotions 36 hours before departure did not bode well. Names were thrown at me and I was told he was going alone. I kept my cool, expressed how hurtful it was and that I wouldn’t make excuses for this outburst of his. I removed myself and went barefoot outside. I am proud of myself that I was able to cry.

I decided that I wasn’t going to go along if I wasn’t wanted, especially if I was going to be giving, giving, giving during the trip. After such a hurtful exchange I felt I needed time to be alone. I was worried I’d be resentful during the trip and I didn’t want that to rub off on the little one. They are so sensitive to us.

The next day I laid it all out on the table. I was raised by parents with reversed gender roles. My mom provided, threatened suicide out of exhaustion, and was never around to raise us. I made my lunches since second grade and still have a problem properly nurturing myself. My dad was home whenever he didn’t have a job and was the “nurturer” and was not so good at that job either. This emasculation from not being the provider for our family and my mother publicly resenting him for it caused a lot of pain for him I am sure. He was very reactive and emotional. And drank a lot. My binge drinking from the age of 14 was celebrated within the family. I was never corrected for it. I am not complaining. It is what it is. They endured painful circumstances and I have compassion for them.

My SO was raised in a similar environment with passive men and dominant women. I do not have a clear idea of positive masculine male figures in my life.

I told him that I wasn’t going to be his mother, and that I need the space to express my femininity through my emotions without them being held against me. I highlighted his abusive behavior. I told him I needed to be cared for emotionally and supported.

He said that hearing it hurt him, but that he realizes that he has never comforted me emotionally and that he doesn’t know how. What he does know how to do is freeze up and react with anger because he interprets emotions expressed as a manipulation. He admitted that it isn’t the case with me and that he needs to see the truth in my emotions.

He was not pleased that despite the heart to heart apology I still was not coming to Florida. I don’t know if that was the right decision for me to make, but it is what I decided to do. I don’t know if I feel guilty because of something he said or did, or if it is a natural response to me standing firm in my needs. I am only just learning how to do that and it is kind of horrifying.

I know this sounds like a big stinky mess and also that I have a predisposition to cling to those that push me away, but this didn’t feel like that. Hence why I feel guilty. This is a very confusing time for me. At least it’s not December, like Phosphene said

### MITpowered26 — 2019-10-11T19:14:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.21529/post-280120>

" I am not putting too much pressure on this area because in time, plus effort, it will improve."
" am currently overcome with guilt. Despite this I still smile at the sun and imagine roots enveloping me when I ground outside. I am a work in progress"

[@KrusinWitchie](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/21509/)

Hello. Your words touched me in this note. Writing has saved my life this year, and I hope same for you, Witchie. You already have the most important piece of the puzzle, you know? You have the 30,000 foot view. See the quotations above .. On my good days, I have to find this piece sometimes. The compassionate, gentle "nudging" along with your journey.

J

### KrusinWitchie — 2019-10-11T20:27:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.21529/post-280138>

> [MITpowered26 said:](/goto/post?id=280120)

>
> Hello. Your words touched me in this note. Writing has saved my life this year, and I hope same for you, Witchie. You already have the most important piece of the puzzle, you know? You have the 30,000 foot view. See the quotations above .. On my good days, I have to find this piece sometimes. The compassionate, gentle "nudging" along with your journey.
>
> J

Thank you, J. I am trying to be kind to myself right now and you know I just feel like I don’t know how. But it seems I am making progress. Now kicking myself... Did I not go to FL because I wanted to teach him a lesson? Is that really more important for ME than soaking up the FL sun for a week is? Surely the joy from a sweet young little girl who loves me would have cheered me up as well?

I thought I was clear headed at the time I made the decision but in fact I wasn’t entirely sure and kept asking myself if I was making the right decision by not going. I was looking for confirmation and I couldn’t find it within myself. I could have looked to my partner who obviously wanted me to go with him but I was just so hurt still by the manipulation. The cup spilleth over with emotions...

I hope he has the strength and compassion to forgive me and see my pain for what it was and nothing more. That’s when I really needed a rational mind to nudge me towards a healthier decision for everyone involved but we both were just too emotional and I thought I was being rational anyways.

Going to go baptize myself in the cool and windy lake and try not to mull over this. The wind should obliterate my ruminating thoughts.

### Phosphene — 2019-10-11T20:40:55+0300

Head out further and let that glorious body of water support you and absolve all your “sins”...

I went out knee deep and did not go all in. Either I am excellent at finding excuses or I was wisely conscious of the fact that I was not hydrated enough to go all in. I’ll fix that part today and be ready for a Sunday sin absolving floating in the deep session.. facing my fear.

### MITpowered26 — 2019-10-11T21:03:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.21529/post-280149>

> [KrusinWitchie said:](/goto/post?id=280138)
>
> Thank you, J. I am trying to be kind to myself right now and you know I just feel like I don’t know how. But it seems I am making progress. Now kicking myself... Did I not go to FL because I wanted to teach him a lesson? Is that really more important for ME than soaking up the FL sun for a week is? Surely the joy from a sweet young little girl who loves me would have cheered me up as well?
>
> I thought I was clear headed at the time I made the decision but in fact I wasn’t entirely sure and kept asking myself if I was making the right decision by not going. I was looking for confirmation and I couldn’t find it within myself. I could have looked to my partner who obviously wanted me to go with him but I was just so hurt still by the manipulation. The cup spilleth over with emotions...
>
> I hope he has the strength and compassion to forgive me and see my pain for what it was and nothing more. That’s when I really needed a rational mind to nudge me towards a healthier decision for everyone involved but we both were just too emotional and I thought I was being rational anyways.
>
> Going to go baptize myself in the cool and windy lake and try not to mull over this. The wind should obliterate my ruminating thoughts.

May I say something?

I can see you are trying. And you are trying at the right things... You are perceiving the right attitudes, which will serve you greatly.

Its a bicycle. You are learning to ride it. Keep getting on the bicycle. That is all. You don't think about balancing when you ride a bike, you just balance. You just feel it.

...

Same is true with knowing what is the right and wrong decisions in this life. YOU CANNOT CONTROL what the SO thinks, reacts, acts. Yes, there are consequences to your actions ... but you just do the best you can in each moment with what you are able to do. And then you just make the decisions. And see the results. And experience the results. And feel the bad and the good and the ugly. And then you learn. And then you grow. And then you come to be okay with the process in time...and you survive.. and then you thrive. And you come on the forum if you need extra support

The dust will ultimately settle where it settles, time and again, and the truth will inevitably evolve and emerge and reveal itself.. you don't control the balance of the bicycle.. you just feel it, and learn to ride it... in time. **Keep getting on the bicycle, even if you don't know what that bicycle really is right now.**

May I suggest something?

HAVE self-doubt. SECOND guess yourself. WORRY about how he will react. KICK yourself. And really do it. BE fragmented. BE whatever it is that is inside you. And take as long as you need with yourself. The result with your SO will reveal itself in time. But more importantly, the experiences that are going on right inside your body, right now? ... will serve to reveal to you even more importance to your life, and ultimately to your family.... it will reveal to you, YOU. Let it come over and over and over again as long as you need. But I **promise you** there will be a day, near or far, where the lesson will click. And you will go, "AHA". And things will be right as rain. It will be beautiful, Witchie. And hold onto that too. And then you will move onto the next lessons of your life on this crazy journey, stronger, and wiser. And you will be able to carry your own bucket of water FIRST, always, always, and then his, and then the little one's.

J

### KrusinWitchie — 2019-12-10T19:05:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.21529/post-283397>

Yesterday morning I found the cats’ water bowl frozen like this. Usually it doesn’t freeze this way. I remember reading here that if water freezes “up” like that it is a deuterium story. Going to look into that. I am hopeful this means my well water is low in deuterium and good to drink.

Home has a few more residents these days. On Halloween I picked up two “barn cats” from my local humane society. One is a feral tabby and the other is a black older gal who can’t use a litter box properly. I didn’t plan to get a black cat on Halloween, it just happened. It was 70F which is unseasonably warm for NH. 5 minutes after I got them settled into the barn, we had a bit of a light show on the power line across the street which cut the power for the night.

We got the cats to help with our rodent population. Unfortunately they had to stay in big dog crates for 3 weeks to get acclimated to their new environment, but not long after their release I found a murdered mole outside. Nice job kitties.

We also moved our four sheep into the farm. It’s already been below 0F here. It makes a 30 degree day feel SO refreshing. Today is in the upper fifties and I even saw a mosquito.. strange.

I haven’t been posting much because I feel I shouldn’t until I commit to cold water immersion CT. I feel I don’t have much to offer/take from the forums without CT except for a good example of what not to do. I also have changed my views on the “witchie” part of my username so I may go back to my initial account “Hannah Barrows” when I do drop in next.

Much love to all

### caroline — 2019-12-14T02:07:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.21529/post-283528>

I love that you are going back to your old name!

C/T is a game changer ....just do it! Please make an effort to go to your special spots at least a couple of times a week.

I will be waiting to hear........

and BTW .....you have so much to offer - don't underestimate yourself . I can feel your power/presence on the other side of the world....

# Thread: What to do while lying down?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-to-do-while-lying-down.21531/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BrunoB — 2018-06-29T16:42:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-to-do-while-lying-down.21531/post-244561>

Hi good day. What do you recommend doing while I'm lying down waiting for sunrise? I clean my nails, I listen to the radio and I should start meditating. What do you suggest? Thank you.

### drezy — 2018-06-29T18:25:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-to-do-while-lying-down.21531/post-244570>

I prefer going back to sleep. I'm fortunate enough to be tuned to wake within a 20 minute window pre sunrise each day.

in lieu of that you may want to experiment and note daily changes with things like:

Chanting "Ommmmm" (It's a phonon thing)
Concentrating on out-breath emphasis (aka deeper outbreaths)
Start on some ice water prior to the light for better light absorption \*
Work on fuller recall of your dreams
Focus on one thing in your life that you are extremely grateful for

\* - My rough notes mention previous posts of early morning CT shunting sex steroid hormones. I don't think ice water would have as pronounced an effect, but it's worth monitoring.

### caroline — 2018-06-30T03:23:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-to-do-while-lying-down.21531/post-244597>

I recommend - just being still ........

why do you need to do something at this important time of day???????

Just being still is the very best meditation ....in my opinion.

It kind of empties my mind of clutter .......especially at the ocean

### Mike David — 2018-07-04T00:12:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-to-do-while-lying-down.21531/post-244904>

Appreciate nature. The complexity of just a tree is amazing. Notice every last detail then look harder there’s even more details. Don’t just say yea that’s a tree whoohoo look at how it’s constructed think about all the little critters it supports in their daily activities ext... I notice something unique in my habitat every day it never lets me down.

### Meli — 2018-07-16T00:26:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-to-do-while-lying-down.21531/post-245939>

I get up and lay down on the floor with a blanket in front of a sliding door that I open up. The sun shines on me as it rises. Once it is shining I get up and go outside on the deck and get a shot of sun sans blanket. Then I work my way outside and stay out for about 2 hours - I meditate, stretch, pray, rub my feet, do a breathing exercise... sit and look at the sun and the birds.

# Thread: Dr. Kruse comments on dental calculus and K2
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-kruse-comments-on-dental-calculus-and-k2.21534/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Benjamin Stone — 2018-06-29T23:45:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-kruse-comments-on-dental-calculus-and-k2.21534/post-244586>

In his book, Dr. Kruse noted addressing K2 deficiency resolved dental calculus/tartar. Some examples were given (butter) for food sources, and there are databases online that suggest others.

Has anyone resolved dental caclulus without supplemental K2?

So, would consuming say duck fat (high in K2, but perhaps not a great fatty acid profile otherwise) be sufficient to address calculus, which I have (and scrape off weekly)? Any other suggestions? I may need to send some food out for testing as results seem inconsistent (wrt liver or ruminants etc).

I eat a paleolithic ketogenic diet, but generally only seafood and ruminant - I do not consume dairy. I strongly prefer not to take any supplements.

Thanks in advance for any insight!

### Jack Kruse — 2018-06-30T21:48:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-kruse-comments-on-dental-calculus-and-k2.21534/post-244625>

You need the sun to make it operate properly.

### nonchalant — 2018-06-30T22:57:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-kruse-comments-on-dental-calculus-and-k2.21534/post-244628>

Hi Benjamin, I had a bad tartar problem a few years ago, when I was living in the city and even after I moved to the country. The calculus would actually reach up to the bite surface of the tooth. I purchased a cheap dental tool and was scraping it off often. The MK-4 version of vitamin K worked well for me. But what worked even better came after I realized what was causing it. Once I stopped walking barefoot near power lines, the trouble just disappeared. It has never returned, in these past few years. My poor dentist just doesn't have much to do now.

### Benjamin Stone — 2018-07-01T17:40:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-kruse-comments-on-dental-calculus-and-k2.21534/post-244674>

Dr Kruse > Thank you. I am deliberately taking more sun this year and look forward to seeing how it affects me.

Nonchalant > Thanks, I am fortunate to be out of the city for the summer, so will see if time up north will make a difference, we have no power lines to be concerned with here! Noted re MK4, I am still hoping to be able to eliminate this condition w/o supplements but I will be open to using them if needed.

### nonchalant — 2018-07-10T02:34:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-kruse-comments-on-dental-calculus-and-k2.21534/post-245482>

# Thread: Must I skip morning exercise before break-fast?

### Benjamin Stone — 2018-06-29T23:54:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/must-i-skip-morning-exercise-before-break-fast.21535/post-244588>

I wake to watch the sun rise with my feet planted on the wet ground each morning.

Typically, I do 100 pushups, 100 squats, in 5 sets, while watching the sunrise, and then go for a short 1 mile walk.

Prior to discovering Dr. Kruse, I would then train fasted around 9-10am and break my overnight fast around 11-1130am.

I am experimenting now with training in the late afternoon (per Dr. Kruse the optimal window) - **I don't like it**, but will see if it likes me and results in better body composition ;-)

It is hard to eat within 30 minutes of the sun rising but I am going to try this summer. I am so accustomed to eating later (typically 11am and 5pm)

My question: I know Dr. Kruse advises against raising cortisol with fasted exercise in his book I have just read, but would the relatively low impact pushups and squats really count in a meaningful way against this? Its just part of a morning routine I would rather not break, to do this on waking while watching sun rise.

Thanks for any answers in advance!

# Thread: Developing eye strain from the sun
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/developing-eye-strain-from-the-sun.21536/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Michael CULLEN — 2018-06-30T14:12:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/developing-eye-strain-from-the-sun.21536/post-244605>

From May on this year I have had the most sunlight I have ever had in my adult life. My skin can tolerate this heat wave in the UK fine, no issues. I have built up some tolerance over the last month. My eyes however are becoming strained.

This seems to be getting worse as I get more time in the sun. Jack does not advise glasses, right? What about blue blockers if need be? I have never had any issues with my eyes, my eyesight is fine, but being out in the sun yesterday has really caused me a lot of problems. Now all the muscles around the eyes are fatigued and I have headache due to this, plus sore eyes.

Do our eyes adjust over time? I am eating seafood, I have just ate a large cod fillet. I am getting enough DHA.

Any thoughts?

cheers

### Jack Kruse — 2018-06-30T15:23:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/developing-eye-strain-from-the-sun.21536/post-244607>

Sun doesn't cause eye strain......it actually improves the extraocular eye muscles and the muscles of the pupil. So as they strengthen and your globe remains oblong there could be an adaptation period.

### Phosphene — 2018-06-30T18:22:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/developing-eye-strain-from-the-sun.21536/post-244618>

[@Michael CULLEN](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20925/) —You could try the following “exercises” which stretch the eye muscles and do a pretty good job of relieving strain.

For extra relaxation, coordinate the movements with long, deep breaths through the nose, exhaling longer than inhaling.

Start with 4-5 minutes and end with a minute or two of “palming” the eyes with cupped hands—resting them in the warmth and darkness. Good luck!

### Jack Kruse — 2018-06-30T20:51:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/developing-eye-strain-from-the-sun.21536/post-244623>

### Sean Waters — 2018-07-06T14:26:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/developing-eye-strain-from-the-sun.21536/post-245134>

> [Michael CULLEN said:](/goto/post?id=244605)
>
> From May on this year I have had the most sunlight I have ever had in my adult life. My skin can tolerate this heat wave in the UK fine, no issues. I have built up some tolerance over the last month. My eyes however are becoming strained.
>
> This seems to be getting worse as I get more time in the sun. Jack does not advise glasses, right? What about blue blockers if need be? I have never had any issues with my eyes, my eyesight is fine, but being out in the sun yesterday has really caused me a lot of problems. Now all the muscles around the eyes are fatigued and I have headache due to this, plus sore eyes.
>
> Do our eyes adjust over time? I am eating seafood, I have just ate a large cod fillet. I am getting enough DHA.
>
> Any thoughts?
>
> cheers

Your eye is the hub for DHA. Anytime you are straining with sunlight is due to lack of absorption (Low DHA).

Test your 06/03... Cod has about 0.05g of DHA per 100g. Wild salmon has around 1.0g per 100g, 20 times more. 10 oysters would give you double that of a Salmon.

If you eat bacon or olive oil or meat, at all, especially the UK Crap, which contain around 10-30g 06, and a fillet of cod, then your ratio is off by 100:1 or more each meal. Get tested. I got mine done in the UK here: [https://www.medichecks.com/nutritional-tests/omega-3-and-6-check-essential-fatty-acids](https://www.medichecks.com/nutritional-tests/omega-3-and-6-check-essential-fatty-acids)

No doctor needed, just prick your finger and its back in 14 days.

I had eye issues with the sun when i used to think Chicken, Almond and Peanut butter and Olive oil was ok. They are omega 6 black holes as someone once described them on here. Get tested! You may need the epi-paleo rx for a few **years** to sort that ratio out.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-07-08T19:16:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/developing-eye-strain-from-the-sun.21536/post-245344>

The most significant melanopsin light response was observed in cells expressing the aequorin receptor, indicating that melanopsin induces a strong increase in intracellular calcium. This should make you stop.........nnEMF all involves calcium flows in humans.

Abe Liboff's work on calcium ion frequency resonance is key here.

The photosensitive active retinoid in humans, 11-cis-retinal, is produced in the RPE and delivered to the photoreceptors in the eye. The RPE is loaded with melanin.

**UVA Phototransduction Drives Early Melanin Synthesis in Human Melanocytes.

What does this imply about the skin and 11-cis-retinal and how it operates with UVA light?
**
This means you need UVA AM light to make retinol for melanopsin function........from the skin.

This means how we dress and what we do in the AM is a big issue and likely a major problem for modern humans.

Vitamin A has many isoforms. Not all are equivalent. Excessive retinoic acid is even more toxic than retinol, consistent with the fact that
retinoic acid is more biologically active. Retinoid therapy for human diseases is often associated
with side effects such as teratogenicity (Accutane).

Chronic exposure to clinical doses of 11-cis
retinoic acid suppresses hippocampal neurogenesis and disrupts hippocampal-dependent memory.
In addition, 13-c/5 retinoic acid intake causes night blindness.

Retinal is the aldehyde version of vitamin A derivative that is most toxic, due to its chemical reactivity with membranes. It acts like a detergent. Even when
vitamin A is used only for light sensing, retinal can be toxic due to its chemical toxicity in
randomly modifying proteins through Schiff base formation.

**Retinal toxicity becomes more severe for organisms using monostable pigments (melanopsin/rhodopsin), which constantly release
free retinal in daylight.

This SINGLE FACT might be why man-made light during day or night
might be behind most modern human diseases.**

As a protein that interacts with retinal, ABCA4 in photoreceptor cells is sensitive to retinal -mediated
photooxidative damage. A photoreceptor cell culture study revealed that retinal is much more
toxic than retinol in mediating photooxidative damage. Photooxidation caused by all-trans
retinal released from monostable pigments has been observed in single vertebrate photoreceptor
cells.

This bread crumb you better not forget.

### Penny — 2018-07-10T20:57:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/developing-eye-strain-from-the-sun.21536/post-245561>

I've always for many years now gone out into the back yard with nothing on - had no idea it was even good for me... it's funny the stuff that you do that "saved you" without even knowing it was doing so - and it's even more ironic when you think you're doing something to improve your health is actually a complete fail - I still eat almonds - for the gut flora - and because nothing kills hunger better IMHO - I also eat at least one Brazil nut per day - I can be outside for hours - and we have a lot of sunshine here - and not burn - I am also in the exact lattitude as my ancestors eating pretty much the same diet -

I take the weekend off from all computer use - you can get so much done

When I look into my past experience working for a plastic surgeon at his anti-aging clinic I can not stop thinking about the two main methods we used to fight “sun related aging & damage.” Rentin A & Intense Pulse Light. I am now trying to understand exactly how this may have hurt our clients & myself, but at the time produced the desired results. I had also been using Rentin A mixed with Lytera 2.0 for the past year, but this time I seem to be developing more “sun” damage. I operated the IPL machine & advised on our go to skin care system Obaji. I used to believe I was a well informed trained esthetician. Now I feel like I had no idea what I was doing to people!

**Maternal Mito:

# Thread: The Story Of Sara
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-story-of-sara.21539/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sara — 2018-07-01T04:18:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-story-of-sara.21539/post-244646>

After reading through a couple of journals, I realize I’ve entered a whole new world of lab and cellular detail that read like Greek to me. Really, all I know about my cells is that they love water and I give it to them. I’m not a foodie; I don’t eat for taste, but for fuel and like to build up an appetite before I eat. Food is like an afterthought. If I eat at all during a day, I will eat something mid-day and be done with it. I only eat organic or home grown foods and juice, maybe, 3 times a week. I just don’t get why we’re supposed to eat 3 meals every day. That’s a lot of food. When I was a child, I was told I had a slow metabolism; maybe that’s why I don’t seem to need too much food; on average, during my adult life, I eat a meal every few days; I usually pop something in my mouth every day like a banana or some raw cheese or some dried fruit and nuts; maybe some avocado. I drink a half gallon to a gallon of water every day with MegaHydrate, 2 drops of potassium iodide to protect my thyroid from radiation, one half tsp of himalayan salt in every liter and a dropper full of Miracle II Neutralizer, also in every liter. My cells feel alive with the sound of life in here. My water has become my recipe for health. In fact, the other day, I took a tsp of BulletProof Brain Octane (a coconut oil concentrate) on an empty stomach and immediately felt nauseous. I was told to eat something and while waiting for a piece of toast, I sipped my water and felt just fine, so the toast never made it to my mouth. Another time, I was saddling up and just as I was about to leave, I felt a bit weak and chastised myself for not eating. I ate a banana and took off with all the energy I needed. I drink Kombucha and Holy Tea; do a colon cleanse once a year and a water/juice fast for 7 days when the mood strikes me. The only other substance I drink is coffee. I love coffee and probably drink too much, but it seems to suit me. I figure with my water intake, the coffee gets pretty much flushed out. And I smoke some, but there’s a caveat: I don’t smoke manufactured cigarettes and smoke 3-4 a day, depending; whenever I get emotional anxiety, the first thing I want is a mug of coffee and a cigarette.

I have no illness’ that I know of; I don’t get seasonally sick; and if I’m around someone who is sick, I take a cap of oregano oil when I get home and maybe some turmeric if I can find where I put it. It seems to be, mostly, respiratory illness’ because most times I’ll feel a tickle in my throat when I swallow, but it doesn’t amount to anything. I do take a tsp of black seed oil almost every day (if I remember) and use magnesium oil also every day. If I can’t be outside for some reason, I take a liquid vitamin D3; oh, and I take a swig of liquid vitamin C each day. I haven’t been to a doctor in 40 years. I’m probably deficient in some respects; we’re all polluted to an extent, but it doesn’t hold me back. I don't take any meds. My main complaint is that I can’t seem to rebuild my muscle mass that was lost on my last 2 horse wrecks over the past 5 years. I stack hay, carry 50lb sacks of feed, lift saddles and all the accompanying chores of keeping horses, but still, my arms and thighs have become scrawny. I lost my libido about 20 years ago; it’s like a switch just turned off and I feel fine without it. I do have some short-term memory loss going on, a bit of tinnitus and I’m losing my hearing. I also wear reading glasses. I have a strong constitution, sleep well and I love dirt. I'm 70 come this July.

My main reason for being here and following Jack is my concern about emfs and am reading Jacks’ nnEMF blogs. I even have a new file marked “New Lexicon.” I am blessed to live in a remote area that has a couple of towers 7 miles away in one direction and another tower 14 miles away to the north. But everyone uses wifi. Everyone’s got one of those phones. Like the world over, everyone’s focused on their little mobile distractor. All the time. I have a desktop computer. The less time I spend on line, the better I feel, but my computer is on a lot; all my writing is on it. That’s what I do when I’m not doing something else. I write. When the phone company runs a landline into my space, I’ll get a rotary phone and probably keep it unplugged unless I need to contact someone. I want to be hardwired on line is all. 5G is the latest invasion and I want to know all about its aspects and its effects on our mitochondria and Natures’ Circadian Rhythms.

I spend a lot of my time in the forest or out in the pasture. I keep watch on Nature for changes in the environment. The trees are failing; they’re brittle and their limbs break off even if there’s no wind. Birds are becoming more scarce; lizards, too and squirrels. Rabbits now; rabbits are getting smarter and even more prolific, if that’s possible. Maybe it’s because they’re getting smarter. Predators seem to be doing alright and the big guys (elk, deer, bear, mountain lion) seem to be acting the same as always, although I did run across an elk last year that had a wasting disease and had to inform the Forest Service. They put her down and were very low-key with an explanation, but I know what I saw; it was like mad cow. I watch the sky constantly to discern the patterns of geo-engineering; it seems to be most dramatic at the change of season, which can be off by as much as a month. I know that Natures’ Circadian Rhythms must be warped due to human influence. The deeper you go into the wilderness, though, it seems more harmonic; more resilient. We have 3 million acres of wilderness to roam and that ain’t nothing to sneeze at these days. At times when I can’t get out there, I smoke cannabis instead; to clear my head. Sativa.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-07-01T05:07:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-story-of-sara.21539/post-244647>

There's never an easy route to the things that matter most in life...........

### Sara — 2018-07-04T06:07:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-story-of-sara.21539/post-244930>

Yeah, it's a lifestyle, Jack. I can't imagine what an easy life would be like; what would the rewards look like? The best place for me is on the edge of nowhere, figuring it out for myself. I want to thank you for affirming my choices; I run on instinct rather than knowledge. Example: I've known artificial light is harmful, but I didn't know why. Now I do. And I wear utex glasses when I'm under it. Like that.

Have a safe holiday!

### Joyfun — 2018-07-16T14:40:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-story-of-sara.21539/post-245976>

\[QUOTE="Sara, post: 244646, member: 21601" I drink a half gallon to a gallon of water every day with MegaHydrate, 2 drops of potassium iodide to protect my thyroid from radiation, one half tsp of himalayan salt in every liter and a dropper full of Miracle II Neutralizer, also in every liter.
I was told to eat something and while waiting for a piece of toast, I sipped my water and felt just fine, so the toast never made it to my mouth.
I take a liquid vitamin D3;
That’s what I do when I’m not doing something else. I write.
I did run across an elk last year that had a wasting disease and had to inform the Forest Service. They put her down and were very low-key with an explanation, but I know what I saw; it was like mad cow.
I know that Natures’ Circadian Rhythms must be warped due to human influence. Sativa.\[/QUOTE\]
Hi [@Sara](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/21601/),
I have a Himalayan salt lamp in my bedroom and a small Himalayan salt night light in the bathroom. However, Dr. Kruse wrote somewhere in the Forum that another kind of salt would be better. I use a sea salt from France. Kosher salt made my ankles swell, so there must be a difference in salts.
I love the Miracle II Neutralizer and the products I used that the inventor developed. Did you ever read the story how the formula came into existence? I wonder if the story is still there to read.
I am curious, was it a person or personal intuition that told you to eat something?
Have you heard or read anything that Dr. Kruse says about supplemental Vitamin D3? Look into it before making any decisions to use more.
You have a very inspired muse to guide your writing. It is beautiful. Have you published?
I am concerned that the Forest Service was "very low-key with an explanation." What do they know that they are not telling?
Let's hope that human influence has only had a small influence on Natures' Circadian Rhythms.

### Sara — 2018-07-16T19:19:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-story-of-sara.21539/post-245994>

Joyfun … I also have a Himalayan salt lamp; one in my bedchamber and one on my desk where I compute. I was told it might mitigate the effects of radiation. Yes, the story of Miracle II; how lovely to find someone who appreciates this fine and magnificent product. The story of the military trying to reverse engineer it gives proof to its origins. Looks like more research for me: vitamin D3.

Thank you for the compliment. I have never had any interest in publishing until recently; I'm Dickinsonesque that way.

### Joyfun — 2018-07-19T14:35:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-story-of-sara.21539/post-246178>

> [Sara said:](/goto/post?id=245994)
>
> Joyfun … I also have a Himalayan salt lamp; one in my bedchamber and one on my desk where I compute. I was told it might mitigate the effects of radiation. Yes, the story of Miracle II; how lovely to find someone who appreciates this fine and magnificent product. The story of the military trying to reverse engineer it gives proof to its origins. Looks like more research for me: vitamin D3.
>
> Thank you for the compliment. I have never had any interest in publishing until recently; I'm Dickinsonesque that way.

Hi [@Sara](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/21601/),
I got to thinking about Miracle II products and ordered 22oz of Miracle II Soap. The shipping cost more than the soap! Then, today I inquired about the cost of shipping Miracle II Neutralizer. I searched for youtubes of Clayton Tedeton and thought this one was very good:

If you haven't seen it, it's worth a look-see. He even mentioned the words "structured water." And then the part where the 3 bottles of Miracle II products did not burn in the car that exploded. Reminded me of the California fires last year that melted cars but the trees were basically OK.
I messed around with my Signature page, deleted my 3 legged stool, and for the life of me I can not figure out how I got it there in the first place. All I can do is put a little one here:

Never underestimate the ability of others to hide disease beneath a carefully curated façade.

I used to see people and not their problems... now I can see their problems in their eyes, their faces, and the rest of their bodies, even if they don't let anyone see it. I can see it, and I see more and more of it the more I learn about quantum medicine.

### Eric Waldron — 2018-07-02T06:10:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tattoo-removal.21542/post-244737>

^In what ways? I'm highly interested in this as well.

### drezy — 2018-07-02T16:50:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tattoo-removal.21542/post-244774>

> [Leland Stillman said:](/goto/post?id=244736)
>
> Never underestimate the ability of others to hide disease beneath a carefully curated façade.

+50 French Scrabble points on that one.

Trained clinicians see patient after patient and when they apply the human knack for pattern recognition as well as putting the pieces together it's an excellent combination.

> [Quantum1 said:](/goto/post?id=244728)
>
> They don't seem have any issues from what I can tell.

I used to think this, but I have noticed several sports injuries in younger men than me. It's usually written up (or written off) as "freak accident".

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...o-cold-and-not-light.17029/page-2#post-180139](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/do-you-need-am-uva-sunlight-in-winter-if-you-are-coupled-to-cold-and-not-light.17029/page-2#post-180139)

There is also mention on the forums of triathlon heart attacks especially in conjunction with cold water.

I think sometimes "freak" is used in reference to these injuries not because the accident was very freakish at a cellular level ,but reality defied our assumptions and expectations. I think the word can be used to wallpaper over something that could use better observation. I think the sentiment is also bundled in with the general term "sudden heart attack". Adjective used to fortify our assumptions and perspectives.

To me seeing someone running on senescent cells , say like brain stem, isn't evident. Thats why I'm glad to hear there are good clinicians out there.

I've been watching what happens in my own mitochondrial line when system after system start to run on senescent cells. While it is evident in the case I'm witnessing, I've learned something that applies to those that appear healthy. It's amazing just how much the body can put up with before a system breaks and when the system finally breaks it breaks dramatically.
