# Thread: My optimal journey
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journey.21543/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 168
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mike David — 2018-07-01T19:48:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journey.21543/post-244701>

I live in Perkasie PA in a single family home on 1 acre of land. My ancestors come mostly from Germany which is about the same latitude as me. My population density is about 700 per sq. Mi. My mom is pretty much healthy just had some heart trouble a few years back probably from a shit diet. She is currently on no medications 60 years young. Grand mom lived to 81 died of a heart attack also had macular degeneration. I personally haven’t seen a doc in about 5 years feel really healthy. I’m 6’1 210 lb. looking to get to 190. That’s down from like 230lb 3 mo. Ago. I worked as an electrician for the past 15 years stopped last August to be stay at home dad not going back. I drank about 60 oz. of coffee a day smoked a pack of cigarettes a day and smoked weed every day just about. Was outside very little and never connected to ground. Ironic being that I was an electrician. Luckily last August I started listening to Jack Kruse podcasts that started me on the right track. I weighed about 240 lbs. and ate a horrible diet. I had joint pain every day but still slept good but couldn’t remember shit forgot things a lot. I no longer have any pain skin cleared up white spots on nails are gone and started dreaming again. Took a lot of teeth pulling figuratively and literally to get here. Still working on the last couple teeth problems. I told myself I wasn’t going to become a member till I quit smoking and coffee and started eating right. I’m happy to report I’m now a member! Haven’t worn socks in like 2 months and shoes minimally. My mental clarity is exponentially better don’t have to right everything down to remember. Starting to really catch what Jack is throwing at me but a long way to go on that. I heard pretty much all the podcasts twice read 7 of the recommended books and countless hours on the forum. Now I can add the blog and webinars to the list.

### Mike David — 2018-07-01T20:00:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journey.21543/post-244703>

This morning woke up at 5 grabbed a mountain valley water and walked to the cemetery barefoot just shorts where I can see the sunrise. Got back at 630 cooked up 3 locally grown eggs and 4 pieces of bacon with 2 cups of locally grown kale. I hit the local farm up twice a week. Then off on my daily dog walk through the park shoes go on for that because it’s paved and I push a stroller with three dogs. Then I sat in the backyard listening to webinar for 2 hours with the boy. Really getting used to the heat. I rotate in and out of the shade but remain connected. I’ll eat 30 longneked clams in bacon fat with turmeric also 1 cup kale at like one o’clock. Most days I don’t eat again. Then bedtime with the sun after some reading. Working on a good CT setup that’s next on the list. I drink 3 or 4 mountain valleys every day. Go right back outside after lunch/dinner. Every day at like 5 go on another dog walk.

### Mike David — 2018-07-01T20:06:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journey.21543/post-244704>

One other thing I take a two minute cold shower and dunk my face in ice water every day no set time just make sure I do it. That’ll change with the CT setup (see Drezy CT experiment).

Wife-“he doesn’t ever watch tv he’s always outside with Mike”. Mind blown yup there’s a better way.

Stick with it. Lay down the template and live it.

I've read so much history and so many other written works to try to understand what redemption is,if it exists, and what makes a man redeemable.

Focusing away from all that and realizing that another person is looking up to you may ironically be the most redemptive thing that you and I will ever experience. While positively influencing another I can scrub up my own existence.

### Mike David — 2018-07-06T00:35:11+0300

Good stuff Drezy. The pool was closed today because it was too hot. Seriously that makes no sense. But I’ve been out all day with a one year old mind you perfectly content. There were actually times today when I was playing with all three monkeys at the same time. That was cool.

### Mike David — 2018-07-07T00:28:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journey.21543/post-245175>

Initially I started listening to webinars starting from the first. This was the way in which I had become accustomed to consuming this info. Also the little monkey in my head prefers video to written as a medium of new info. Assimilation. The reality is the blog is where it’s at. Just have to slap that little guy around a little and tell him no that’s not the best way. Will take much longer but what doesn’t kill me will only make me stronger. My goal in embarking on this programming of a massive amount of new info. is to make better connections in real life in real time based on what I know and to better explain to others who want to listen. If it took Jack 18 months to assimilate the info. I know I will be taking a big bite of humble pie. If I never give up it will never be more than I can chew just have to chew slowly.

### Mike David — 2018-07-07T01:11:54+0300

### Mike David — 2018-07-07T06:22:38+0300

### Mike David — 2018-07-08T15:56:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journey.21543/post-245321>

The rewilding project:
5:00 GTFU bro, cold face dunk,quicky cold shower
5:30-6:00 drive to cemetery view the sunrise and take in the day with conscious breathing exercises
6:00-7:00 prepare BAB consume BAB. Strict epipaleo rx foods
7:00-10:00 yard swing. Blog consumption on printed documents. Grounded 100%
10:00-11:30 dog Walk sneakers with rubber cut off and hole cut for front foot to ground contact. No blacktop. Discovering new paths.listen to old webinars. April 2016 was a real brain teaser you were right Drezy.
11:30-1:30 monkey duties
1:30-3:00 review blog consumption reflect in the back yard while grounding
3:00-5:00 outdoor activities
5:00-6:00 chow time. Epipaleo rx style. Don’t overdue it.
6:00-8:00 dog walk. See above. Start fire.
8:00-10:00 blog consumption. Chill out talk. In the backyard.
10:00 lights out

Will see how much modern life can take me out of optimal. Ground rules. No data usage outside of webinars and printing blogs. Like the playlist idea for only using data one time then they are stored somewhere not sure how to playlist webinars will be looking into that. Will have to make runs to the staples to print blogs. I can only turn the light switch off. Rig something up for the bed Connected to the earth directly without the wife knowing. Her intuition will kick in when she gets better sleep then I will tell her why. She’s on the blue light constantly podcasting and tweeting and sleeps near her phone. The benefits of sleeping in the same bed at this point in time outweigh the side effects. I think grounding the bed is a huge thing. I got up at four and peed my brains out. Wonder if I drink too much water to be woken up like that not cool man. Found I’ve been analyzing my stool I deal with poop a lot actually with dogs cats and one year old. I even get to see the other kids poop half the time they forget to flush. Digging that Bristol stool chart. I wonder if the poop chart applies to pets too. Caution TMI : If so there normal and I’m more fiber. Can’t help it. I get one big one every morning. Protocol=8 weeks. At this time I will evaluate the situation and get some labs. Thanks Dr. Kruse.

### Mike David — 2018-07-10T17:04:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journey.21543/post-245530>

Today I did a speeze into the sun rays. The heavy particles fell to the ground but about 10 little spit particles were vibrating and not moving about. This went on for 5-10 seconds then vanished. These were not sun spots I am well adjusted to sunlight. I was looking at a huge spider web in the sun. Every detail was apparent. Little parts were broken but the design was interesting. The area of the elongated rectangles increased ever so gradually as it went from the open center. I pictured electrons dancing around on this intricate web design. Compartmentalizations is necessary in breaking down complexity but only beneficial when there is communication with the whole.

I got through 2012 and 2013 of webinars. Spending almost all waking hours outside and I still got mad shit done in the house. No longer intimidates me just enjoy the journey I say you will get there some day.

### Mike David — 2018-07-12T03:28:25+0300

Was a good relaxing day. Stayed away from blue light and emf for the most part. Hours of grounding and sun, I heard somewhere, that-helps with that. Im keeping the food thing basic by adopting KISS techniques. I used to get sleepy after like an hour of reading. It seems now reading has no effect on my wakefulness. I helped a friend solve a problem at work that’s been an energy vampire. Went on a long bike ride with the boys pulling one of those bike trailers for tiny. I don’t strenuously exercise like ever so I was surprised how well my leg muscles held up. When I go for the dog walks I take my time. Nobody else takes their time except the old people I meet in the park every morning. They enjoy conversation and like jokes and don’t look like there chasing something. I say hi to everybody just to see their reactions. I like talking to the parapalegic that can’t formulate words. He’s so damn happy and never complains. I can’t understand a thing he says but he laughs at jokes. This lady was at the bridge yelling at kids for riding bikes on the bridge. Their mom was yelling back “why are you so mean”. I thought one reason might be the sunglasses. She turned to me and explained why she was yelling (guilty much) I just smiled.

### Mike David — 2018-07-14T16:33:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journey.21543/post-245882>

I like to play devils advocate. Even if I agree with what’s being said there some curiosity for what the unrepresented side has to say. I try and formulate what this side looks and sounds like. A lot of times I realize the other side has some good points that helps give more context to my position. Sometimes the devil persuades me to jump ship and stand on the other side. My belief or opinion on any given subject Is a temporary state of mind reserving the right to be changed when previously unheard information becomes heard. Sometimes new information is heard but not fully understood for this type of info. There is a mental waiting room where judgement is reserved in favor of investigation. I can no longer rely on experts to clarify I need the building blocks the experts used to see how the puzzle fits myself. Headed out camping for a week gonna get some quality time in nature with the modern phone disconnected. This will be a great way to quiet everything down and map out a path for the future. GET EXCITED!

### Mike David — 2018-07-16T17:01:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journey.21543/post-245981>

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/a-few-paragraphs-is-not-the-complete-story.20400/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/a-few-paragraphs-is-not-the-complete-story.20400/)

I have much to learn. I have much to learn about learning at least I’ve come to the right place. In the past I would just skim the surface of a topic I was interested in thinking the 30000’ view was all I needed to be informed. Not getting caught up in the messy details saying to myself just get to the point. Little did I know I missed a lot of key points I was to lazy to inquire about. It’s at this 30000’ level that a mitochondriac asks the first of many questions going on a journey to the bottom where the real answers may be found. Working on asking better questions or I should say asking questions period.Im always too eager to help others opening myself up to putting my foot in my mouth. Like Dr. Kruse said fix yourself first before trying to help others. Just wish I wasn’t so hard headed in letting myself learn the hard way.Im done looking for pupils instead I will seek the masters.

### Mike David — 2018-07-20T03:02:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journey.21543/post-246245>

My son and I did a little hypothetical experiment of sorts today. We created our own country. We crafted a flag gave it a name (the terra pax republic) we crafted a govt. structure and enacted some basic laws and requirements for our citizens. We tried to think of ways we could sustain ourselves and protect ourselves from invaders. We tried to think of the things that could cause the collapse of our republic and ways to prevent them. This was a lot of fun and it went on for hours. We realized just how complicated the whole idea of becoming a country can be. A fly landed on my leg I sat and watched as it probed my skin I thought what was its purpose for probing my skin was he getting some type of nourishment from microscopic things on my skin I cannot see. I put my finger up to it and realized I could make it spin in circles. I poked it and made it fly away it came back. I fake slapped it and it did not react. I slapped my arm and it flew away and came back. I then tried to slap its life away and it flew away and didn’t come back. Could the fly calculate my intentions? Did I give off some kind of energy it could sense but I could not? Who knows but it was fun to think about.

### Mike David — 2018-07-21T16:10:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journey.21543/post-246343>

Well the trough had a huge crease at the bottom where the metals are put together to hold the water so I sent it back. Still going out supply fishing today. My foot is GTG walking barefoot with no bandage this morning completely sealed up overnight. Just some remnants of internal bleeding and just some slight pain. When I was a child I was obsessed with sports and their stats. I would try to make a formula to input each teams stats into to see who was better despite their records. I realized very quickly depending on what stats I gave more importance to I could change the rankings by using the same stats. I could essentially make the stats tell me whatever I wanted. Is this how science works for those who are not objective and have something to lose by the results? It’s important to not just take results at face value but be able to deconstruct the results to find the underlying formula that was assumed in the beginning to be correct. Does your reality reside in reality?

### Mike David — 2018-07-23T18:19:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journey.21543/post-246524>

What is my optimal? I think it’s like laundry. No matter how much I do there will always be more the next day. It’s more mental than physical. It’s about being a mathlete as opposed to an athlete. Optimal is about never ending discovery. Just like an athlete no matter how many achievements always says there’s room for improvement and goes to practice the next day. Our environment is constantly changing for better or worse and I have to be the water that can form to any container I’m placed in (shout out to Bruce Lee). In the past optimal was not on the menu also I knew nothing of what was on the optimal menu. So the journey starts by realizing your clothes are dirty and they need washing. The journey washes the dirty clothes then dries dries them so they can become dirty once again. My ignorance made me believe my fate was predetermined by genetics. I was going to succumb to heart disease in my 5th or 6th decade like the rest of the men in my tree. The responsibility for my own well being was bypassed with this knowledge and I was free to do as I pleased knowing my fate was predetermined. Coming to the realization that this is not the case and the decisions I made every day had everything to do with fate was hard to come to grips with. A sort of guilt of ignorance. But how was I to know any better. When I was 16 my parents gave me a carton of cigarettes in my stocking for Christmas. We had a tv and an ashtray in every room of the house including the bathroom. The ignorance was all around me and permeated through my being. I had to realize comfort was the enemy an illusion of my existence. If I was going to live a long healthy life and buck the trend I was going to have to get out of my comfort zone and not fall back on excuses (they are like assholes everyone has one and they all stink). But where to go for answers. I would latch onto an idea and ride it out until I found a flaw then I would throw the baby out with the bath water. This would continue while the obvious remained in my blind spot. Certain things I did every day provided enough comfort that made searching for a solution hypocritical. It wasn’t until every hypocracy was addressed that I felt I was in the right road. I had to ask better questions and become adapted to finding my own answers. A world of discovery opened up to me that I could have never imagined. The fear of the unknown has turned into the path of the curious. The knowledge that one person or book or YouTube video could never provide all the answers is huge. The simplicity of the solutions is astounding. I am in a never ending search to expand my menu and alter my thinking. When the mind is right the body will follow telling the mind your on the right track. The circle of life comes back to the beginning to start a new circle that is better able to reach the beginning once again.

### Mike David — 2018-07-24T01:57:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journey.21543/post-246552>

Went to the pool today was raining on and off and you would say who cares your at the pool right. Well every time it started to rain like 90% of the patrons went running for the hills swimsuits and all. They waited under a roof overhang for the rain to stop. That made the once crowded pool quite the roomy place to be. People even left the pool itself because it was raining. Must be tough going through life thinking nature is out to get you. Went over to the Giant today didn’t feel like fighting traffic to the Wegmans and I had to replenish my water stock. Wegmans doesn’t sell non plastic water. To my surprise the mountain valley was $1 off cleared the shelf. I scored some wild caught gulf shrimp and some nature valley wild caught scallops. They will go good with the salmon fillets. I ran into William today at the park he walks around with a cockatiel on his shoulder and stops to talk and let the baby touch his bird(that was for you Drezy). Dan was out patching his front walk and we a had a good chat about random stuff. I told him I was going to start CT. He asked why I told him just trying to stay alive you know. He wears a funny hat that covers his neck and face from the sun poor Dan. John was out mowing his lawn again sometimes he takes off work to do it always worried about his lawn not looking like Pebble Beach. He wears long pants and shirt to “protect himself from the sun”. Here I am with just mesh shorts living it up telling him how good the sun is for you. Something tells me he thinks I’m crazy well his loss I guess. I helped my 12 year old build a fort in the backyard and he attached are new country flag to a pvc pipe and put it on the fort. That’s are capitol building where are citizens can come once a week to air their grievances and propose new laws to vote on. Yesterday I read a good bit of Dr. Moreno’s book going somewhere a real page turner. Today I resumed the CT series on the blog and gave a listen to the Power Company webinar. It’s crazy I haven’t played chess or listened to the sports talk app in months. I used to be obsessed with both don’t miss them at all. But I’ll take up anyone on a chess challenge any place any time. No more effects whatsoever with my foot I almost feel indestructible. Well off on my three dog walk gonna crank up the Quantum Computer webinar and roll out. Cheers

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journey.21543/post-246619>

Ask your doc to either:
draw blood for this tests in his office
or
give you script with word attachment pointing to this list:
\=============

1 [\-------](http://www.icd10data.com/ICD10CM/Codes/E00-E89/E50-E64/E63-/E63.9) Pregnenolone, Mass Spectrometry--Test Number: 140707 CPT Code: 84140
2 [\-------](http://www.icd10data.com/ICD10CM/Codes/R00-R99/R50-R69/R53-/R53.81) Progesterone--Test Number: 004317 CPT Code: 84144

3 ------- Cortisol, AM & PM Test Number: 104000 CPT Code: 82533 (7;30AM and noon)
4 [\-------](http://www.icd10data.com/ICD10CM/Codes/R00-R99/R50-R69/R68-/R68.82) Dehydroepiandrosterone (DHEA) Sulfate--Test Number: 004020 CPT Code: 82627
5 ------- Testosterone, Total, Serum Test Number: 004226 CPT Code: 84403

6 ------- Testosterone, Free and Weakly Bound--Test Number: 143255 CPT Code: 84402; 84403
7 [\-------](http://www.icd10data.com/ICD10CM/Codes/E00-E89/E00-E07/E03-/E03.9) SHBG
8 [\-------](http://www.icd10data.com/ICD10CM/Codes/E00-E89/E20-E35/E27-/E27.40) Estrogens, Total; Test Number: 004549 CPT Code: 82672

9 [\-------](http://www.icd10data.com/ICD10CM/Codes/R00-R99/R50-R69/R63-/R63.8) Estradiol, Sensitive Test Number: 140244 CPT Code: 82670

10 [\-------](http://www.icd10data.com/ICD10CM/Codes/E00-E89/E20-E35/E29-/E29.1) Estrone, Serum Test Number: 004564 CPT Code: 82679

11 ------- Metabolic Panel (14), Comprehensive Test Number: 322000 CPT Code: 80053
12 ------- Complete Blood Count (CBC) With Differential Test Number: 005009 CPT Code: 85025
13 ------- NMR LipoProfile® With Insulin Resistance Markers (With Graph)
Test Number: 123638 CPT: 80061; 83704
14 ------- Thyroid-stimulating Hormone (TSH)- Test Number: 004259 CPT Code: 84443
15 ------- Thyroxine (T4)- Test Number: 001149 CPT Code: 84436

16 ------- Thyroxine (T4), Free, Direct, Serum-Test Number: 001974 CPT Code: 84439
17 ------- Triiodothyronine (T3)- Test Number: 002188 CPT Code: 84480

18 ------- Triiodothyronine (T3), Free, Serum- Test Number: 010389 CPT Code: 84481

21 ------- Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum
22 ------- IGF-1
23 ------- IGFBP3
24 ------- Insulin, Free and Total, Serum Test Number: 140350 CPT Code: 83525; 83527
25 ------- Hemoglobin (Hb) A1c With GlycoMark® Reflex Test Number: 503205 CPT Code: 83036; 84378
26 ------- Homocysteine, Plasma Test Number: 706994 CPT: 83090

27 ------- Vitamin D, 25-Hydroxy Test Number: 081950 CPT: 82306

### JanSz — 2018-07-24T23:41:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journey.21543/post-246625>

Remember calcium scoring is directly tied to homocystine level.
So if you have to minimize any labs, you must do homocystine (hell or high water)
\==============================================
Go to place where tests are done.
Ask for a calcium score test.
they call it (CT CARDIAC SCORING)
Use my test as example.
It cost me $90 out of pocket.
Look at my place's website, possibly you can get some info for your area.

Just watched a video that brought a tear to my eyes. Captain Galeazzi had both his legs blown off by a roadside bomb and his left arm was completely mangled. Not only did he survive he thrived. After enduring horror and pain of an unimaginable level and countless hours of rehabilitation he passed his MCAT and got accepted to Harvard Medical School. When asked what kind of doctor he wants to be he said “a good one”. That last part reminded me of a certain neurosurgeon we all know. Keep up the good work Dr. Kruse.

### Mike David — 2018-07-26T02:52:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journey.21543/post-246728>

Man babies are a lot of work. Little man is getting around a lot better now I’m like a lion chasing a gazelle almost constantly. But he’s not used to all the activity and gets worn down fast. Why do you have to put everything in your mouth? Love this little dude wouldn’t trade a second of it. I followed Dr. Kruse on Facebook and their is a lot of good stuff in there. I noticed my tech time increasing gradually have to put a bit of a leash on that. We’ve had the worst weather here the last four days raining constantly. Dying for some good sun maybe instead of a rain dance I’ll devise a sun dance and sacrifice a goat to the gods over a nice campfire. Oh wait everything’s wet and it’s impossible to keep this thing going. That must be why the big man invented gasoline and cardboard. This goat isn’t getting any riper better get this thing going.

### Mike David — 2018-07-27T02:52:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journey.21543/post-246779>

I don’t buy vegetables at the grocery store anymore. I go in my backyard and pick the leaves off the weeds that grow naturally without me tending to them. I found the most common one outback is a plant the Indians called white man foot. Grows well in rocky soil without me using hose water to maintain it. My neighbors spend tons of money on chemicals to eradicate this weed from their lawns. Just have to make sure the dogs didn’t pee on it before I eat it. Takes some getting used to but my taste buds are coming around. I also learned it’s good to crush up and use for bee stings and mosquito bites which comes in handy when you walk around barefoot mostly naked all day. Funny thing is I almost never get mosquito bites living by a creek and being outside almost all the time.

Just catching up on your journal—man you’re doing awesome! It’s no small feat to dive deep into this site and care for a tribe of kids at the same time.

Thanks for the reminder about edible weeds. My yard is chock full of two varieties of ‘white man’s foot’ (plantago/plantain). I knew it was medicinal but had no clue it was edible. Bit late in the season now (better when young) but how do you prepare it? Some good info here—one site of many.

### Mike David — 2018-07-30T17:54:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journey.21543/post-247005>

Took the dogs on a last walk for the week this morning. Headed to the catskill mountains for the week. Put down 15 oysters and 4 eggs for BAB. Chilled outback and listened to some good music while taking in the new day. Going to get some peace and quiet and check out some new untapped wilderness. Looking forward to some mountain sunrises. Going to do a lot of thinking and scheming while away see what new roads I can uncover. Going after this week to meet with my doc and get some hydrothermal magnetic plasma draw to see where I stand in my health optimization adventure. My CT tank should be arriving in the next couple of weeks that will help with my redox I’m sure. Can I get a six pack before school starts without doing one sit-up? We shall see. Havent watched tv in weeks that is a pretty big deal for me. Got my iPhone time down quite a bit but still room for improvement. I found out the boys have been playing fortnite till like 2-3 AM. Now when I head for the sack at 10 I confiscate the PlayStation and laptops. This should be my last post for the week have to prove to myself I can go 5 days without the internet. Been adopting the teachings of the Dalai Lama by helping others and donating a ton of stuff to pennridge fish a local nonprofit that helps needy families. I sat down with a homeless man and chatted for a bit he looked like he needed a friend. I gave him $5 and went on my way the dogs were very patient. I’ve been given so much in my short time on this rock,never asked for a dime, achieved everything through hard work. It’s amazing how the universe works if your hearts in the right place. It’s time to start giving back any way I can.

### Phosphene — 2018-07-30T18:35:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journey.21543/post-247009>

Good for you—enjoy. Maybe you’ll run into my sons who are currently kayaking somewhere up there off the grid.

Wanted to reply about the mammal thing. Was formulating a snazzy response in my head but should just spit out a few things and move on!

The first thing that came to my mind was...mammary. As in breast feeding. Think it’s an absolute crime that it’s still looked upon as difficult, or inconvenient, or even shameful. There was no way in hell I wasn’t going to do it, and I did give my sons the gift of exclusive breast feeding until solids, then whenever they wanted till they weaned themselves in early toddlerhood. Some of the most joyful times of my life, and one of my greatest accomplishments.

### Mike David — 2018-08-02T02:07:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journey.21543/post-247245>

I must be in a good place here in the mountains because my phone is on no service pretty much everywhere I go and the tuner in the car just keeps going when I press seek button. There’s a beautiful white horse in the field across the street during sunrise I just sit there and watch him. I went up bouchoux trail with the kids with tiny on my back a 3 mile hike up a nice steep grade then at the top of the mountain you got a majestic panoramic view with the Delaware river flowing through the mountains. Was raining this morning but I still did some “CT” in the pool. When tiny took a nap I took a trip up the mountain outback 3/4 of the way up I found an abandoned logging road ended up walking for over 2 hours. I’ve been getting some sun but not a lot due to the clouds but still spending much time outside and grounded chasing babies and reading. I’m getting good at finding my spot where I left off. No seafood oh well I’ll survive. I’ve been restricting the kids sugar intake best I can they haven’t thrown me overboard yet. Tomorrow it’s off to bethel woods otherwise known as the location of Woodstock looking forward to that.

### Mike David — 2018-08-26T04:59:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journey.21543/post-249433>

Been spending some time on the outskirts of Estes Park CO. What an amazing place everywhere I look I see natural beauty untouched by the hand of man. Sitting outside in the morning in just shorts while it’s 45 degrees is so refreshing. Jump into the hot tub then air dry a couple of times is great. Hiking the mountain trails in beautiful RMNP is an eye opening experience. Sitting on the porch swing reading blogs and Native American accounts of war with US soldiers is even more eye opening. I am the only shirtless one on the trail people look at me like I’m nuts as they lather on the screen. Love the smell of the ponderosa pines 10000’ up. Haven’t even a hint of sunburn at this altitude. No shortage of breath as well this higher redox thing kicks ass. There gonna have to drag me outa here kicking and screaming. I apologize to Drezy but I did not forget that work will be done.

### Mike David — 2018-08-28T18:16:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journey.21543/post-249585>

I’ve come to realize just how similar Dr. Kruse is to my drill instructor in boot camp. They both know the consequences of not getting on board with their message is a painful untimely death. When I arrived to hells fury Chicago I stood there in front of the Chief Petty Officer as an individual and a very naive one at that. Chief took one look at me asked me a few questions and could see all my weekneses. He took his orders from a higher power and failure was not an option. For Dr. Kruse that higher power is Nature. Failure is also not an option for Dr. Kruse the consequences are death. Chief had to take 125 individuals from all walks of life and get them to step in unison in 13 short weeks. What a massive uphill battle this will prove to be. With trust in the process from the higher order and his wealth of life experience he had no concern that this task will be accomplished on time. He knew my thought and feelings were not congruent with his because I have not seen what he had. No time was wasted in letting me know how much that fact really pissed him off. Any time my thoughts or actions didn’t line up with his vision of me, he was not shy about demanding I knew it. The only difference hear is when Chief let me know,it was much more physically painful. When Dr. Kruse gives you the business it’s more mentally painful. How else would you perform brain surgery without a scalpel. The sooner my vision of myself lined up with Cheifs the easier everyday life was to navigate. The last year is no different. As time passed and visions aligned I came to find out Chief was a pretty cool and funny dude. He was not a mean old cuss like day one Mike had claimed. I never looked at Dr. Kruse as a mean old cuss just someone who was confident and wasn’t afraid to speak his mind under any circumstances. I hope one day to be able to meet Dr. Kruse in person to see for myself if he is a cool and funny dude. Amazingly after those short 13 weeks all 125 of us individuals were transformed into one cohesive tight knit unit. It’s no less of a feat to bring a lay person into the world of quantum biology and get them to be the ultimate individual. The ironic part hear is Dr. Kruse needs you to be more of an individual to survive in his world. Nobody will be shooting at you in civilian world. You must not listen to anybody but yourself in Dr. Kruse world. A world where normal and conformity to old ways is demanded and will get you killed, just more slowly and painfully than in Cheifs world. I have no less respect for Dr. Kruse than I had for Cheif after those 13 weeks. “Follow me through the dark swampy Forrest and I shall lead you to the lush green pastures of seemingly never ending life.” -Nature as told by Dr. Kruse. I thank you kind sir.

### Mike David — 2018-08-30T18:45:19+0300

Why did you come here in the first place? Why must you fall back on your Neolithic ways? The creature comforts of the past have been shown the light of day. They are the wolves in sheep clothing. What don’t you fucking understand about that truth little monkey. Think of the changes you already made and how much better you feel from them. Docs message has no holes you need to follow the message to a T. There’s no going back brotha. Your not smarter than Jack you can’t break any rules and get away with it for long. Your no spring chicken and all those bad decisions will catch up with you. How do you know you aren’t in a disease state right now. Yea you feel great and have more energy but conclusions can’t be made on blind faith. There’s a clear path to follow the great Doc has done all the heavy lifting for you. Talk is cheap actions speak louder than words. You must learn once and for all from the struggles of BD. What did his Neolithic brain get him how easy it is to forget when it no longer stares you in the face. This journey may be difficult at times sometimes it will go against intuition and bring your fears up from the depths they’ve been buried in. When the going gets tough optimal gets going old Neolithic monkeys cower in the corner and suck on their thumbs waiting for the storm to magically pass. Rise up Michael and meet the challenges of your modern existence head on. You have no choice in the matter. BD has tought you that lesson all too well.

### Mike David — 2018-09-09T02:10:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journey.21543/post-250546>

The old way is going to go down kicking and screaming. Doing optimal in this modern world is like a recovering alcoholic keeping a bottle of vodka around for a rainy day. The rainy days are the most challenging. Mostly due to not getting a ton of sun or just not being outside doing whatever all time. I go on dog walks in the rain but I wear a shirt. I heard Dr. Kruse say O2 in the environment is increased during rain storms. You really don’t have to deal with that many days of constant rain where you can’t get outside, but these days stick out in my mind and are the most challenging. I love spending hours on end in the sun, I do a lot of yard work, go on twice daily dog walks and check out state and local parks. My sleep schedule is spot on asleep at 10 awake at 5-530. No alarms like clockwork sleep is very good and I’m energized in the Am. I found a closer place to see the sunrise so that’s a bonus. My evening walks coincide with the falling sun so I never miss that either. I have seafood with my eggs and bacon in the AM, just after sunrise mind altering reflection time. I have no problem not eating till like 4 when I make dinner, we get free blue aprons for 3 or 4 meals a week because they are advertising on the podcast. I can’t say I’m too particular about what I eat for dinner. It’s usually not a lot of whatever. Due to my sleep schedule and being outside a lot I don’t get much artificial light at all. I put my phone on the red screen all the time and have been working on using it less and less. I turn it off at least half the time. I do read blogs on it, I started a sort of half hearted approach to reading the blog and not enough was sticking. I started back at the first one and am reading all comments as well. No stone will be left unturned. When I get to them I will listen to webinars that coincide with the timing of the blog posts I’m reading. There’s quite a big gap to bridge here considering my complete lack of consideration for all things health related up to about 10 months ago. I’m quite confident in my ability to overcome large obstacles at this point in time. I fill the metal tub with hose water and sit in it for a half hour a night at 8-9 o’clock. This part of the day quite frankly sucks but whatever has to be done. I’m reading a lot more and my comprehension is getting better. I read more in the last month than I did from 18-36 years old. Not relying on tv or the internet helps with having time to read. I’ve never stuck with any one thing for very long in my past one of my downfalls I guess. But I’m still juicing to learn all about this quantum cell theory and quantum biology. My wife called it hoodoo voodoo internet bullshit. That did not bother me she won’t even look at material I give her. She spends almost every waking hour podcasting or on social media and smokes a pack a day. Like a freight train headed to the cliff. I see me from 2-3 years ago in her. Her emotional outbursts and overreactions have no effect on me any longer. When she tells me how shitty she feels I tell her to drink more water and get into the sun. That doesn’t go over well but I feel like I have to give the best advice I can. The kids are addicted to laptops and playstation so I play the bad guy and institute restrictions on them. Doing schoolwork at home with them helps keep them off the juice. Can’t help but imagine just how horrible the environment is to be in at school. Much worse than when I was there. When I find out what there studying I make them question everything. Half at least what they teach is a joke. Homeschooling sounds better and better every day the shooter drills and cameras everywhere are affecting them psychologically. A strong curiosity is not something that came naturally for me. I think learning how much bullshit experts try to feed you and possibly doing something you think is helpful actually be harmful fuels my curiosity. Seeking truth and understanding of this backwards mixed up world is what gets me out of bed in the morning. Smelling out the bullshit is my mission I will no longer act until I have a good sound answer to why. People I speak to daily have a lot of fears but can provide little as far as answers. I limited to a great extent my contact with bullshit by cutting out tv and most of internet. What I found at Dr. Kruse website is truth down to the core. My seed is planted here and from here my flower will bloom.

### Mike David — 2018-09-12T03:52:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journey.21543/post-250796>

Going great following Dr. Kruses lead. Keep it coming. I thought to myself today Dr. Kruse uses Facebook to try to bring people to the site. I think I can help in this way to to try to bring people to the site. I can share his posts on my feed but I only have like 20 or so friends and they all have gotten a lot of his messages already. Sooo I see a post regarding health related recommendations and I see my chance to strike. I drop some Dr. Kruse outside the box knowledge send links (only ones that can be attained with a simple google search) and let the mud fly. I don’t care about the critics and there are plenty of them quite creative sometimes but low and behold there are reasonable inquiries of curious people who are not know it alls and just maybe can get outside the box. These are my targets. I drop the site link and hope for the best. Most people and insanely ignorant and I could care less about there incoherent reactions. If just one person comes here to be educated I feel good about doing it. I told a bunch of diabetics there’s a potential cure for them and they may not have to rely on insulin shots till the day they die. I took a good brow beating for this one too but who gives a shit what they think. It’s all about finding that one nonsheeple in the abyss that might be able to save themselves. Dr. Kruse if you do not agree with this methodology for any reason just say it and I will stop. Sometimes I get crazy ideas and don’t see all the angles. If not I will continue from time to time to take my brow beating like a man and hope for the best.

### Mike David — 2018-09-19T22:08:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journey.21543/post-251365>

The more the leaves drop the less harder I have to try to get the first morning sun. I can get it at the playground right down the street. Getting a little chillier in the AM. Still spend most my days outside chasing the little guy around and reading. I’m looking for a sparsely populated place on the western side of North Carolina as a possible landing spot. Got a ton of work to do on the house to get it ready for sale. Shooting for next spring. Have to talk the wife out of moving to Colorado. Looking for a place with at least an acre. Still chewing on different ideas for a career choice once the little guy starts going to school. I think about this a lot. Pretty soon I’ll be going all meat and seafood to see if I can get through the winter without putting on the usual 20-30 pounds. The more I learn about these once alien subjects the more ideas I’m able to tie together. Like seeing the Forrest through the trees by shaking the leaves. So beautiful around here in the fall. You pick up little nuggets of information here and there that allows you to make slight adjustments to get you closer to optimal. I spend a lot more time these days in silent reflection embracing the silence to think more clearly.

### Mike David — 2018-09-22T17:59:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journey.21543/post-251535>

As I sit here on my yard swing and stare at my feet I still am not used to tan feet. They look like someone else’s feet I never had tan feet. I took the little man to the doctor and this is what she said. Your putting sunscreen on my man right? Probably noticing his epic baby tan. My non confrontational personality immediately blurred out yup. Thinking my logical explanation could never see its way through the layers of conformational bias the conversation about trying to give my one year old cancer stopped there. The dentist gave me a script for antibiotics after he yanked a molar as a precaution against infection. I took the script from him and filled it the full bottle sits there permanently unopened. I took my ass outside and opened wide. I thought to myself what would that guy have said if I told him I don’t need the script I spend most my time outside in sunlight so my redox potential can probably handle any infection. Well I’m sure he didn’t have the time to enter into the details of such conversation and he might then be worried I might get an infection and not use his magic pills. What he doesn’t know won’t hurt him. That thing healed up nicely. About 3 times a week I have a seafood smorgasbord for breakfast. This morning was a 5oz. Salmon filet,15 mussels, 15 clams, 2 farm fresh eggs. The eggs in the container are not all the same size wonder if eggs are wasted because of their size. Where the hell did people get the idea their food had to be “pretty” and symmetrical. Time for the 3 dogs and a stroller dog walk. One last thing some people say hi to the dogs some say hi to the baby first very few say hi to me first a third don’t say shit. But the people that engage always acknowledge me as I’m the only one able to interact with them. Yesterday this couple emphatically said hi to the dogs then after I said hi they both gave me a cold blank stare and kept walking. It was like a stare that said we don’t speak human we speak dog. Do they make cute little dog sounds when there trying to figure out what to do for dinner?

### Mike David — 2018-09-29T15:24:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journey.21543/post-251953>

Finally got sun for sunrise for the first time this week. Plan for today is get all the sun. Finally the sun has showed its face this feels so wonderful I feel like celebrating. Went to bed early last night like 2030. Still got up at 0530. Little man is running around like a wild man these days. Not much time for much else now that he can get around so good. Nap time is reading time for me. Becoming comfortable with the still silence of my thoughts. Best is when the silence leads to no thought then the ideas start to manifest. Thinking of becoming a dog walker when tiny grows up and goes to school. It can lead to some kind of doggie daycare. These things are gold mines in my neck of the woods. I’ll get to spend most of my time outside and be able to hire somebody to do the computer/office work. I think I just threw up in my mouth with that thought sorry give me a minute. I’ll start just walking them for now but that is years in the future. Mornings are around 55 now out there in shorts feels a little more like CT. I shiver my balls off when CTing uncontrollably. Joints get stiff and fatigued I have to go to my happy place to calm things down then it slowly starts again. Meditation practice helps with my CT. I’ve been able to get a lot of the housework done before the sun rises now. When I wake up I get right to work. Then while the sun is out I can play outside. Lunch is out so I just have to make tiny something when he gets hungry. Dinner is usually at 5. I made a chart of chores so the kids have to earn every last minute they spend on laptops and computers. I’m the electronics nazi. My wife on the other hand is on the computer or social media pretty much every waking moment. It’s a total social media addiction it posses much of her free from work thought. Even while playing on the floor with tiny she’s on the phone texting shit. Readings on the meter in her office are off the charts. She spends almost no time outside only to come talk to us but that phone is never far. She complains about stupid little things that annoy her and can hardly handle any real life drama. I guess her drama handling is all used up on social media. I used to be like that an angry asshole yelling and whatnot. Now I speak softly and allow my words to carry the big stick. How silly it is worrying all the time. I can’t let things I can’t control bother me anymore. If someone wants to pop off at the mouth and try to tell me much of what I’ve been studying for the last year is complete nonsense I can’t control that. I just recently started to realize how much hogwash is in the climate change model. I really thought this was the defining paradox of our lifetimes one can be so blind when they aren’t seeking the truth. Most just want to be agreed with. I enjoy disagreeing it makes me happy when I can disagree and challenge somebodies thought this simple and sound explanations and questions. The more you know the more you realize you don’t know. Anybody who claims to know a lot doesn’t understand shit. They often times know a lot about a very narrow window of subjects and are surprisingly blind to the big picture. I always told my prospective employer at a job interview I know a little of everything but I’m not the expert at anything. A little grows a little every day. That is how I operated my business. Those big electrical companies have there little expert divisions where a guy would just do residential everyday they are completely lost in any other setting (throw up in my mouth). One day I’ll be in an old ladies attic another I’ll be hooking up capacitors to a 4000 amp distribution center talk about EMF. I wired dentist offices fixed runway lights fixed motors and hooked up VFD. If it was something new I underbid the job so I could learn. What I liked the most was troubleshooting. This is an art anybody can tear a bunch of shit apart and get lucky by replacing the right thing. The art is to do the least amount to find the core issue. To study every last detail until an obvious path presents itself it’s like the wires talk to you if you ask them how and what there doing. Well that is a closed chapter now never going back to that EMF existence.

### Phosphene — 2018-10-05T18:49:46+0300

### Mike David — 2018-10-12T01:58:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journey.21543/post-252936>

a poem inspired by JK

The powers that be dont think for me
Theres a man called Doc from the third rock
He’ll crack open your mind so your not blind
His knowledge is immense why so tense
Its probably EMF it makes him different from the rest
Hes not blowing smoke his content will make you choke
The sun can heal see how it makes you feel
Take off your shoes feel good as new
The complexity of water wil make you wonder
What he learned in school really isnt true
Theyll say theres nothing we can do jokes on you
The paradox of life begins with light
You say its the food jk may get rude
He doesnt care what you think you need to know your opinion stinks
The three legged stool is meant for you
Light water magnetism brings you to the schism
They have alot to say it doesnt matter anyway
What is laid out on the blog helps to clear the fog
This info. is epic it cuts through the epoc
Nature brings the rain and reverses the pain
If your not good for yourself put the ego on the shelf
You can reverse the disease go in the tank and freeze
For what you learned in the past will get kicked in the ass
From Leptin one to everything under the sun
Learning new things thats fit for a king
Stay thirsty my friends and stay till the end
Read Read Read until your eyes bleed
This may not be for most because their brains are toast
Here comes the Doc the paradigm boat will rock
Better not blink that boats gonna sink.
Much love to his people far from sheeple

sincerely mitochondriac in training

### Mike David — 2019-02-08T20:09:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journey.21543/post-262619>

Been an interesting winter. I have days I curse nature for sucking so bad. Then you’ll get a few really good days. I’m starting to not have so much bad feelings with the really cold no sun days. Just have to pay more attention to detail and focus on what’s best. I’m getting UV now in PA. According to D minder I was out there when it first showed up a week back. Skin in the game for sure. Was only UV 2 but it’s the thought that counts. I was actually pretty pumped for this. 45 deg. And windy right now but sun is out 100%. I’m in my little stone cacoon to block the wind some and feel the sun radiate off the rocks. Almost feel like summer lol. No shirt no shoes no problem sit right here and become one with the universe.There were times on cold days I just got the minimum outside time I would reach near the daily avg. screen time. Technology diet is my number one thing for coming out of winter. I’m cooking seafood on the grill on this Himalayan salt plate. Takes a while but it’s kinda cool doing something different. Salmon is my go to I try out shellfish too. Big bacon egg salmon omelette this morning. They always have the glass mountain valley bottles for sale at giant. Everybody gets the way cheaper plastic. You get what you pay for. Except mine are on great sale and it’s almost best of both worlds. I changed the tires on the stroller they were all the way bald. Me and tiny are walking further and further without the stroller every week it seems. He spent almost an hour running around in this field by the graveyard just having fun. Graveyard is a good low population density zone for me. Also go to the parks a lot. Those towers are everywhere.

### Mike David — 2019-02-16T03:38:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journey.21543/post-263305>

I escaped the cold and I am in sunny St. Augustine for a week. 6:30 I awoke like I usually do and headed right out on foot to the ocean. You guys that live by the beach are so lucky but then again you make your own luck in that respect. I was surprised to see so many other people there on the beach at dawn. BUT they were all or maybe 99.9% wearing shoes and even jackets and long sleeves. I went right in the water and it was cold. I spent a couple hours out there then went back to eat my BAB. I’ve eaten every meal here outside while grounding. Been obsessed with grounding lately, practicing and researching. It’s nice to be outside without forced CT. I still hate the cold but I overcome my mental blockage because I know the temp. Stress is much needed. I live a blessed existence and it takes work to be stressed temporarily. I spent almost all day outside soaking in everything nature had to offer. I even noticed a little color on my ghost ass. I eat almost a carnivore diet and thoroughly enjoy it. I have noticed being a little more groggy when I first wake up but I am always up by 6:30. I noticed little aches and pains creeping back in. This is no doubt due to not getting as much sun and grounding everyday. I felt almost invincible last summer and fall. Oh how the mighty have fallen. I could use to lose a few pounds but I did not gain as much this winter as usual. My regressions have given me a new vigor to be optimal at the necessary cost. I will admit to not getting lab work done. WHY??? I see people post lab results for help then silence. I hear GPs don’t know how to evaluate labs correctly and don’t know all the best ones to run and I didn’t want to waste the money. Then I saw this great thread in the forums labeled LABS. It’s like I couldn’t see the Forrest through the trees. Lots of great help for people on those threads. I just need to stop being a little bitch and proceed to action. I also didn’t want to spend more on labs as I was shelling out thousands on teeth work. I’ve also looked through the blogs,emails and threads to get a solid list of the optimal labs. I now can lock horns with the doc if need be to explain why I want this or that lab done. The doctors office is a completely alien place for me and now I’ve lost some respect for regular docs because of all of this optimal disease reversal info. (Right or wrong that’s what my gut tells me) I’m going in to give my doc the benefit of the doubt and let her prove me wrong it’s only right. I stopped watching YouTube and cut way way down on Facebook. I don’t use a computer ever can’t wven remember the last time but I do have this phone. I’ve gotten way better at downloading what I need then going into airplane mode. I’ve been getting to sleep regularly at 9:30-10:00 and sleeping like a baby till morning. Sometimes I do get up around 3 or 4 to pee then back out till 6. The wife told me a few weeks back that I was snoring again (gut punch). That was a big wake up call that winter was winning and I needed more stress of the cold. She hasn’t woken me up at all recently so I’m going to keep climbing that optimal mountain. I hate the cold. ( I can see the eye rolls but that’s my antagonist I guess) Headed to the Daytona 500 Sunday with all the EMF but at least I can be shirtless in the sun and grounded while enjoying the fine racing. We got a flight on Aligent air and they don’t have WiFi and we flew out of and into small airports. No ill effects from the flight. Much respect to the Good Doc, Drezy and JansZ for all the time they take to help on this forum I’m sure there are more but youz are the ones I notice the most.Im also getting the 23 and me done for good measure.

### Mike David — 2019-02-23T19:13:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journey.21543/post-263861>

Interesting article on global life expectancy over last two centuries.
[https://ourworldindata.org/life-expectancy-globally](https://ourworldindata.org/life-expectancy-globally).

Modern medical interventions have brought us a long way. Just learning that you have to wash your hands as a doctor saved countless lives in the 19 century. Availability of clean drinking water and abundant food sources have helped a great deal as well. We have seemed to be obsessed with treating the sick and not talking much about what you do to not become sick. To be able to live out your golden years on your own terms. To avoid being tethered to a broken system of thinking. To avoid the spirit breaking wallet biopsies most endure as they age. Little can be known about why a person is sick by observation and testing alone. Everybody has there own unique thunderstorm inside them. The effects on your biology from your thunderstorm are realized long before your conscious mind senses trouble making it appear like something occurred all of a sudden. Scientific observations and brave new ideas are hallmarks of an advancing civilization. What has science and brave new ideas told us over the last two centuries that should lead you to believe nature is our dominant medical paradigm for over 4 billion years. Man is far behind and is easily led astray in his pursuits for longevity by a lack of understanding coupled with money and the need to be accepted by your peers. If you want to stay healthy without guessing you need to see how nature has been constructing our three dimensional reality for over 4 billion years. There has been a lot of great work done by great people over the last 200 years that allow us to see into the past to see how we were built and to know make makes us fall apart. “I know how the peices fit because I watched them as they fell away” Jack Kruse. If your apart of the majority in today’s world you need to be worried.

Looks like at least 10 miles away though, so I’ll probably return.

### Mike David — 2019-02-28T22:00:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journey.21543/post-264180>

Light cycles are getting longer and uv is on the uptick. This time of year is unique in that you can get some uv and heat from the sun while getting CT. Ground is still frozen but I just wear socks to ground for now. I am hooked up with 4 local csa farms to get what grows here. I’ve been almost freezing my water and drinking with lime slices. 2 liters a day. I’ve been relying a lot on protein and fat with some chocolate some dark green vegetables long grain rice and coffee. I use ghee butter bacon fat tumeric good salt and pepper religiously. Staples are eggs bacon salmon shrimp. I always order seafood when out. Sleep is solid from 10-1030 to 6. The birds are singing now when I awake. Dogs are chasing squirrels again in the park. I usually have from 6-7-730 to do my morning thing. Out front on the step is the perfect place right now to see the sun rising in peace. Going on 5 hours outside today. I’m going to take temp. Readings periodically through the day maybe some blood pressure readings. Measure fluid intake and outflow. Looking into a ph test. I can take pulse rates periodically. I’ll be spending more time outside everyday. I go 24 hours no food once a week. The leatherbottomed moccasins are great for winter walks. Little man is down with the cold big time.

### Mike David — 2019-03-03T01:57:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journey.21543/post-264324>

Spending a nice weekend in Annapolis for the wife’s birthday. Eating seafood every meal so far. It’s so much better here than at home. I got my nine year old to play outside with us most the day. This is a big deal because he usually just plays in front of a screen most the time. I usually sleep good but last night I slept better. Woke up with that feeling like I didn’t know where I was and thought I was still in dreamland. Visiting Florida was nice for its weather a few weeks back and a light tan but I would get headaches at night. That was unusual for me. Didn’t sleep great either. Environment must really suck down there. No headaches here in Annapolis or back home in PA. Crab deviled eggs, crab cakes, salmon fillets, oyster bar OH MY lovin’ the food here. Started getting little aches and pains a month or so back. Doubled down on the basics Dr. Kruse talks about and I’ve noticed last couple days no pain just gain. Playing outside a lot more and walking a lot still no pains. Just the occasional one in my ass. (Life with three kids) We got Harper BOOM!

### Mike David — 2019-03-08T21:15:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journey.21543/post-264831>

Sitting on my front step this morning with my two dogs and a cat taking in the sunrise and all of its beauty. I even forgot for a minute or two that it was 11 degrees. I just sit and watch the birds and the squirrels. Nice to see them slowly coming back must be a good sign. I heard about the time change coming and I had to laugh because I could give a shit about mans version of time. My time is set by my natural environment. My body is fully in tuned with her cycles. Went to bed at 8 last night and awoke at 6 good times. Got two new books to read: cross currents and the rainbow and the worm. Started on cross currents and I like what I see. I dream of being able to advance the energy side of medicine with cool experiments but probably above my pay grade. I see the doctor of the future not putting anything inside their patients to heal them. No drugs no cuts no pokes or prods just using a mastery of energy just right. Got a call reguarding life insurance I told them the ball of fire in the sky and the miracle of water is all the insurance I need lol then I heard if you’d like to make a call... Tiny is good for like an hour tops in this bitter cold. I have to tape his gloves on because he keeps taking them off then complaining his hands are cold. Anything to buy more time outside this time of year. I got a pulse and blood oxygen saturation detector, ph strips, blood pressure gauge, and blood glucose meter in the mail. All tests are in normal range so far. Yesterday I drank 2 liters of water and peed 40 oz. I’m proud of myself for being able to cut way down on the phone use. Reading,writing, monitoring,meditating,drawing and playing with toys are a much better way to spend your time than scrolling. I got a Ethernet cord to plug into the laptop to read my blogs. I do two a day then let it all settle into my brain. Some days I reread what I read the past day because it takes a lot to teach a monkey quantum science. I’m pretty dead set now on starting a dog walking business and slowly morphing it into a kennel/grooming/quantum biology center for dogs. I can promise you one thing you havent seen a dog kennel like the one in my mind that I’ve been drawing. I made a blueprint for our bathroom remodel. This is the first step to selling this house and moving south. I’m attracted to the smoky mountains and I talked the wife out of moving to Arizona I think. Colorado is still in her cards though. I’m not gonna lie it’s damn beautiful but we’re taking a week in Greysville Tennessee none the less. I’m hoping seeing is believing in the wife’s eyes. Here’s to spring sooner than later. New Years is coming up in a couple weeks and hopefully the snow is gone by then.

### Mike David — 2019-03-12T17:58:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journey.21543/post-265264>

I traveled deeper into Jeremy’s world yesterday than at any point previously. I was just going about my day in the usual way when I played webinar roulette. 3/14 popped up about overcoming a bad redox. It really got me thinking especially the part about climbing on the rocks and how Jeremy overcomes by optimizing all of his pathways while climbing and being true to his self. I just got done reading Dr. Becker’s book as well so I’m thinking about DC electricity and stimulating it for my benefit. I went onto 4/14 while tiny was napping, he sleeps longer when we’re playing outside in the cold for long periods. The physiology of water copper and collagen was something that I let guide my actions later that day. I remember stripping copper in my backyard on weekends to get a little extra badly needed money. I would get it given to me by my boss and I had to strip it bare to get the most out of it. Much knowledge of touch and applying pressures at just the right angle and intensity is required to get efficient at stripping wire. I thought about the pressures Jeremy put on his collagen while climbing. I thought how this increases his DC electric charge in his collagen and water also how it helps Jeremy to breathe through his skin. How can I imitate this pressure onto my collagen and water? When tiny awoke we walked down to the grassy hill and I did something I used to love to do as a kid. I rolled down the hill and tiny did too. It was like a feeling of giddyness came over me. I did it again and again we had an amazing time just playing in nature. This morning I took tiny onto the big hill out front. The one I roll the pumpkins down, they go for 6 or 7 seconds hopefully missing the trees across two walking paths and into the creek. We walked on the hill for some time putting different pressures on my collagen. He’s going to be taking an epic nap here soon I can feel it in my own collagen. I have my eyes on the cliffs down by the bridge that goes over the railroad tracks. Lots of good cliffs down there some are about 30’ high. I going to continue to seek out different ways of playing in nature and challenging my touch and pressure abilities and having fun in nature. I collected a nice pile of dead wood for an outdoor fire later in the backyard. I wonder if I can get myself to tap into what Jeremy has to help repair the damage caused by years of smoking.

### Mike David — 2019-03-15T15:39:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journey.21543/post-265552>

Pie day was a good one. Could even say great and I think I will and I didn’t even eat any pie. Was outside more yesterday than the previous three combined. Weather was nice and I would go out on a adventure with my crew traversing the topology of my surroundings then going back to relax a little in the backyard then back out to another adventure rinse and repeat. Tiny is walking on his own a lot more and likes climbing. I played basketball with Gavin at the park courts. Later I played football with Austin and his friends at the other park. Never ate dinner never thought about going to sleep. I slept for 8 hours right where I landed. I was essentially eating all day a grand feast just nothing entered my gut through my mouth. I talked a lot with old people I met in the park I elevated their outlook on humanity ever so slightly by showing them people still play outside even kids too! They express great joy when telling me about seeing us outside walking all the time. Not even spraying the shit out of the sun could dampen my rays yesterday. I was up before dawn ready to kick some more quantum ass.

I will admit the first time I listened to the 3/19 webinar I did not hear it/ feel it. I thought Doc needed a month off so just threw this thing together to get something out. I said are you getting soft on me. I only got through half of it before I was interrupted. I went back to it today. Doc doesn’t do shit Willy nilly and he didn’t start 3/19. It really made me think of how it relates to me personally. Using talk as touch was not something I thought about too much in the past. Sure there are times I find the right thing to say at the right time but I always look at myself as a less intuitive person and more guided by logic. I can learn a lot here with this lesson of touching by talking and just let the words flow out more freely. I need to fine tune my intuition instead of disregarding it. I started today by talking to the parapalegic guy who can’t talk down in the park. It feels a little weird but I could see in his face the joy of interaction. I just kept talking even when he thought I was done and started up his cart I kept talking and he just sat there. I made out he wanted to know my dogs names and he liked petting them. Intuition is also useful when I encounter a zombie human (self centered asshole). I can feel it in my bones with some people I meet and I know not to waste my time or energy. This came in handy as a electrical contractor to help me avoid the one percent. The one percent of folks who are just out to screw you to get something for free no matter how good the job went. It’s funny how the guy without arms and legs that work looks happier than most of the people with two good arms and legs.

It’s not neatly manicured and prepared just for my walking. I need to figure multiple means of movement to adapt to an ever changing path.

I awoke yesterday and couldn’t smell anything and energy levels were low. I ate a small breakfast (shrimp,turmeric omelette) then went out and filled the horse trough with hose water. Today I awoke and could once again smell the roses. Still a little runny but much better. Way more energy too. I’m going to do a couple more rounds today it soooooo nice out today. Dr. Edgar Cayce would give two readings a day from his couch to people of all walks of life. Everyday for 40 years. He would get groups of people with the same ailments question him about how to address the problem. His asst. was perplexed as to why he would recommend something different to different people. He asked Edgar if they all have measles why would you recommend science backed therapy for one and faith based healing for others. He said you can’t recommend a treatment the patient doesn’t believe in because if they don’t believe it won’t work. If you lose faith in your doctor or don’t trust your surgeon you must find a new one. Your thoughts and feelings absolutely impact the effectiveness of your treatment. You are never out of time as long as your breathing. Seek deep inside and figure out what works for you. Seeking deep inside doesn’t require a google search or a industry standard practice to be followed. Shut out the outside to inquire into the knowledge of one human consciousness. You will never be led astray by being true to yourself. The idea of ME is an illusion. Your putting yourself on an island and starving yourself to death. Focus on us and we to achieve greatness and peace of mind.

### Mike David — 2019-04-23T05:20:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journey.21543/post-268668>

Just got back from five days in Gatlinburg Tenn. Every morning watching the sunrise burn the smoke off the mountains while soaking in the hot tub. I see why there called the Smoky Mountains. I took the trolley to some hiking spots in the national park. What a absolute beautiful place. Just wandering around without purpose surrounded by nature stress free and care free. My senses became slowly heightened the longer and deeper I travelled into the wilderness. It’s like I subconsciously made the change in my mind to heighten my senses to make up for dropping down in the food chain. The hair standing up on my neck from the sound of branches breaking. The heart beating faster from fear of attack. Truly feeling alive ready to make that fight or flight decision at a moments notice. Compare this primordial existence to the days I spent with the family walking about the shops taking in the viral marketing. Don’t get me wrong I love spending time with the family it still was a lot of fun but really couldn’t compare to life closer to the edge. In town they have this replica space needle you can take an elevator up to the top. As my eyes looked up I could see the camouflage WiFi transmitters all around the top of this thing. Imagine what the meters would read at the top of the needle. Thousands of people happily go to the top everyday never knowing what their doing. None of it really bothers me anymore it’s really everywhere I just do my part to avoid it best I can. NEVER HIKE ALONE IN BEAR COUNTRY. ALWAYS GO WITH SOMEONE YOU CAN TRIP AND OUTRUN.

# Thread: How to handle a women with wish to have more children
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-handle-a-women-with-wish-to-have-more-children.21544/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Butters — 2018-07-01T22:05:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-handle-a-women-with-wish-to-have-more-children.21544/post-244707>

Hello fellows.
What would you do with women that want to have more kids soon, but seem not to listen to your health advice?
I am especially concerned with blue light and usage of mobile phone.
How can you have success to convince her, that she has already the responsibility for the health of her future child?
Should you try to tell her the important facts again and again or should you let her be? But then should you tell her, that you will not make a child, when she wont listen to you?
Im not talking from extrem cases but normal young women that have no big problems but some little issues.

What is the appropriate reaction or behavior in this situation?

### Jack Kruse — 2018-07-01T23:03:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-handle-a-women-with-wish-to-have-more-children.21544/post-244710>

I know what I would do......

### Butters — 2018-07-02T12:16:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-handle-a-women-with-wish-to-have-more-children.21544/post-244741>

That doesnt help me .
I know generally you should do yourself first and dont waste effort to other people when they arent ready. But this is a whole different thing.
Because when you are married and have already one kid things get more difficult.

### Jason Coates — 2018-07-02T13:40:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-handle-a-women-with-wish-to-have-more-children.21544/post-244742>

Marriage comes with negotiation and compromise. I would work towards mitigating as much as you can and then leading by example. Once a partner sees the benefits of change in you they either join you or leave you as best they can.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-07-02T14:58:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-handle-a-women-with-wish-to-have-more-children.21544/post-244745>

It was not a difficult decision for me. It was a difficult decision for my wife......

### Jason Coates — 2018-07-02T15:09:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-handle-a-women-with-wish-to-have-more-children.21544/post-244747>

I kicked a 13 year Ambien habit (3 years ago) and now can fall asleep in 5 to 10 minutes. That!!! Because of 1. Big breakfast + 2. Morning sun-gazing = wife paying attention.

### Jenelle — 2018-07-02T15:30:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-handle-a-women-with-wish-to-have-more-children.21544/post-244748>

[@Butters](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/9350/) I remember your posts and concern during her pregnancy 2 years ago. How much progress would you say has been made since that time ... in her beliefs and ways of living lining up with yours?

### Butters — 2018-07-02T15:56:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-handle-a-women-with-wish-to-have-more-children.21544/post-244751>

During pregnancy and nursing it was Way netter. But now she again adopted working on the Laptop = full Blue light during the Day Till last our when Flux Kicks in. Mobile Phone calls on the head a few times the week. And of course looking in the phone without Filter over the Day. Nothing extreme but i Care.

### Lahelada — 2018-07-02T19:36:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-handle-a-women-with-wish-to-have-more-children.21544/post-244781>

> [Butters said:](/goto/post?id=244751)
>
> During pregnancy and nursing it was Way netter. But now she again adopted working on the Laptop = full Blue light during the Day Till last our when Flux Kicks in. Mobile Phone calls on the head a few times the week. And of course looking in the phone without Filter over the Day. Nothing extreme but i Care.

Why does she not do these things ? Forgetfullness,comfort or disbelief? Also maybe you could base your reluctance to have children on something more "tangible" yet relevant. Space,money,education or career issues,at least one of which may also be relevant? That buys you time to lead by example.

### caroline — 2018-07-03T09:56:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-handle-a-women-with-wish-to-have-more-children.21544/post-244836>

Have a listen to an interview that Dr. Joe Mercola did with Dr. Klinghardt.

I am sure your wife would want to have a healthy child ......shouldn't that be the most important consideration?

With the things she is doing - she is not only damaging herself but an unborn child - I personally think that you can't be a party to that

hopefully she will watch this with you ....in fact - I would insist that she watch it. Having a healthy, happy, intelligent child is very serious stuff these days.

After taking a walk around the park at sunset, considering it's my birthday month I asked my wife Lizeth if she'd be willing to buy me an RF meter as a gift (I usually don't do gifts, but figured this was not only worth it, but necessary for our apartment search early next year lol). One slightly leaning towards the more economical side in terms of money. lol I searched around the forum boards and saw a member recommend this meter, however, it was sometime ago, so I wanted to make sure it would still prove to be accurate enough. ### Katie Durham — 2019-08-30T15:26:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rf-meter.21546/post-276859>

Coming up to my 1 year anniversary here and I \*just\* discovered how much radiation a wireless keyboard and mouse can generate. "Just" because I let myself be blinded by the convenience and never checked.

I brought my ENV RD-10 to work. (I have a few meters but this is the one I tend to reach for first for spot checks.) I have tested other areas of my office before. This time I put it between me and the keyboard and mouse. Holy moly! I was really surprised by the mouse. Any movement sets it off. I put in a request for wired. If my company won't pay for it I'll do it myself.

Retirement in 4 years. Sooner if I don't like what I'm experiencing.
